description: Huyang was a Mark IV architect droid and Jedi Order professor who, for over a thousand generations, instructed younglings in the construction of their lightsabers following the Gathering — the rite in which each initiate retrieved a kyber crystal. Operating for millennia aboard the Jedi training vessel Crucible, he claimed to have overseen the lightsaber construction of nearly every Jedi in the Order's history, including Grand Master Yoda and Mace Windu. During the Clone Wars he worked alongside Padawan Ahsoka Tano while escorting a group of younglings to Ilum, and after the fall of the Jedi Order he became Ahsoka's longtime companion and pilot aboard her T-6 shuttle. Decades later, during the New Republic era, he aided Ahsoka, Sabine Wren, and Hera Syndulla in their search for the missing Ezra Bridger and Grand Admiral Thrawn, remaining a steadfast repository of Jedi knowledge and one of the few living links to the Order's ancient past.
title: Huyang
timeline:
25020 BBY - Manufacture

Huyang is constructed as a Mark IV architect droid a few years after the founding of the Jedi Order, tasked with guiding younglings in building their lightsabers.

25020 BBY–20 BBY - A Thousand Generations of Teaching

For over a thousand generations Huyang serves aboard the Jedi vessel Crucible, overseeing the lightsaber construction of countless initiates, including future Masters Yoda and Mace Windu.

~20 BBY - The Gathering on Ilum

During the Clone Wars, Huyang accompanies Ahsoka Tano and a group of younglings to the ice caves of Ilum for the Gathering, where each youngling retrieves a kyber crystal.

~20 BBY - Building Lightsabers Aboard the Crucible

Huyang instructs the younglings in assembling their first lightsabers as they journey home, before the Crucible is attacked by the pirate Hondo Ohnaka's gang.

~20 BBY - Captured and Rescued

Damaged and dismantled during the pirate raid, Huyang is later reassembled and helps Ahsoka and the younglings escape, reaffirming his bond with them.

19 BBY - Companion to Ahsoka

Sometime after the end of the Clone Wars, Huyang joins Ahsoka Tano aboard her T-6 shuttle, becoming her enduring traveling companion.

9 ABY - Searching for Ezra and Thrawn

During the New Republic era, Huyang assists Ahsoka, Sabine Wren, and Hera Syndulla in their quest to locate the lost Ezra Bridger and prevent the return of Grand Admiral Thrawn, surviving the campaign that carries them to the distant galaxy of Peridea.

events.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

title: Maz Kanata
description: Maz Kanata is a diminutive, orange-skinned Force-sensitive alien of an undefined species and one of the galaxy's oldest known individuals, born roughly a thousand years before the war between the First Order and the Resistance. For much of her extraordinarily long life she ran a castle on the forest world of Takodana, earning a reputation as a "pirate queen" by offering safe harbor to smugglers, travelers, and rogues of all allegiances — provided they respected her house rules forbidding politics and war under her roof. A collector of antiques and relics, she came to possess the lightsaber built by Anakin Skywalker and later wielded by Luke Skywalker, which she offered to Rey in 34 ABY as the young scavenger's destiny began to unfold. After the First Order destroyed her castle, Maz allied herself with the Resistance, lending her wisdom and combat experience through the final campaign against the Sith Eternal, and she survived the war.
timeline:
973 BBY - Birth

Maz Kanata is born, a member of a species that remains undefined in canon. The Rise of Skywalker Visual Dictionary later gives her age as 1007, consistent with this birth year.

~973 BBY–34 ABY - Centuries as Takodana's Pirate Queen

Over roughly a millennium, Maz establishes and maintains her castle on Takodana, welcoming smugglers and travelers while enforcing strict prohibitions against politics and war, and amassing a vast collection of relics and antiques.

~19 BBY–34 ABY - Keeper of the Skywalker Lightsaber

At some point Maz comes into possession of the lightsaber Anakin Skywalker built during the Clone Wars and later passed to his son Luke. She keeps it stored in her castle's vaults, deflecting questions about how she acquired it.

34 ABY - Han Solo, Rey and Finn Visit Takodana

Maz receives Han Solo, Rey, Finn, and BB-8. Sensing Rey's potential, she offers her the Skywalker lightsaber, urging her to embrace the call of the Force.

34 ABY - Destruction of Maz's Castle

The First Order attacks Takodana in pursuit of BB-8 and the map to Luke Skywalker, destroying Maz's ancient castle. Maz survives and afterward aligns herself with the Resistance.

35 ABY - Aiding the Resistance Against the Final Order

Maz supports the Resistance from its base on Ajan Kloss during the campaign against Emperor Palpatine's returned Sith forces, and is present among the survivors celebrating the victory at the Battle of Exegol.

title: Yoda
description: Yoda was a diminutive, green-skinned member of an unnamed and mysterious species who became the most revered Jedi of the modern age, serving as Grand Master of the Jedi Order and as a member of the Jedi High Council for centuries. Born around 896 BBY, he began instructing younglings roughly a century later and trained generations of Jedi over some 800 years, including Count Dooku and, indirectly, nearly every Jedi of the late Republic. Renowned less for his lightsaber prowess — though he was among the greatest duelists alive — than for his wisdom, humility and profound connection to the living Force, he guided the Order through the Naboo crisis, reluctantly led the Grand Army of the Republic as a general during the Clone Wars, and was ultimately deceived along with the rest of the Council by Darth Sidious. After Order 66 he dueled the newly declared Emperor in the Senate chamber, failed to defeat him, and accepted exile in the swamps of Dagobah, where he spent two decades learning from Qui-Gon Jinn's spirit how to retain his identity after death. In 3 ABY he trained Luke Skywalker in the ways of the Force, and in 4 ABY, at the age of 900, he died and became one with the Force — returning years later as a Force spirit to counsel Luke a final time on Ahch-To.
timeline:
~896 BBY - Birth

Yoda is born, a member of a species whose name and homeworld are never revealed in canon.

~796 BBY - Begins training Jedi

About a century into his life, Yoda takes up the instruction of younglings, a vocation he will continue for roughly 800 years.

~232 BBY - Grand Master during the High Republic

By the height of the High Republic Era, Yoda serves as Grand Master of the Jedi Order, mentoring younglings and traveling the frontier alongside Jedi such as Master Zia and the initiates of Tenoo.

~132 BBY - Counsel after the Brendok tragedy

In the final years of the High Republic, Vernestra Rwoh brings Yoda word of the deaths of Indara, Torbin and Kelnacca, concealing the truth of what happened on Brendok.

~102 BBY - Trains Dooku

Yoda instructs the young Dooku, who will become one of the Order's most formidable Jedi before falling to the dark side.

32 BBY - The Phantom Menace

Yoda tests the young Anakin Skywalker and senses grave danger in the boy's fear; after Qui-Gon Jinn's death he reluctantly permits Obi-Wan Kenobi to train Anakin and recognizes that a Sith Lord has returned.

22 BBY - Attack of the Clones and the outbreak of war

Yoda leads the clone army to Geonosis to rescue the Jedi strike team, rescues Anakin and Obi-Wan from Dooku, and duels his former apprentice — the first battle of the Clone Wars.

22–19 BBY - The Clone Wars

Serving as a general of the Republic, Yoda leads campaigns such as the defense of Rugosa, survives an assassination attempt on Vjun, defends the Jedi Temple against Cad Bane's schemes, and grows increasingly troubled by the Order's role in the war.

~19 BBY - Journey to the living Force

Guided by the voice of Qui-Gon Jinn, Yoda undertakes a pilgrimage to the priestesses of Serenno's ancient world and to Moraband, confronting Darth Bane's shade and learning that he can retain his consciousness after death.

19 BBY - Order 66 and the duel with Sidious

Yoda survives the clone betrayal on Kashyyyk with the aid of Chewbacca and Tarfful, returns to Coruscant, and duels Darth Sidious in the Senate chamber. Unable to defeat the new Emperor, he withdraws.

19 BBY - Exile on Dagobah

Accepting that the Jedi have failed, Yoda goes into hiding in the swamps of Dagobah, continuing his studies with Qui-Gon's spirit and awaiting a new hope.

~2 BBY - Guiding Ezra Bridger

Through the Force, Yoda speaks to Ezra Bridger in the Jedi Temple on Lothal and later at the Temple's Force nexus, warning him of the perils of vengeance and pointing him toward Malachor.

3 ABY - Training Luke Skywalker

Luke Skywalker arrives on Dagobah at Obi-Wan's urging. Yoda tests him, reluctantly accepts him as a student, raises his X-wing from the bog, and warns him against leaving to confront Vader on Bespin.

4 ABY - Death and becoming one with the Force

At 900 years old, Yoda tells a returned Luke that Vader is his father and that another Skywalker remains, then dies and vanishes into the Force.

34 ABY - Return as a Force spirit

On Ahch-To, Yoda appears to a despairing Luke, calls down lightning on the ancient Jedi tree, and reminds him that failure is the greatest teacher — prompting Luke's final stand.

title: Jabba Desilijic Tiure
description: Jabba Desilijic Tiure, better known as Jabba the Hutt, was a Hutt gangster and crime lord who ran a vast criminal empire from his palace on the Outer Rim desert world of Tatooine. A member of the powerful Grand Hutt Council and the Desilijic clan, Jabba controlled much of the galaxy's piracy, slaving, spice-running, and smuggling operations, employing bounty hunters and enforcers to protect his interests. He is remembered for sponsoring the Boonta Eve podrace, for his dealings with smugglers such as Han Solo during the Galactic Civil War, and for holding both Han Solo (frozen in carbonite) and Princess Leia Organa captive. He met his end during the rescue of Han Solo, when Leia strangled him with the very chain he had used to enslave her aboard his sail barge over the Great Pit of Carkoon.
timeline:
600 BBY - Birth

Jabba Desilijic Tiure is born into the influential Desilijic clan of Hutts, a species originating from Nal Hutta.

~32 BBY - Sponsoring the Boonta Eve Classic

An established crime lord on Tatooine, Jabba presides over the Boonta Eve podrace won by the young Anakin Skywalker, underscoring his dominance over the desert world.

22 BBY - The rescue of his son Rotta

During the Clone Wars, Jabba's son Rotta is kidnapped in a Separatist plot. Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano recover the Huttlet, and a grateful Jabba grants the Republic passage through Hutt-controlled space.

~22-19 BBY - Power during the Clone Wars

Jabba wields influence from the Grand Hutt Council and weathers threats to his family and holdings, including the treachery of Ziro the Hutt.

~1 BBY - Bounty on Han Solo

After the smuggler Han Solo jettisons a spice shipment to avoid capture, Jabba places a bounty on his head, sending bounty hunters after him.

0 BBY - Operations at the time of the Battle of Yavin

Jabba continues to run his smuggling and enforcement network out of Tatooine, confronting Han Solo over unpaid debts before Solo leaves on the mission to destroy the Death Star.

3 ABY - Han Solo delivered in carbonite

Following the events on Cloud City, the bounty hunter Boba Fett brings Han Solo, frozen in carbonite, to Jabba's Palace, where the smuggler is displayed as a trophy.

4 ABY - Death over the Great Pit of Carkoon

During the rescue of Han Solo, Jabba captures Luke Skywalker, Leia Organa, and their allies. Aboard his sail barge above the Sarlacc's pit, Leia strangles Jabba with the chain of her enslavement, killing him as the rescue erupts into battle.

title: Chewbacca
description: Chewbacca, affectionately called Chewie, was a Wookiee warrior, smuggler, mechanic, pilot, and resistance fighter from the forest planet Kashyyyk. Renowned for his loyalty, strength, and skill as a co-pilot, he fought across generations of galactic conflict — the Clone Wars, the Galactic Civil War, and the war between the First Order and the Resistance. During the Clone Wars he was captured by Trandoshan slavers and imprisoned on Wasskah, escaping with the aid of fellow captive Jedi Commander Ahsoka Tano, and later helped defend his homeworld and cover Jedi Grand Master Yoda's escape during the Battle of Kashyyyk. He is best known as the lifelong companion and co-pilot of Han Solo aboard the Millennium Falcon, a partnership that began around a decade before the Battle of Yavin. Bound to Han by a Wookiee life debt, Chewbacca became a central figure of the Rebel Alliance, fighting at battles such as Yavin, Hoth, and Endor, and continued to serve the New Republic and later the Resistance, standing beside a new generation of heroes into the war against the First Order.
timeline:
200 BBY - Birth on Kashyyyk

Chewbacca is born on the forest world of Kashyyyk, homeworld of the Wookiee people.

~21 BBY - Captured by Trandoshan Slavers

During the Clone Wars, Chewbacca is captured by Trandoshan slavers and taken to the moon Wasskah to be hunted for sport.

~21 BBY - Escape from Wasskah with Ahsoka Tano

Working alongside fellow captive Jedi Commander Ahsoka Tano and a group of younglings, Chewbacca helps organize a resistance and escapes the Trandoshans' hunting grounds.

19 BBY - Battle of Kashyyyk

Chewbacca and Chieftain Tarfful lead Wookiee forces defending Kashyyyk alongside the Grand Army of the Republic under Jedi General Yoda. When Order 66 is issued, the two Wookiees help Yoda escape the planet and the destruction of the Jedi Order.

~10 BBY - Meeting Han Solo

Imprisoned by the Empire, Chewbacca meets Han Solo on Mimban, and the two escape together, beginning a lifelong friendship. He takes part in the Kessel heist aboard the Millennium Falcon.

0 BBY - The Battle of Yavin

Chewbacca and Han take on a charter from Obi-Wan Kenobi and Luke Skywalker, becoming entangled in the Rebellion and aiding in the rescue of Princess Leia and the destruction of the Death Star.

3 ABY - The Battle of Hoth and Bespin

Fleeing the Empire, Chewbacca flies the Falcon to Cloud City, where he fights Imperial forces and helps rescue the frozen, carbonite-encased Han Solo's companions after Han is captured by Boba Fett.

4 ABY - Rescue of Han Solo and the Battle of Endor

Chewbacca helps free Han from Jabba the Hutt and joins the strike team on the forest moon of Endor, contributing to the destruction of the second Death Star.

34 ABY - The Hunt for Luke and the Death of Han Solo

Reunited with the Millennium Falcon, Chewbacca aids the Resistance against the First Order and is present when Han is killed by his son, Ben Solo (Kylo Ren), on Starkiller Base.

34 ABY - The Battle of Crait

Chewbacca continues to fly the Falcon and support the dwindling Resistance during its escape and stand at Crait.

35 ABY - The Battle of Exegol

Chewbacca fights in the final campaign against the resurrected Emperor Palpatine and the Sith Eternal, surviving the war as one of the Resistance's most enduring heroes.

title: Ki-Adi-Mundi
description: Ki-Adi-Mundi was a Cerean Jedi Master from the planet Cerea, distinguished by his species' towering binary brain and elongated cranium, who served on the Jedi High Council across an extraordinary span of galactic history — from the golden age of the High Republic through the fall of the Galactic Republic. He was already a seated Council member during the crisis on Brendok and the hunt for the Sith assassin Mae roughly a century before the Clone Wars, and he was still serving on the Council when Qui-Gon Jinn presented Anakin Skywalker to the Jedi after the Invasion of Naboo, famously insisting that the Sith had been extinct for a millennium. He fought in the Battle of Geonosis that opened the Clone Wars and became a Jedi General in the Grand Army of the Republic, leading clone forces at Geonosis's second campaign, Dantooine, Quell and elsewhere, and taking part in the Council's deepening suspicion of Chancellor Palpatine. Ki-Adi-Mundi died in 19 BBY on the crystalline world of Mygeeto, shot down by the Galactic Marines he commanded when Palpatine issued Order 66 and began the Great Jedi Purge.
timeline:
Unknown date - Birth on Cerea

Ki-Adi-Mundi was born on Cerea, a world of the Mid Rim. Canon has never established the year; he was already an adult Jedi Master more than a century before the Clone Wars.

132 BBY - Serving on the Jedi High Council during the High Republic

Mundi sat on the Jedi High Council on Coruscant during the final century of the High Republic, taking part in the Order's response to the murders of Jedi Master Indara and others and the investigation into the fugitive Mae.

132 BBY - The Brendok inquiry

He questioned Master Sol and the Council's handling of the events surrounding the witches of Brendok, and was among those who debated how much to disclose to the Senate as the Order's public standing came under strain.

32 BBY - The Naboo crisis and the return of the Sith

As a Council member, Mundi heard Qui-Gon Jinn's report of a Sith attacker on Tatooine and declared it impossible, insisting the Sith had been extinct for a millennium. He was present when the Council reluctantly permitted Anakin Skywalker's training after Qui-Gon's death.

22 BBY - Battle of Geonosis

Mundi joined Mace Windu's task force to rescue Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala on Geonosis, surviving the arena battle that ignited the Clone Wars.

22 BBY - Jedi General of the Grand Army

Commissioned as a Jedi General, Mundi took command of clone troopers — later the Galactic Marines under Commander Bacara — and led Republic offensives against the Separatist Alliance.

22–20 BBY - Campaigns of the Clone Wars

He fought across the galaxy during the war, including the second Geonosis campaign against Poggle the Lesser's droid foundries, actions on Dantooine and Quell, and the defense of Republic worlds against Separatist incursions.

19 BBY - Council suspicions of the Chancellor

In the war's final year Mundi joined Council debate over Palpatine's refusal to relinquish emergency powers and the growing conviction that the Sith Lord controlled the Senate.

19 BBY - Death on Mygeeto

Leading the Galactic Marines through the crystalline cityscape of Mygeeto, Mundi was cut down by Commander Bacara and his own clone troopers the moment Palpatine issued Order 66, becoming one of the first casualties of the Great Jedi Purge.

title: Count Dooku
description: Count Dooku, also known by his Sith title Darth Tyranus, was a Force-sensitive human male born into the noble ruling family of Serenno. Trained as a Jedi by Grand Master Yoda, he became one of the Order's most respected swordmasters and lightsaber duelists, but grew disillusioned with the Jedi's subservience to a Republic he saw as corrupt. Following the death of his former Padawan Qui-Gon Jinn, Dooku left the Jedi Order, reclaimed his hereditary title of Count of Serenno, and secretly became the second Sith apprentice of Darth Sidious. As the public leader and Head of State of the Confederacy of Independent Systems, he ignited and led the Separatist cause during the Clone Wars, orchestrating the conflict as part of Sidious's plan to destroy the Jedi and seize control of the galaxy. He trained dark side agents such as Asajj Ventress and Savage Opress and dueled the galaxy's greatest Jedi. Ultimately, Dooku was betrayed by his own master: during the Battle of Coruscant he was defeated and executed by Anakin Skywalker at Sidious's command, a casualty of the very Sith treachery he had embraced.
timeline:
102 BBY - Birth on Serenno

Dooku is born into the wealthy noble House of Serenno, heir to the title of Count. As a Force-sensitive infant he is given over to the Jedi Order and taken to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant.

~92 BBY - Training under Yoda

As a youngling and later Padawan, Dooku is trained in the ways of the Force by Grand Master Yoda, growing into one of the Order's most skilled and respected members and a master of lightsaber combat.

~44 BBY - Master to Qui-Gon Jinn

Dooku takes Qui-Gon Jinn as his Padawan, forging a bond that would later shape his disillusionment with the Jedi and the Republic.

32 BBY - Departure from the Jedi Order

The death of his former apprentice Qui-Gon Jinn at the Battle of Naboo deepens Dooku's disillusionment. He leaves the Jedi Order and reclaims his hereditary title as Count of Serenno.

~32 BBY - Becoming Darth Tyranus

Dooku is taken as the second apprentice of the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, adopting the identity Darth Tyranus. On Sidious's orders he arranges for the creation of a secret clone army on Kamino under the name of the late Jedi Sifo-Dyas.

~24 BBY - Rise of the Separatist movement

Dooku publicly emerges as the charismatic leader of a growing secessionist movement, rallying thousands of star systems in opposition to the Galactic Republic.

22 BBY - The Battle of Geonosis

Dooku's Separatist plans are exposed on Geonosis. He captures Obi-Wan Kenobi, duels Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker, and Yoda, and escapes as the first battle of the Clone Wars erupts, delivering the Death Star plans to Sidious.

22-19 BBY - Leading the Confederacy in the Clone Wars

As Head of State of the Confederacy of Independent Systems, Dooku directs the Separatist war effort, trains dark side agents such as Asajj Ventress and Savage Opress, and repeatedly clashes with Jedi across the galaxy.

19 BBY - Death at the Battle of Coruscant

During the Separatist assault on Coruscant, Dooku duels Anakin Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi aboard the flagship Invisible Hand. Anakin overpowers and disarms him, then decapitates him at Palpatine's command—revealing Dooku had been a disposable pawn in his own master's scheme.

title: Plo Koon
description:
"

Plo Koon was a male Kel Dor Jedi Master from the planet Dorin who served on the Jedi High Council through the final decades of the Galactic Republic. Renowned for his level-headed judgment, compassion and unflappable calm, he wore an antiox breath mask and protective goggles to survive in oxygen-rich atmospheres, and he was a gifted pilot and swordsman who also commanded the rare Force technique known as Electric Judgment. He sat on the Council during the Naboo Crisis and fought at the First Battle of Geonosis that opened the Clone Wars, then served as a Jedi General leading the 104th Battalion — the \"Wolfpack\" — alongside Clone Commander Wolffe, with whom he shared an unusually close and respectful bond, insisting that clones be treated as men rather than materiel. Among his most enduring legacies was discovering the Force-sensitive Togruta child Ahsoka Tano on Shili and bringing her to the Jedi Temple, remaining a mentor and protector to her for the rest of his life. He survived the destruction of his fleet by General Grievous's warship Malevolence, took part in the rescue of Even Piell from the Citadel, and fought across countless campaigns before Order 66 in 19 BBY, when the clone pilots escorting him over the bridge cities of Cato Neimoidia turned on their general and shot down his starfighter, killing him instantly.

"
timeline:
"

74 BBY - Birth on Dorin

Plo Koon is born a member of the Kel Dor species on Dorin, a world whose helium- and gas-rich atmosphere means he must wear an antiox mask and goggles anywhere else in the galaxy.

~36 BBY - Finding Ahsoka Tano on Shili

Sent by the Jedi Order to investigate a powerful Force signature, Plo Koon locates the infant Togruta Ahsoka Tano in the wilds of Shili and brings her to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant, beginning a lifelong bond as her protector.

32 BBY - The Naboo Crisis

Now a member of the Jedi High Council, Plo Koon is present when Qui-Gon Jinn presents Anakin Skywalker for testing and when the Council debates the return of the Sith after the Battle of Naboo.

22 BBY - The First Battle of Geonosis

Plo Koon joins the two-hundred-strong Jedi strike team that descends on Geonosis to rescue Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker and Padme Amidala, surviving the arena battle that ignites the Clone Wars.

22 BBY - General of the 104th Battalion

Given command of the clone troopers of the 104th Battalion, nicknamed the Wolfpack, Plo Koon forges a deep partnership with Commander Wolffe and insists his men be regarded as individuals rather than expendable soldiers.

22 BBY - Destruction of his fleet by the Malevolence

Plo Koon's task force is annihilated by General Grievous's ion-cannon warship Malevolence near Abregado; adrift in an escape pod with a handful of survivors including Wolffe, he is rescued by Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano and helps expose the weapon's nature.

~22 BBY - Defense of Kamino

Alongside Obi-Wan Kenobi, Plo Koon helps anticipate and repel a Separatist assault on the cloning facilities of Kamino led by Grievous and Asajj Ventress.

~21 BBY - The Citadel

Plo Koon takes part in the Republic operation to extract Jedi Master Even Piell from the Separatist prison fortress on Lola Sayu, commanding the relief force that recovers the survivors.

22-19 BBY - Campaigns of the Clone Wars

Across the war Plo Koon leads front-line actions from Felucia to the Outer Rim while continuing to serve on the Jedi High Council, and stands among the Masters who wrestle with Ahsoka Tano's expulsion and vindication.

19 BBY - Death over Cato Neimoidia

While patrolling the bridge cities of Cato Neimoidia in his Jedi starfighter, Plo Koon is caught by Order 66 when the clone pilots of his own escort open fire; his fighter is destroyed and he is killed in the crash, one of the first Jedi to fall in the purge.

"

title: Sheev Palpatine
description: Sheev Palpatine, secretly the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, was a human politician from Naboo who rose to become Supreme Chancellor of the Galactic Republic and ultimately Emperor of the first Galactic Empire. Trained in the dark side by Darth Plagueis, he cultivated dual identities — the affable public senator Palpatine and the shadowy Sith master Sidious — using both to engineer the galaxy's collapse. He manipulated the Trade Federation's blockade of Naboo to win the chancellorship, orchestrated the Clone Wars from both sides, and used the conflict as a pretext to purge the Jedi Order through Order 66 and declare himself Emperor in 19 BBY. He ruled the galaxy through fear and the dark side until he was cast down by Darth Vader at the Battle of Endor in 4 ABY. Yet Sidious cheated death: having prepared cloned bodies and a hidden cult on Exegol, he returned decades later, only to be finally destroyed by Rey and the combined spirits of the Jedi in 35 ABY. He is remembered as the galaxy's greatest villain and the architect of the Republic's fall and the Empire's rise.
timeline:
84 BBY - Birth on Naboo

Sheev Palpatine is born into an aristocratic family in Theed, the capital of Naboo.

~65 BBY - Apprenticeship to Darth Plagueis

As a young man, Palpatine is discovered and taken as the Sith apprentice of Darth Plagueis, becoming Darth Sidious and learning the ways of the dark side while building a political career.

~52 BBY - Enters Naboo politics

Palpatine establishes himself in public life, eventually representing Naboo as its senator in the Galactic Senate on Coruscant.

~32 BBY - Murder of Darth Plagueis

Following the Rule of Two, Sidious kills his master Plagueis in his sleep, becoming the reigning Sith Lord and taking his own apprentice, Darth Maul.

32 BBY - The Phantom Menace

Sidious orchestrates the Trade Federation's blockade and invasion of Naboo, using the ensuing crisis to have Chancellor Valorum ousted and to be elected Supreme Chancellor of the Republic.

22 BBY - Attack of the Clones and the Clone Wars begin

Manipulating the Separatist crisis through his apprentice Count Dooku, Palpatine is granted emergency powers as the Clone Wars erupt, secretly commanding both the Republic and Separatist forces.

22–19 BBY - The Clone Wars

Palpatine prolongs the galaxy-spanning war to consolidate power, erode the Senate, and set the Jedi Order up for destruction.

19 BBY - Revenge of the Sith

Sidious turns Anakin Skywalker to the dark side as Darth Vader, issues Order 66 to exterminate the Jedi, reorganizes the Republic into the Galactic Empire, and declares himself Emperor.

0 BBY - A New Hope

Emperor Palpatine rules through the Death Star and his apprentice Vader as the Rebel Alliance destroys the battle station at Yavin.

3 ABY - The Empire Strikes Back

The Emperor directs Vader to hunt down Luke Skywalker, sensing the young Jedi's potential and plotting to turn him.

4 ABY - Death at the Battle of Endor

Aboard the second Death Star, Palpatine attempts to corrupt Luke, but Vader turns on his master and hurls him into the reactor shaft, killing the Emperor at the Battle of Endor.

~5–35 ABY - Secret return on Exegol

Having transferred his essence into cloned bodies prepared on the hidden Sith world of Exegol, a weakened Sidious survives and builds the Sith Eternal cult and a secret armada while orchestrating events from the shadows.

35 ABY - The Rise of Skywalker

Palpatine reveals his return and lures his granddaughter Rey to Exegol. Drawing on the strength of all the Jedi, Rey turns his own Force lightning against him, destroying Sidious and his cult once and for all.

title: Qui-Gon Jinn
description: Qui-Gon Jinn was a Force-sensitive human male Jedi Master who lived during the final decades of the Galactic Republic. Trained by Count Dooku and later mentor to Obi-Wan Kenobi, he was respected within the Jedi Order for his wisdom and skill, yet his maverick adherence to the philosophy of the Living Force — following the will of the Force even when it conflicted with the High Council — kept him at odds with the Jedi Council, whose offered seat he never took up on the Council's terms. During the Naboo crisis of 32 BBY he discovered the young slave Anakin Skywalker on Tatooine and became convinced the boy was the prophesied Chosen One destined to bring balance to the Force. Qui-Gon was struck down by the Sith Lord Darth Maul during the Battle of Naboo; with his dying breath he charged Obi-Wan with training Anakin. He later became one of the first Jedi to learn to retain his consciousness after death, returning as a guiding voice through the Force.
timeline:
~80 BBY - Birth

Qui-Gon Jinn is born to a family on a terrestrial world that remains unidentified in canon, leaving the planet as an infant to be raised within the Jedi Order.

~72 BBY - Training in the Jedi Order

Identified as strongly Force-sensitive, Qui-Gon is trained at the Jedi Temple on Coruscant and eventually taken as the Padawan of Jedi Master Count Dooku.

~44 BBY - Master and Apprentice

Now a Jedi Master, Qui-Gon takes the young Obi-Wan Kenobi as his Padawan learner. Around this period he undertakes the fateful mission to Pijal recounted in the novel Master & Apprentice, and is offered but declines a seat on the Jedi High Council.

32 BBY - The Naboo Blockade

Sent with Obi-Wan by Chancellor Valorum to negotiate with the Trade Federation over its blockade of Naboo, Qui-Gon escapes an assassination attempt and reaches the surface, rescuing Queen Amidala and the Gungan Jar Jar Binks.

32 BBY - Discovery of Anakin Skywalker

Stranded on Tatooine, Qui-Gon meets the slave boy Anakin Skywalker, senses his extraordinary connection to the Force, and wins his freedom through a podrace, believing him to be the Chosen One.

32 BBY - Duel on Naboo and Death

During the liberation of Naboo, Qui-Gon duels the Sith Lord Darth Maul in the Theed generator complex and is mortally wounded. As he dies, he asks Obi-Wan to train Anakin as a Jedi.

~19 BBY and beyond - Voice in the Force

Having learned to preserve his identity after death, Qui-Gon's spirit later guides Obi-Wan Kenobi and Yoda, and speaks to Obi-Wan during his years of exile.

title: Mace Windu
description: Mace Windu was a revered human Jedi Master and one of the most powerful members of the Jedi High Council during the final decades of the Galactic Republic. Born on Haruun Kal, he rose to become the Order's foremost warrior, a master of the Force and creator of the rare lightsaber combat form Vaapad, wielding a distinctive amethyst-bladed lightsaber. He served for a time as Master of the Order and, during the Clone Wars, as a senior Jedi General in the Grand Army of the Republic. A staunch defender of Jedi tradition and deeply wary of Anakin Skywalker and the growing shadow over the Republic, Windu ultimately uncovered Supreme Chancellor Palpatine as the Sith Lord Darth Sidious. He nearly defeated the Sith in single combat, but was betrayed by Anakin and killed, a turning point that sealed the fall of the Jedi Order and the rise of the Galactic Empire.
timeline:
72 BBY - Birth on Haruun Kal

Mace Windu is born on the humid jungle world of Haruun Kal, home to the Korunnai people, and is identified as strong in the Force.

~70 BBY - Taken into the Jedi Order

Discovered to be Force-sensitive, the young Windu is brought to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant to be trained as a Jedi.

~40 BBY - Rise to Jedi Master and the High Council

Windu becomes one of the most accomplished Jedi of his generation, earning a seat on the Jedi High Council and developing the aggressive lightsaber form known as Vaapad.

32 BBY - The Phantom Menace

As a senior Council member, Windu helps question the captured Sith threat and is present when Qui-Gon Jinn presents Anakin Skywalker; he is skeptical of training the boy but consents after Qui-Gon's death.

22 BBY - Attack of the Clones and the Battle of Geonosis

Windu leads a Jedi strike team to rescue Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin, and Padmé on Geonosis, personally slaying the bounty hunter Jango Fett as the Clone Wars erupt.

22–19 BBY - Jedi General in the Clone Wars

Windu serves as a general in the Grand Army of the Republic, leading campaigns across the galaxy and serving at times as Master of the Order while balancing his duties on the Council.

19 BBY - Revenge of the Sith: exposing Darth Sidious

After Anakin reveals that Chancellor Palpatine is the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, Windu leads a team of Jedi Masters to arrest him and defeats him in a duel in the Chancellor's office.

19 BBY - Death on Coruscant

As Windu prepares to end the Sith threat, Anakin intervenes and severs his hand; Sidious then unleashes Force lightning and hurls Windu from the window to his death, a betrayal that marks Anakin's turn to the dark side.

title: Nute Gunray
description: Nute Gunray was a Neimoidian male business executive and politician who rose to become Viceroy of the Trade Federation and, later, one of the most visible figures of the Confederacy of Independent Systems. Greedy, cowardly and easily manipulated, he was recruited by the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, who used the Federation's commercial grievance over the taxation of trade routes as the pretext for the blockade and invasion of Naboo in 32 BBY — a manufactured crisis engineered to topple Supreme Chancellor Finis Valorum and elevate Sidious's public persona, Senator Sheev Palpatine, to the chancellorship. Gunray occupied Naboo, imprisoned its population and pressured Queen Padmé Amidala to sign a treaty legitimizing the occupation, only to be captured when the Naboo and Gungans retook Theed. Despite four trials before the Supreme Court he kept his title and his freedom, and by 22 BBY he had thrown in with Count Dooku's separatist movement, demanding Amidala's death as the price of the Trade Federation's droid armies and signing the Confederacy into being on Geonosis. Throughout the Clone Wars he sat on the Separatist Council, surviving capture on Rodia in a scheme that saw him briefly held by the Republic before Asajj Ventress broke him out. His usefulness ended with the war: hidden on Mustafar with the rest of the Council on Sidious's orders, he was cut down by the newly christened Darth Vader in 19 BBY, the final casualty of the conspiracy he had helped set in motion.
timeline:
Birth year unknown - Born on Neimoidia

Nute Gunray was born a Neimoidian male, a species raised in grub hives whose survivalist upbringing bred the acquisitiveness and cowardice that defined him. Canon sources record no birth year.

~34 BBY - Rises to Viceroy of the Trade Federation

Gunray climbed the Trade Federation hierarchy to the office of Viceroy, commanding its vast mercantile fleet and battle droid army, and came under the secret influence of Darth Sidious.

32 BBY - Blockade of Naboo

On Sidious's instruction, Gunray ringed Naboo with Trade Federation battleships in protest of the Republic's taxation of the outlying trade routes, opening the crisis that would make Palpatine Supreme Chancellor.

32 BBY - Attempted murder of the Jedi ambassadors

When Chancellor Valorum sent Jedi Qui-Gon Jinn and Obi-Wan Kenobi to negotiate, Gunray tried to have them killed aboard his flagship with dioxis gas and droidekas; both escaped to the planet below.

32 BBY - Invasion and occupation of Naboo

Gunray landed his droid army, seized Theed, and herded the Naboo into camps while pressing Queen Amidala to sign a treaty that would make the occupation legal. She refused and fled offworld.

32 BBY - Defeat at the Battle of Naboo and capture

Amidala returned with Gungan allies; when Anakin Skywalker destroyed the Droid Control Ship, Gunray's army shut down and he was taken prisoner in his own throne room and delivered to the Republic.

Between 32 and 22 BBY - Four trials, no punishment

Gunray faced four trials before the Supreme Court and escaped meaningful sanction, retaining his viceroyalty — an outcome that deepened public cynicism about Republic justice.

22 BBY - Backs the Separatist movement and demands Amidala's death

Approached by Count Dooku on Geonosis, Gunray agreed to commit the Trade Federation's droid armies to the Confederacy only on the condition that Senator Padmé Amidala be assassinated; earlier attempts on her life on Coruscant were made at his urging.

22 BBY - Signs the Confederacy into existence

In the Geonosian war room Gunray signed the treaty founding the Confederacy of Independent Systems alongside the Techno Union, Banking Clan and Commerce Guild, and took a seat on the Separatist Council.

22 BBY - Battle of Geonosis

The Clone Wars erupted with the Republic's assault on Geonosis. Gunray fled the planet as his droid forces were routed, and spent the war moving between hidden Separatist strongholds.

~21 BBY - Capture on Rodia

Lured to Rodia by Senator Onaconda Farr's feigned defection, Gunray was arrested by Padmé Amidala and Jar Jar Binks and taken into Republic custody as the highest-value Separatist prisoner of the war.

~21 BBY - Escape aboard the Tranquility

While being transported for interrogation by Jedi Luminara Unduli and Ahsoka Tano, Gunray was freed by Asajj Ventress and the treacherous Senate Guard captain Argyus, returning to the Separatist Council.

19 BBY - Ordered into hiding on Mustafar

With Dooku and General Grievous dead and the war ending, Darth Sidious directed Gunray and the remaining Separatist leaders to the volcanic world of Mustafar, promising their safety.

19 BBY - Killed by Darth Vader

Anakin Skywalker, newly anointed Darth Vader, arrived on Mustafar and slaughtered the Separatist Council. Gunray, begging for mercy and reminding Vader that Sidious had promised him safety, was the last to be cut down — ending the Confederacy and the Clone Wars.

title: Bail Organa
description: Bail Prestor Organa was a human male from Alderaan who served as his world's senator, Viceroy and First Chairman, and became one of the founding architects of the rebellion against the Galactic Empire. A principled voice for diplomacy in the Galactic Senate during the twilight of the Republic, he sat on the Loyalist Committee alongside Padmé Amidala and Mon Mothma, resisted Chancellor Palpatine's steady accumulation of emergency powers throughout the Clone Wars, and was among the signatories of the Petition of the Two Thousand. He witnessed the execution of Order 66 firsthand at the Jedi Temple, helped Obi-Wan Kenobi and Yoda escape into hiding, and afterward adopted Padmé's newborn daughter Leia, raising her as a princess of Alderaan with his wife, Queen Breha Organa. Over the next two decades he used Alderaan's wealth and his own senatorial cover to fund and shelter dissident cells — supporting the Lothal rebels, recruiting Ahsoka Tano as an intelligence coordinator under the codename Fulcrum, and working with Mon Mothma to knit scattered insurgencies into the Alliance to Restore the Republic. He was killed in 0 BBY when the Death Star destroyed Alderaan, shortly after sending Leia to Tatooine to find Obi-Wan Kenobi.
timeline:
~67 BBY - Birth on Alderaan

Bail Prestor Organa is born into Alderaan's ruling House of Organa. Canon never states the year outright; 67 BBY comes from reference-book material and should be read as an estimate.

~32 BBY - Enters the Galactic Senate

Organa takes up Alderaan's seat in the Galactic Senate around the time of the Naboo crisis, beginning decades of service as a reformist and advocate of diplomacy.

22 BBY - The Loyalist Committee and the Military Creation Act

As separatist pressure mounts, Organa serves on Chancellor Palpatine's Loyalist Committee with Padmé Amidala and Mas Amedda, and is present in the Chancellor's office when emergency powers are granted and the Clone Wars begin.

22-19 BBY - The Clone Wars

Throughout the war Organa leads humanitarian relief efforts, negotiates for neutral worlds such as Mandalore and Christophsis, and works with Amidala and Mon Mothma to oppose bills expanding military spending and executive authority.

19 BBY - The Petition of the Two Thousand

Organa joins Amidala, Mon Mothma and other senators in formally demanding that Palpatine return his wartime powers to the Senate — an act that plants the seed of the future Rebel Alliance.

19 BBY - Order 66 and the fall of the Republic

Organa rides to the burning Jedi Temple and narrowly escapes clone troopers, then rescues Yoda and Obi-Wan Kenobi aboard the Tantive IV. He hears Palpatine proclaim the Galactic Empire before the applauding Senate.

19 BBY - Adopting Leia

After Padmé Amidala dies giving birth on Polis Massa, Organa takes the infant Leia home to Alderaan, where he and Breha raise her as their daughter and heir, concealing her true parentage.

~18-10 BBY - Quiet resistance

Serving the Imperial Senate as a loyal-seeming statesman, Organa funnels Alderaanian resources to displaced peoples, shelters hunted Jedi and dissidents, and quietly seeks out insurgent groups.

~9 BBY - Leia's kidnapping and Obi-Wan's return

When ten-year-old Leia is abducted by mercenaries working for the Inquisitorius, Organa calls on Obi-Wan Kenobi to recover her, and is later forced to send a desperate transmission to Tatooine as the hunt closes in on his family.

~5-2 BBY - Building the Alliance

Organa recruits Ahsoka Tano to coordinate rebel intelligence as Fulcrum, supplies the Phoenix Cell and the Lothal rebels, and works with Mon Mothma to unify scattered cells into the Alliance to Restore the Republic.

0 BBY - Yavin 4 and the mission to Kenobi

With news of the Death Star confirmed, Organa breaks openly with the Empire at the Alliance council on Yavin 4, pledges Alderaan's support, and sends Leia to Tatooine to bring Obi-Wan Kenobi into the war.

0 BBY - Death in the destruction of Alderaan

Grand Moff Tarkin orders the Death Star to destroy Alderaan as a demonstration of Imperial power. Bail and Breha Organa die with their world and its people.

title: Jango Fett
description: Jango Fett was a human Mandalorian bounty hunter widely regarded as the best in the galaxy during the final years of the Galactic Republic. Hailing from Concord Dawn, he wore distinctive Mandalorian armor equipped with a jetpack, a flamethrower, and dual WESTAR-34 blaster pistols, and flew the Firespray-class starship Slave I. Around 32 BBY he was chosen — through Count Dooku, acting on behalf of the fallen Jedi Sifo-Dyas — as the genetic template for the clone army secretly bred on Kamino, and in payment he requested an unaltered clone whom he raised as his son, Boba Fett. In 22 BBY, Fett was hired by Dooku to assassinate Senator Padmé Amidala, a plot that unraveled when Obi-Wan Kenobi tracked him to Kamino and then to Geonosis. During the First Battle of Geonosis, Fett was killed in the arena by Jedi Master Mace Windu, though his genetic legacy endured in the Grand Army of the Republic and in his son.
timeline:
~66 BBY - Birth on Concord Dawn

Jango Fett is born, becoming tied to the Mandalorian people and their warrior culture.

Rise as a Bounty Hunter

Fett grows into the galaxy's most feared and capable bounty hunter, mastering marksmanship and unarmed combat and adopting his signature Mandalorian armor and starship Slave I.

~32 BBY - Chosen as the Clone Template

Recruited through Count Dooku on behalf of the Jedi Sifo-Dyas, Fett becomes the genetic source for the clone army bred in secret on Kamino. As part of his payment he requests an unaltered clone, whom he names Boba and raises as a son.

22 BBY - The Assassination Plot

Working for Dooku, Fett is contracted to kill Senator Padmé Amidala. He hires the shapeshifter Zam Wesell, then kills her with a toxic dart to keep his employer's identity hidden.

22 BBY - Confrontation on Kamino

Obi-Wan Kenobi traces the assassination to the cloning facility on Kamino and confronts Fett, who battles the Jedi and escapes with Boba aboard Slave I, dueling Kenobi through an asteroid field before fleeing to Geonosis.

22 BBY - Death at the Battle of Geonosis

During the First Battle of Geonosis, Fett fights in the arena but is beheaded by Jedi Master Mace Windu, ending his career as the clone template of the Republic army.

title: General Grievous
description: Grievous, born Qymaen jai Sheelal, was a Kaleesh male warlord from the Wild Space world of Kalee who became the cyborg Supreme Commander of the Droid Army of the Confederacy of Independent Systems during the Clone Wars. A brutal and calculating field commander rather than a Force-sensitive, he was rebuilt into a towering armored cyborg housing little more than his organic brain, eyes and internal organs, and he was trained in lightsaber combat by Count Dooku so that he could hunt Jedi — a pursuit that made him infamous for the collection of lightsabers taken from his kills. Serving directly beneath Dooku and, unknowingly, the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, Grievous led Separatist campaigns across the galaxy from his flagship, including the Malevolence offensive, the capture of Jedi Master Eeth Koth, an attempted invasion of Kamino, and the raid on the Jedi Temple's holocron vault by way of his forces. His greatest strike came in 19 BBY when he led the Separatist attack on Coruscant and abducted Supreme Chancellor Palpatine, only to lose his fleet's flagship and flee to Utapau, where he was cornered and killed by Jedi General Obi-Wan Kenobi — a death that, with Dooku's, effectively decapitated the Separatist war effort on the eve of the Empire.
timeline:
Unknown date - Birth on Kalee

Qymaen jai Sheelal is born a Kaleesh male on the Wild Space world of Kalee. Canon sources do not establish a year.

Before 22 BBY - Rebuilt as a cyborg

Sheelal's body is destroyed and he is reconstructed as a heavily armored cyborg, retaining only his brain, eyes and some organs. Taking the name Grievous, he enters the service of the Confederacy of Independent Systems.

Before 22 BBY - Trained by Count Dooku

Count Dooku personally instructs Grievous in lightsaber combat, teaching the non-Force-sensitive general to fight and kill Jedi. Grievous begins collecting the lightsabers of his victims as trophies.

22 BBY - Supreme Commander of the Droid Army

With the outbreak of the Clone Wars, Grievous commands the Separatist droid armies in the field, becoming one of the most feared figures of the war.

~22 BBY - The Malevolence campaign

Grievous commands the ion-cannon warship Malevolence in a terror campaign against Republic fleets and medical stations before Jedi forces cripple and destroy it.

~22 BBY - Battle of Bothawui

Grievous's armored column is ambushed and defeated by Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano, forcing the general to abandon the field.

~21 BBY - Capture of Eeth Koth

Grievous overwhelms and captures Jedi Master Eeth Koth, broadcasting the humiliation across the galaxy; a Jedi strike team led by Obi-Wan Kenobi rescues Koth, but Grievous escapes.

~21 BBY - Invasion of Kamino

Alongside Asajj Ventress, Grievous leads a Separatist assault on the cloning facilities of Kamino, which is repelled by Republic and clone defenders.

~20 BBY - Defeat on Florrum

Grievous is captured by pirates and Jedi younglings on Florrum and later traded back to the Separatists in a prisoner exchange.

19 BBY - Battle of Coruscant

Grievous leads the surprise Separatist attack on the Republic capital and abducts Supreme Chancellor Palpatine, holding him aboard the flagship Invisible Hand.

19 BBY - Escape from the Invisible Hand

After Anakin Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi board his flagship and Dooku is killed, Grievous flees the crippled cruiser in an escape pod and retreats to Utapau, becoming leader of the Separatist Council in Dooku's absence.

19 BBY - Death at the Battle of Utapau

Tracked to Utapau, Grievous duels Obi-Wan Kenobi and is killed when Kenobi shoots him in his exposed organic sac, igniting him from within. His death precedes the execution of Order 66 and the collapse of the Confederacy.

title: Wilhuff Tarkin
description: Wilhuff Tarkin was an Eriaduan bureaucrat, military officer, and politician whose career bridged the final decades of the Galactic Republic and the rise of the Galactic Empire. Born into the prestigious, militaristic Tarkin family in 64 BBY, he served in the Republic's Outland Regions Security Force and as Governor of Eriadu before returning to military service during the Clone Wars, where he became a close ally of Chancellor Sheev Palpatine. Under the Empire he rose to become the first Grand Moff, commanding the Outer Rim territories, and he championed the "Tarkin Doctrine" of rule through fear of overwhelming force. He is best known for overseeing the completion of the first Death Star, ordering the destruction of Alderaan, and dying aboard the battle station when it was destroyed by Luke Skywalker at the Battle of Yavin in 0 BBY.
timeline:
64 BBY - Birth on Eriadu

Wilhuff Tarkin is born on the Outer Rim world of Eriadu, a scion of the ambitious and militaristic Tarkin family.

Republic Era - Early Career and Governorship

Tarkin serves in the Republic's Outland Regions Security Force before entering politics, eventually becoming lieutenant governor and then Governor of Eriadu and the Seswenna sector.

~22 BBY - Clone Wars Service

With the outbreak of the Clone Wars, Tarkin renews his military service as a captain in the Republic Navy, serving under Jedi General Even Piell.

~21 BBY - Imprisonment at the Citadel

Captured and held at the Separatist Citadel prison, Tarkin is rescued alongside Even Piell by a Jedi-led team including Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano, deepening his ties to the Republic's leadership.

~19 BBY - Rise Under Palpatine

Promoted to admiral and increasingly trusted by Chancellor Palpatine, Tarkin becomes a leading figure in the militarization of the Republic as the Clone Wars draw to a close.

19 BBY - Founding of the Empire

With the proclamation of the Galactic Empire, Tarkin is named its first Moff and is given oversight of the secret Death Star project, helping formulate the Tarkin Doctrine of rule through fear.

~18-4 BBY - Grand Moff and Imperial Enforcer

Elevated to the rank of Grand Moff, Tarkin is granted authority over the Outer Rim. He pursues rebel cells, oversees Imperial projects, and works to bring the Death Star to completion.

0 BBY - Death Star Operational and Destruction of Alderaan

The Death Star becomes operational under Tarkin's command. To demonstrate its power and coerce Princess Leia Organa, he orders the annihilation of the planet Alderaan.

0 BBY - Death at the Battle of Yavin

Refusing to evacuate the battle station, Tarkin is killed when Luke Skywalker destroys the Death Star during the Battle of Yavin.

title: Cad Bane
description: Cad Bane was a ruthless Duros bounty hunter from the planet Duro, widely regarded as the best and most feared bounty hunter of the Clone Wars era following the death of Jango Fett at the First Battle of Geonosis. Recognizable by his wide-brimmed hat, twin blaster pistols, and cold professionalism, he was repeatedly employed by Darth Sidious for high-risk operations, including a raid on the Jedi Temple to steal a holocron and, later, the kidnapping of Force-sensitive infants. He clashed with Jedi such as Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker, and Mace Windu, orchestrated the breakout of Ziro the Hutt, and briefly allied with figures like Aurra Sing and Darth Maul. In the war's final months he was hired to recover the enhanced clone Omega, crossing paths with Clone Force 99 and mentoring—and dueling—a young Boba Fett. Decades later, during the era of the New Republic, Bane resurfaced on Tatooine working for the Pyke Syndicate, where he was ultimately killed by his old rival Boba Fett.
timeline:
~62 BBY - Birth on Duro

Cad Bane is born a Duros, on or from the planet Duro; the year is derived from his apparent age rather than a directly stated canon date.

~27 BBY - Working the underworld

Bane establishes himself as a hardened bounty hunter, working alongside associates such as Aurra Sing years before the Clone Wars.

22 BBY - Rise to the top

Following Jango Fett's death at the First Battle of Geonosis, Bane becomes recognized as the galaxy's premier bounty hunter.

~22 BBY - Holocron Heist

Hired by Darth Sidious, Bane infiltrates the Jedi Temple, steals a holocron, and later kidnaps Force-sensitive children as part of a Separatist scheme before being captured.

~21 BBY - Senate hostage crisis and Ziro's breakout

Bane seizes hostages within the Galactic Senate to force the release of Ziro the Hutt, then breaks the crime lord out of prison.

~20 BBY - Continued Clone Wars contracts

Bane takes on numerous jobs, dueling Jedi including Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker and briefly crossing paths with Darth Maul's operations.

~19 BBY - Hunt for Omega

In the final year of the war and its immediate aftermath, Bane is hired to recover the young clone Omega, wounding Fennec Shand and clashing with Clone Force 99.

~19 BBY - Mentor and rival to Boba Fett

Bane encounters and duels a young Boba Fett, seemingly killing him before Boba survives, seeding a lasting rivalry.

9 ABY - Return on Tatooine

Working for the Pyke Syndicate during the Pyke conflict on Tatooine, Bane intimidates and shoots the marshal Cobb Vanth and pressures Boba Fett to abandon his claim.

9 ABY - Death at Boba Fett's hands

In the Battle of Mos Espa, Bane faces Boba Fett in a final showdown and is run through with a Tusken gaderffii stick, ending his life.

title: Jar Jar Binks
description: Jar Jar Binks was a Gungan from the swamps of Naboo whose bumbling good intentions repeatedly placed him at the center of galactic history. Banished from Otoh Gunga for his clumsiness, he was rescued by Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn during the Trade Federation's invasion of Naboo in 32 BBY and served as the guide who brought the Jedi and Queen Padmé Amidala to his people, brokering the Gungan-Naboo alliance that broke the occupation; he was promoted to Bombad General for his accidental heroics at the Battle of Naboo. Elevated to Junior Representative for Naboo in the Galactic Senate, he was serving in Senator Amidala's place in 22 BBY when he proposed granting Supreme Chancellor Palpatine emergency powers — the motion that created the Grand Army of the Republic and set the Republic on the path to becoming the Galactic Empire. Through the Clone Wars he undertook improbable missions to Rodia, Aleen, Bardotta and his own homeworld, often paired with Jedi such as Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi and Mace Windu, and he was among the mourners at Padmé Amidala's funeral in 19 BBY. Under the Empire he faded from politics, and by 5 ABY he was living in Theed as a shunned clown and street performer, entertaining refugee children while adults blamed him for the Empire's rise.
timeline:
~52 BBY - Birth on Naboo

Jar Jar Binks is born a Gungan on the planet Naboo. Canon never states a birth year; the commonly quoted 52 BBY is a Legends-era figure and should be treated as an estimate.

Before 32 BBY - Exile from Otoh Gunga

His clumsiness and repeated accidents lead Boss Rugor Nass to banish him from the Gungan city of Otoh Gunga, leaving him to scavenge alone in the Naboo swamps.

32 BBY - Meeting Qui-Gon Jinn

During the Trade Federation invasion, Jar Jar is nearly crushed by an invasion transport and saved by Qui-Gon Jinn, whom he pledges a life debt to and guides, with Obi-Wan Kenobi, to Otoh Gunga.

32 BBY - Journey to Tatooine and Coruscant

Traveling with Queen Padmé Amidala's party, he is stranded on Tatooine, where the group meets young Anakin Skywalker, before continuing to Coruscant to petition the Senate.

32 BBY - Brokering the Gungan-Naboo alliance

Returning to Naboo, Jar Jar leads the Queen to the Gungan sacred place, where Amidala's plea unites the two peoples against the Trade Federation.

32 BBY - Battle of Naboo and promotion to Bombad General

Named a general by Boss Nass, he blunders his way through the Gungan Grand Army's clash with the droid army on the Great Grass Plains, contributing to the liberation of Naboo.

Between 32 and 22 BBY - Junior Representative in the Senate

Jar Jar becomes Naboo's Junior Representative in the Galactic Senate, serving alongside Padmé Amidala after she leaves the throne to become senator.

22 BBY - Motion granting Palpatine emergency powers

Standing in for Amidala after an assassination attempt, he moves that the Senate grant Supreme Chancellor Palpatine emergency powers, enabling the creation of the clone army and the outbreak of the Clone Wars.

22 BBY - Mission to Rodia

Mistaken for a Jedi while accompanying Amidala on Rodia, "Bombad Jedi" Jar Jar helps free her from Nute Gunray's grasp and keeps the Rodians from defecting to the Separatists.

22 BBY - Clone Wars campaigns

He serves in a string of unlikely wartime episodes, including the rescue of Kit Fisto's forces alongside Obi-Wan Kenobi and the effort to stop Nuvo Vindi's Blue Shadow Virus on Naboo.

21 BBY - Relief mission to Aleen

Sent with clone commander and droids C-3PO and R2-D2 on a humanitarian mission to the quake-stricken world of Aleen, Jar Jar helps restore the planet's balance.

20 BBY - The Naboo doppelganger plot

When the Separatist scientist Rish Loo manipulates Boss Lyonie to turn the Gungans against Theed, Jar Jar impersonates the Boss and rallies the Gungan army back to Naboo's side.

20 BBY - Mission to Bardotta

At the personal request of Queen Julia, with whom he shares a romance, Jar Jar and Mace Windu investigate the disappearance of the Bardottan Dagoyan Masters and stop the Frangawl Cult.

19 BBY - Padmé Amidala's funeral

After the fall of the Republic and the proclamation of the Galactic Empire, Jar Jar walks in the funeral procession for Padmé Amidala in Theed.

19 BBY to 5 ABY - Outcast under the Empire

Blamed by many for the Empire's rise and shunned once more by his own people, he leaves politics behind and lives quietly on Naboo.

5 ABY - Clown of Theed

Performing as a clown in the Plaza of the Catalan, Jar Jar befriends Mapo, a boy burned and orphaned by Imperial bombing, and agrees to train him as a performer — the last canon glimpse of his life.

description: Thrawn, born Kivu'raw'nuru with the core name Vurawn and known during his early Chiss military career as Mitth'raw'nuru, was a Chiss male officer of the Chiss Ascendancy who became a Grand Admiral in the Imperial Navy during the Galactic Empire. Distinguished by blue skin, glowing red eyes, and an angular face, he was renowned as a brilliant, methodical, and ruthless strategist who believed victory required deeply understanding an enemy's philosophy, art, and culture. After defending the Ascendancy's borders in the Unknown Regions, he allied himself with the Empire, rose swiftly through its ranks under mentors and rivals alike, and was tasked by Governor Arihnda Pryce with crushing the growing rebellion on Lothal. His campaign against the Spectres ended when the purrgil dragged his flagship, the Chimaera, into hyperspace along with Ezra Bridger, stranding him in the distant galaxy of Peridea. Years later he returned to known space amid the events of the Ahsoka series, remaining one of the Empire's most formidable surviving commanders.
title: Thrawn
timeline:
~59 BBY - Birth on Rentor

Thrawn is born a Chiss of the Ascendancy on the world of Rentor, given the name Kivu'raw'nuru. The year is not stated in canon and is an estimate drawn from later context.

~19 BBY - Rise in the Chiss Ascendancy

Adopted into the Mitth family and serving as a naval officer, Thrawn defends the Ascendancy against threats in the Unknown Regions, displaying the tactical genius that defines his career.

~19 BBY - Encounter with Anakin Skywalker

During the Clone Wars, Thrawn crosses paths with Jedi General Anakin Skywalker while pursuing a shared threat, an alliance he later recalls.

~18 BBY - Defection to the Galactic Empire

Exiled to the fringes of Chiss space, Thrawn is discovered by Imperial forces and brought before Emperor Palpatine, joining the Empire alongside translator Eli Vanto.

~5 BBY - Promotion to Grand Admiral

Through a string of decisive victories, Thrawn ascends the Imperial hierarchy to become one of only twelve Grand Admirals in the Imperial Navy.

~4 BBY - War against the rebels of Lothal

At Governor Arihnda Pryce's request, Thrawn takes command of efforts to dismantle the rebel cell on Lothal, studying his enemies and hunting the Spectres and the wider rebellion.

~2 BBY - Uncovering Project Stardust and the Bendu

Thrawn presses his campaign against the rebels, contends with the mysterious Bendu on Atollon, and oversees Imperial development projects while closing in on the Lothal resistance.

~1 BBY - Vanishing over Lothal

During the liberation of Lothal, Ezra Bridger lures purrgil to seize Thrawn's flagship, the Chimaera, and jump to hyperspace, carrying Thrawn and Ezra to the distant galaxy of Peridea.

~9 ABY - Return from Peridea

Stranded for years, Thrawn is located by Morgan Elsbeth's allies; during the events of the Ahsoka series he boards the Chimaera and escapes Peridea, returning toward the known galaxy as a renewed threat.

endYearEvent: Episode IV: A New Hope

title: Obi-Wan Kenobi
description: Obi-Wan Kenobi, also known in his later years as Ben Kenobi, was a legendary human Jedi Master born in 57 BBY on Stewjon and taken to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant as a Force-sensitive child. Trained as the Padawan of Qui-Gon Jinn, he rose to become a Jedi Knight and later a Jedi Master and member of the Jedi High Council. He is best known for training Anakin Skywalker, serving as a Jedi General in the Grand Army of the Republic during the Clone Wars, and surviving Order 66 and the fall of the Republic. After Anakin's turn to the dark side as Darth Vader—whom Obi-Wan defeated on Mustafar—he went into exile on Tatooine to watch over the infant Luke Skywalker. Emerging years later to aid the Rebellion, he mentored Luke in the ways of the Force before sacrificing himself in a duel with Vader aboard the Death Star in 0 BBY, becoming one with the Force and continuing to guide Luke as a spectral presence.
timeline:
57 BBY - Birth on Stewjon

Obi-Wan Kenobi is born on the planet Stewjon. Discovered to be Force-sensitive, he is taken as an infant to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant to begin his training.

~44 BBY - Apprenticed to Qui-Gon Jinn

As a Jedi youngling nearing the age limit for selection, Obi-Wan is taken as the Padawan learner of Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn.

32 BBY - The Invasion of Naboo

During the crisis over Naboo, Obi-Wan and Qui-Gon encounter Anakin Skywalker. After Qui-Gon is slain by Darth Maul, Obi-Wan defeats the Sith and, honoring his master's dying wish, pledges to train Anakin. He is knighted and takes Anakin as his Padawan.

22 BBY - Outbreak of the Clone Wars

Investigating an assassination attempt on Padmé Amidala, Obi-Wan discovers the clone army on Kamino and the Separatist plot on Geonosis. The Battle of Geonosis begins the Clone Wars, in which he serves as a Jedi General.

22–19 BBY - Jedi General in the Clone Wars

Now a Jedi Master and Council member, Obi-Wan leads clone troopers across countless campaigns alongside Anakin and Ahsoka Tano, becoming one of the war's most celebrated commanders.

19 BBY - Duel on Mustafar and Order 66

Obi-Wan defeats General Grievous, then survives Order 66. After Anakin falls to the dark side and becomes Darth Vader, Obi-Wan defeats him in a duel on Mustafar, leaving him gravely wounded. He then delivers the newborn Luke Skywalker to the Lars family on Tatooine.

~19–0 BBY - Exile on Tatooine

Living as the hermit "Ben" Kenobi, Obi-Wan watches over Luke from afar while remaining in hiding from the Empire.

9 BBY - Rescue of Leia Organa

Drawn out of exile, Obi-Wan rescues a kidnapped young Leia Organa and confronts a resurgent Darth Vader, reaffirming his resolve to protect the Skywalker children.

0 BBY - Sacrifice aboard the Death Star

Reuniting with Luke, Obi-Wan begins his Jedi training and travels to the Death Star. In a final duel with Darth Vader, he allows himself to be struck down, vanishing into the Force so he can guide Luke as a spectral mentor.

3 ABY - Guidance on Hoth and beyond

As a Force spirit, Obi-Wan appears to Luke, urging him to seek out Yoda on Dagobah to complete his Jedi training.

title: Bib Fortuna
description: Bib Fortuna was a male Twi'lek from Ryloth who rose from the criminal underworld to become the majordomo and chief of staff of the Hutt crime lord Jabba Desilijic Tiure. Born into a prominent Twi'lek clan with deep ties to organized crime — much to the disgust of his politically minded cousin Beezer Fortuna — he worked as a slave trader preying on his own species before entering Jabba's service. For decades he ran the day-to-day operations of Jabba's Palace on Tatooine, screening visitors, managing the court and its entertainments, and handling his master's business, all while privately loathing the Hutt and coveting his throne. He was present in Jabba's box at the Boonta Eve Classic podrace on Tatooine in 32 BBY, remained at the Hutt's side through the Clone Wars and the rise of the Empire, and in 4 ABY admitted Luke Skywalker into the palace during the Rebel effort to rescue Han Solo — an intrusion that ended with Jabba's death aboard his sail barge over the Great Pit of Carkoon. In the power vacuum that followed, Fortuna at last claimed the palace and Jabba's territory for himself, ruling as a minor crime lord until 9 ABY, when the bounty hunter Boba Fett, returned from the Sarlacc, shot him dead and took the throne.
timeline:
Date Unknown - Birth on Ryloth

Bib Fortuna was born a male Twi'lek on Ryloth, into a prominent clan with close ties to the criminal underworld. Canon gives no birth year; he was clearly an adult by 32 BBY.

Date Unknown - Slave trader in the underworld

Before entering Hutt service, Fortuna worked as a slave trader who trafficked in members of his own species, a trade his cousin Beezer Fortuna resented the family for.

Date Unknown - Becomes Jabba's majordomo

Fortuna took service with Jabba Desilijic Tiure on Tatooine, eventually becoming his majordomo and chief of staff and running Jabba's Palace day to day.

32 BBY - The Boonta Eve Classic

Fortuna attended the Boonta Eve Classic podrace in Jabba's private box at the Mos Espa Grand Arena, the race won by the young slave Anakin Skywalker.

~19 BBY - Serving Jabba under the new Empire

In the aftermath of the Clone Wars and the founding of the Galactic Empire, Fortuna continued to receive visitors and conduct business on Jabba's behalf at the palace, including dealings with mercenaries and clone deserters passing through Tatooine.

4 ABY - Admitting Luke Skywalker

When Luke Skywalker came to the palace to free Han Solo, Fortuna — pressed by a Jedi mind trick — brought him before Jabba, against the Hutt's wishes.

4 ABY - The death of Jabba the Hutt

Jabba was killed aboard his sail barge at the Great Pit of Carkoon during the botched execution of Skywalker, Solo and their companions. Fortuna survived the collapse of Jabba's criminal empire.

~4 ABY to 9 ABY - Crime lord of Jabba's Palace

With his master dead, Fortuna finally seized the throne he had long coveted, ruling the palace and a diminished remnant of Jabba's Tatooine holdings.

9 ABY - Killed by Boba Fett

Boba Fett, having escaped the Sarlacc and recovered his armor, entered the palace with Fennec Shand, shot Fortuna dead on his dais, and claimed the throne for himself.
