title: Gial Ackbar
description: Gial Ackbar was a male Mon Calamari from the ocean world of Mon Cala who became one of the most celebrated military commanders in galactic history, serving across four conflicts over roughly six decades. As captain of the Mon Calamari Guard during the Clone Wars, he fought at the side of Prince Lee-Char against the Quarren separatists and the Karkarodon warlord Riff Tamson, helping preserve his people's throne with Republic and Gungan aid. Under the Galactic Empire he was enslaved along with much of his species, and after his rescue he rose through the Alliance to Restore the Republic to become commander of the rebel fleet, planning and leading the assault on the second Death Star at the Battle of Endor in 4 ABY — the engagement that made his warning of a trap into one of the most quoted moments of the war. He continued to serve the New Republic as an admiral and statesman for his homeworld before retiring, then answered General Leia Organa's call to join the Resistance against the First Order, advising the fleet through the destruction of the Hosnian system and the Battle of Starkiller Base. In 34 ABY he was killed when First Order fighters strafed the bridge of the Resistance flagship Raddus during the evacuation of D'Qar, ending a career defined by patient, meticulous tactical genius.
timeline:
~55 BBY - Birth on Mon Cala

Gial Ackbar is born on the ocean world of Mon Cala. Canon gives only an approximate window for his birth, roughly between 61 BBY and 52 BBY.

~22 BBY - Captain of the Mon Calamari Guard

Ackbar rises to captain of the Mon Calamari Guard and serves as a trusted military adviser to King Yos Kolina and his son, Prince Lee-Char.

21 BBY - The Mon Cala civil war

After the king's assassination, Quarren separatists backed by the Karkarodon Riff Tamson attack the Mon Calamari. Ackbar fights alongside Jedi reinforcements, Anakin Skywalker's forces and Gungan warriors sent by Padmé Amidala, protecting Lee-Char through the underwater battles.

21 BBY - Victory and Lee-Char's coronation

With Tamson defeated and the Quarren reconciled, Lee-Char is crowned king of a reunited Mon Cala, with Ackbar established as his leading military commander.

~19 BBY - The Empire subjugates Mon Cala

Following the rise of the Galactic Empire, Mon Cala is occupied and much of its population, Ackbar included, is pressed into servitude.

~4 BBY - Service with the Rebel Alliance

Freed from Imperial captivity, Ackbar joins the Alliance to Restore the Republic, where his tactical skill quickly earns him fleet command and the rank of admiral.

4 ABY - Briefing at Sullust

Ackbar presents the Alliance battle plan against the second Death Star, coordinating Han Solo's strike team on the Endor moon with the fleet's attack on the battle station.

4 ABY - Battle of Endor

Commanding from the Home One, Ackbar realizes the Alliance has flown into an Imperial ambush and orders the fleet to engage the Star Destroyers at point-blank range, a decision that keeps the rebels alive until the Death Star's shield falls and the station is destroyed.

5 ABY - Campaigns for the New Republic

Ackbar continues to lead fleets in the final campaigns against Imperial remnants as the New Republic is established, and serves his homeworld in its government.

~28 ABY - Joining the Resistance

Retired from New Republic service, Ackbar answers Leia Organa's summons and helps organize the Resistance against the rising First Order, becoming its senior naval adviser.

34 ABY - Battle of Starkiller Base

After the First Order destroys the Hosnian system, Ackbar helps plan the Resistance strike that brings down Starkiller Base.

34 ABY - Death aboard the Raddus

During the Resistance evacuation of D'Qar, First Order TIE fighters and Kylo Ren's TIE silencer strafe the bridge of the flagship Raddus. Ackbar is blown into space and killed, leaving Leia Organa to command the surviving fleet.

deathEvent.title: Episode VIII: The Last Jedi

title: Bo-Katan Kryze
description: Bo-Katan Kryze was a human female Mandalorian warrior of Clan Kryze and the younger sister of Duchess Satine Kryze, who rose from insurgent to Mand'alor and ultimately reunified her shattered people. Rejecting Satine's pacifist New Mandalorian government, she joined the terrorist splinter group Death Watch and led its elite Nite Owls under Pre Vizsla, helping Vizsla ally with the Sith Lord Maul's Shadow Collective to seize Mandalore in 20 BBY. When Maul killed Vizsla, claimed the Darksaber and murdered Satine, Bo-Katan broke away and fought Maul's regime as a resistance leader, aiding Obi-Wan Kenobi's escape and then joining Ahsoka Tano and the Republic's 501st Legion in the Siege of Mandalore in 19 BBY, after which she was installed as regent — only to be discarded when the Galactic Empire rose weeks later. She spent the Imperial Era leading a Mandalorian resistance, accepting the Darksaber from Sabine Wren in 1 BBY to become Mand'alor, but her claim ended in catastrophe when she surrendered the blade to Moff Gideon and the Empire carried out the Great Purge of Mandalore and the Night of a Thousand Tears. Broken and exiled, she was drawn back into the fight in 9 ABY by the Mandalorian Din Djarin: she guided him to Ahsoka Tano, helped capture Gideon, later rescued Din from the Mandalorian mines, glimpsed a living mythosaur in the Living Waters, and — after her castle on Kalevala was destroyed — bridged the divide between the exiled clans and the Children of the Watch. Leading the united Mandalorians in the Battle of Mandalore, she saw Gideon killed and the Darksaber destroyed, and reignited the Great Forge as the accepted leader of a people finally returning home.
timeline:
Date unknown - Birth into Clan Kryze

Bo-Katan was born a human female princess of Clan Kryze, of House Kryze, on Mandalore. Canon has never established her birth year or her age relative to any fixed event; she was the younger sister of Satine Kryze.

~21 BBY - Death Watch exiled from the Mandalore system

Rejecting her sister's pacifist New Mandalorian rule, Bo-Katan sided with Death Watch and its leader Pre Vizsla. After Vizsla was publicly exposed as the group's leader, Death Watch was driven from Mandalorian space and operated from exile.

~20 BBY - Nite Owls on Carlac

Commanding the Nite Owls, Bo-Katan helped run Death Watch's camp on the frozen world of Carlac, where the group terrorized a Ming Po village. She clashed with Ahsoka Tano, who exposed Death Watch's brutality to Lux Bonteri.

~20 BBY - Alliance with Maul and the Shadow Collective

Bo-Katan and Vizsla discovered the stranded Sith brothers Maul and Savage Opress. Together they forged the Shadow Collective, coercing the Black Sun, the Pyke Syndicate and the Hutts into a criminal army aimed at Mandalore.

~20 BBY - The takeover of Mandalore

With the Collective's crime waves staged to discredit Satine's government, Death Watch swept in as liberators. Satine was imprisoned and Vizsla installed himself as ruler, with Bo-Katan at his side.

~20 BBY - Maul seizes the Darksaber and Satine dies

Maul defeated and beheaded Vizsla in single combat, taking the Darksaber and rule of Mandalore. Bo-Katan refused to serve an outsider and led loyalist Nite Owls into open resistance; she freed Obi-Wan Kenobi, but Maul murdered Satine before their eyes, and Bo-Katan covered Kenobi's escape.

19 BBY - The Siege of Mandalore

Unable to dislodge Maul alone, Bo-Katan appealed to the Republic. Allied with Ahsoka Tano and Captain Rex's 501st Legion, she fought through Sundari's undercity; Maul was captured and Bo-Katan's forces retook the capital.

19 BBY - Regent of Mandalore, then discarded

Installed by the Republic as regent, her authority evaporated within weeks. Order 66 and the proclamation of the Galactic Empire ended the Clone Wars, and the new regime replaced her with compliant Imperial Mandalorian rulers.

1 BBY - Accepting the Darksaber

Fighting alongside Sabine Wren, Bo-Katan helped rescue Alrich Wren and destroy the Duchess, an Imperial weapon that vaporized Mandalorians inside their beskar armor. Sabine offered her the Darksaber, and Bo-Katan accepted it in her sister's memory, hailed as Mand'alor by the assembled clans.

~4 BBY to 4 ABY - Leading the Mandalorian resistance

As Mand'alor she rallied clans against Imperial occupation, but Mandalore's insurgency drew ever harsher Imperial reprisals.

~4 ABY to 5 ABY - The Great Purge and the Night of a Thousand Tears

Moff Gideon promised to spare Mandalorian lives and cities if Bo-Katan submitted and disarmed. She surrendered the Darksaber to him; he betrayed her, and the Empire bombarded Mandalore into a glassed ruin, scattering the survivors.

9 ABY - Meeting Din Djarin on Trask

Hunting Imperial weapons shipments with Koska Reeves and Axe Woves, Bo-Katan rescued Din Djarin, dismissed his Children of the Watch creed as a cult, and directed him to Ahsoka Tano in exchange for his help hijacking an Imperial freighter.

9 ABY - Boarding Gideon's cruiser

She joined Din's strike team to rescue Grogu and capture Moff Gideon. Din defeated Gideon and won the Darksaber, and Bo-Katan — bound by the tradition that the blade must be won in combat — refused to take it as a gift.

9 ABY - Exile on Kalevala

Her followers abandoned her for Axe Woves' mercenary fleet, and Bo-Katan withdrew alone to Kryze Castle on Kalevala.

9 ABY - The mines of Mandalore and the mythosaur

Answering a plea from Din, she descended into the ruined mines beneath Sundari, rescued him from a cybernetic Imperial creature, and saw a living mythosaur beneath the Living Waters — proving Mandalore was not cursed and remained habitable.

9 ABY - Kalevala destroyed

Gideon's forces bombarded Kryze Castle. Rescued by Din, Bo-Katan accepted refuge with his covert after being confirmed by the Armorer as one who had walked the Living Waters.

9 ABY - Reuniting the clans

Sent by the Armorer to gather the scattered Mandalorians, she reclaimed Axe Woves' fleet and persuaded exiles and the Children of the Watch to fight as one people.

9 ABY - The Battle of Mandalore

The united Mandalorians assaulted Gideon's hidden base. Gideon destroyed the Darksaber in combat with Bo-Katan and taunted that she had lost everything, but he was killed and the planet retaken.

9 ABY - Reigniting the Great Forge

With Mandalore reclaimed and reconstruction begun, Bo-Katan relit the Great Forge, leading her reunified people without need of the Darksaber. She remains alive; canon records no death.

endYearEvent: Star Wars Rebels
title: Maul
description: Maul, originally styled Darth Maul, was a Force-sensitive Dathomirian Zabrak male who rose from a Nightbrother of Dathomir to become the secret Sith apprentice of Darth Sidious. Trained from childhood in the dark side and an unmatched practitioner of Juyo lightsaber combat wielding a double-bladed saber, he was unleashed on Naboo, where he killed Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn before being bisected by Obi-Wan Kenobi and left for dead. He survived in madness for years until his brother Savage Opress recovered him and Mother Talzin restored his mind and gave him cybernetic legs. Consumed by vengeance against Kenobi and the Jedi, he built the Shadow Collective and seized control of Mandalore, only to be defeated and cast aside by Sidious. In the years after the Clone Wars he operated as a crime lord, leading the Crimson Dawn syndicate and later scheming as a shadowy warlord. His decades-long obsession ended on Tatooine, where he was slain in a final duel with Obi-Wan Kenobi.
timeline:
54 BBY - Birth on Dathomir

Maul is born a Nightbrother of the Zabrak clans on Dathomir, a son of the Nightsister leader Mother Talzin.

~54 BBY - Taken by Darth Sidious

As a young child, Maul is given to or taken by Darth Sidious, who raises and trains him in secret as his Sith apprentice, Darth Maul.

32 BBY - Duel on Naboo

Sent by Sidious during the Naboo crisis, Maul kills Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn but is bisected by Obi-Wan Kenobi and falls down a reactor shaft, presumed dead.

~32 BBY - Survival on Lotho Minor

Sustained by the dark side and hatred, a broken Maul survives on the junk world of Lotho Minor for years, his lower body replaced by spider-like appendages as his mind fractures.

~20 BBY - Rescue and restoration

Savage Opress locates his brother and brings him to Mother Talzin, who restores Maul's sanity and fits him with cybernetic legs, reigniting his quest for revenge against Obi-Wan Kenobi.

~20 BBY - The Shadow Collective and Mandalore

Maul unites Death Watch and criminal syndicates into the Shadow Collective, seizes control of Mandalore, and kills Duchess Satine Kryze to torment Kenobi.

~20 BBY - Betrayed by Sidious

Darth Sidious arrives on Mandalore, kills Savage Opress, and defeats and captures Maul, unwilling to tolerate a rival.

19 BBY - Siege of Mandalore

Having escaped and retaken Mandalore, Maul is confronted by Ahsoka Tano and Republic forces; captured, he escapes amid the chaos of Order 66 as the Republic falls.

~13 BBY - Shadow warlord

During the early Imperial era Maul continues to build his hidden power base and manipulate criminal and dark-side factions from the shadows.

~10 BBY - Crimson Dawn

Maul secretly leads the Crimson Dawn crime syndicate, directing its operations through lieutenants such as Qi'ra during the events surrounding Han Solo's early exploits.

2 BBY - Death on Tatooine

Tracking Obi-Wan Kenobi to Tatooine, Maul confronts his old enemy in a brief final duel, is struck down, and dies in Kenobi's arms.

Episode III: Revenge of the Sith

title: Owen Lars
description: Owen Lars was a human male moisture farmer who worked the Lars homestead in the Great Chott salt flat on the desert world of Tatooine. The son of Cliegg Lars, he became stepbrother to the young Jedi Anakin Skywalker when Cliegg freed and married Anakin's mother, Shmi Skywalker. Owen met Anakin in 22 BBY, when the Padawan came to Tatooine searching for Shmi after she had been abducted by Tusken Raiders; Anakin recovered her body only for her to die in his arms, and Cliegg — crippled in the failed rescue attempt — did not long outlive her. Owen and his partner Beru Whitesun married and kept the farm running, and in 19 BBY, after the fall of the Republic and Anakin's turn to Darth Vader, Obi-Wan Kenobi brought them Anakin's infant son Luke Skywalker to raise in obscurity. Fiercely protective and deeply wary of the Jedi legacy that had destroyed his family, Owen kept Luke bound to the farm and told him his father had been a spice-freighter navigator, even warning Kenobi away from the boy. In 0 BBY, Imperial stormtroopers tracking the astromech droid R2-D2 and the stolen Death Star plans came to the homestead and killed Owen and Beru — a loss that severed Luke's last tie to Tatooine and set him on the path to becoming a Jedi.
timeline:
~52 BBY - Birth

Owen was born to Cliegg Lars, a Tatooine moisture farmer. No canon source fixes the year; 52 BBY is a long-standing reference-work figure inherited from earlier material.

~32 BBY - Shmi Skywalker joins the Lars family

Cliegg Lars purchased Shmi Skywalker from the Mos Espa junk dealer Watto, freed her, and married her, making Owen the stepson of Shmi and stepbrother of Anakin Skywalker, whom he had not yet met.

22 BBY - Shmi's abduction and death

Tusken Raiders seized Shmi from the homestead. Cliegg lost a leg in the failed rescue, and Anakin Skywalker arrived with Padmé Amidala searching for his mother — the first time Owen met his stepbrother. Anakin found Shmi in the Tusken camp, where she died in his arms, and she was buried at the homestead.

~22 BBY - Death of Cliegg Lars

Broken in body and spirit after Shmi's death, Owen's father died, leaving Owen and Beru Whitesun to run the farm alone.

19 BBY - The Lars family takes in Luke Skywalker

Following the fall of the Republic and Anakin's fall to the dark side as Darth Vader, Obi-Wan Kenobi delivered the infant Luke Skywalker to Owen and Beru on Tatooine. Owen accepted the child at Beru's urging and raised him as a nephew, hiding his parentage.

9 BBY - Guarding Luke from the Empire

With Obi-Wan Kenobi living as a hermit nearby, Owen rebuffed the former Jedi's attempts to train or contact Luke. When Inquisitors and bounty hunters hunting Kenobi came to Tatooine, Owen and Beru armed themselves and defended their homestead and the boy.

~0 BBY - Keeping Luke on the farm

Owen repeatedly delayed Luke's applications to the Imperial Academy and to piloting work, insisting he was needed for another season of harvest.

0 BBY - Purchase of C-3PO and R2-D2

Owen bought the protocol droid C-3PO and the astromech R2-D2 from Jawa traders, unaware that R2-D2 carried the stolen Death Star plans and a message from Princess Leia Organa for Obi-Wan Kenobi.

0 BBY - Death at the Lars homestead

Imperial stormtroopers tracing the droids' sale reached the homestead and killed Owen and Beru, burning their bodies and the farm. Luke found their remains on his return, and with nothing left on Tatooine, he left with Kenobi.

deathEvent.title: Episode IV: A New Hope

title: Hondo Ohnaka
description: Hondo Ohnaka is a Weequay pirate, smuggler and self-styled businessman whose decades-long career made him one of the galaxy's most persistently opportunistic survivors. Operating out of the Outer Rim — most famously from a fortified base on Florrum at the head of the Ohnaka Gang — he first drew galactic attention during the Clone Wars by capturing and ransoming Count Dooku, and thereafter repeatedly crossed paths with Obi-Wan Kenobi, Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano, alternately robbing them, arming their enemies and, when the price or the mood was right, helping them. Weapons dealing to the Onderon rebels, the abduction of a group of Jedi younglings, and the destruction of his operation by Maul's Shadow Collective all followed in quick succession. Reduced to scavenging under the Empire, he resurfaced years later as a fence and freelancer who befriended the Padawan Ezra Bridger, sold the Ghost crew both useful intelligence and useless junk, and drifted into loose cooperation with the rebellion. Decades on he was still trading — running Ohnaka Transport Solutions out of Black Spire Outpost on Batuu and quietly borrowing the Millennium Falcon for jobs — a scoundrel whose loyalty was always for sale but whose affection for a handful of Jedi was, by his own insistence, entirely genuine.
timeline:
Unknown date - Birth

Canon has never given Hondo Ohnaka a birth year. He is a Weequay, a species associated with the world Sriluur, and by the Clone Wars he is an established pirate captain with a long criminal history behind him.

~32 BBY - Dealings with Darth Maul

Around the time of the Naboo crisis, a younger Ohnaka is already operating as a pirate and trader in the criminal underworld, crossing paths with Darth Maul during the Sith apprentice's hunt for a captured Jedi Padawan.

~21 BBY - Capturing Count Dooku

Ohnaka's gang seizes Count Dooku and attempts to ransom the Separatist leader to the Republic, ending up with Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker as unwilling guests — and then prisoners — at his Florrum stronghold.

~21 BBY - Extortion on Felucia

His pirates shake down Felucian farmers for their harvest, only to be driven off after the villagers hire a band of bounty hunters trained up with Jedi help.

~20 BBY - Arming the Onderon rebels

Ohnaka sells weapons to the Onderonian insurgency fighting the Separatist puppet government, profiting from a rebellion the Jedi were quietly backing.

~20 BBY - The kidnapped younglings

His crew boards the Jedi training cruiser Crucible and takes Ahsoka Tano and a group of younglings captive on Florrum; the younglings escape, steal his ship and force him into a grudging alliance against General Grievous.

~20 BBY - Florrum destroyed

Maul's Shadow Collective, backed by Death Watch, assaults Florrum and shatters the Ohnaka Gang, leaving Hondo with a handful of survivors and no base of operations.

~3 BBY - Meeting Ezra Bridger

Now a lone operator flying the Broken Horn, Ohnaka encounters the young Padawan Ezra Bridger, spins him a story of partnership, and begins a long, mutually exasperating relationship with the crew of the Ghost.

~2 BBY - The last battle on Agamar

Ohnaka drags Ezra, Zeb and Chopper into a scheme to loot a Separatist supply ship, waking a droid army still fighting the Clone Wars decades after it ended.

~2 BBY - Selling junk to the rebellion

He continues fencing salvage, stolen goods and dubious intelligence to the growing rebel network, including a shipment of purportedly valuable cargo that proves worthless.

~1 BBY - Working with Saw Gerrera

A deal with the rebels goes sideways and Ohnaka finds himself alongside Saw Gerrera's partisans investigating an Imperial construction project, before slipping away to save his own skin.

~34 ABY - Ohnaka Transport Solutions on Batuu

Decades later and still very much alive, Ohnaka runs a shipping-and-smuggling outfit from Black Spire Outpost on Batuu, hiring freelance crews and making use of the Millennium Falcon while the First Order and Resistance struggle around him.

endYearEvent: Ahsoka
title: Marrok
description: Marrok was a mysterious, heavily armored Force-sensitive humanoid warrior who served the Galactic Empire's Inquisitorius — reportedly under the designation First Brother — after training as a member of the Jedi Order before the fall of the Republic. Inducted into the Inquisitorius before Darth Vader alongside Barriss Offee, the Fourth Sister and the Eleventh Brother in the early Imperial Era, he hunted surviving Jedi and, with the Eleventh Brother, pursued the crime lord and former Sith Lord Maul during Imperial operations on the Mid Rim world of Janix. He is best known for his silent, brooding presence in the New Republic era, when he served the Nightsister Morgan Elsbeth as a mercenary alongside the fallen Jedi Baylan Skoll and his apprentice Shin Hati, wielding a scarred, sputtering red blade in Elsbeth's search for the exiled Grand Admiral Thrawn. Marrok was cut down by Ahsoka Tano on Seatos in 9 ABY, and rather than dying as an ordinary man, his armor burst with thick green smoke as his body shriveled away — strongly implying he had been sustained or reanimated by Nightsister magick, leaving his true identity and the true moment of his death among the most persistent unanswered mysteries in current canon.
timeline:
Unknown - Birth

Marrok's birth year, homeworld and species are never established in canon; he is identified only as a male humanoid. He was born some decades before the Clone Wars, since he was old enough to have trained with the Jedi Order before its fall.

Before 19 BBY - Service in the Jedi Order

Marrok was a member of the Jedi Order prior to the rise of the Empire, making him one of the Jedi who survived Order 66 and later turned against his own kind.

~18 BBY - Inducted into the Inquisitorius

In the early Imperial Era, Marrok knelt before Darth Vader as he was inducted into the Inquisitorius, alongside Barriss Offee, the Fourth Sister and the Eleventh Brother.

~10 BBY - Hunting Maul on Janix

Serving the Empire as an Inquisitor, Marrok worked with the Eleventh Brother during operations on the Mid Rim world of Janix, pursuing the crime lord and former Sith Lord Maul. He reported to a cloaked superior he addressed only as "my lord."

After 4 ABY - Mercenary for Morgan Elsbeth

Following the Empire's collapse, Marrok was hired by the Nightsister Morgan Elsbeth, serving in her forces alongside the Force-sensitive mercenaries Baylan Skoll and Shin Hati.

9 ABY - Ambush on Arcana

Marrok shadowed Ahsoka Tano and Huyang on Arcana, where Elsbeth's people recovered a star map pointing the way to the distant galaxy holding Grand Admiral Thrawn.

9 ABY - Duel on Seatos and death

On the forest world of Seatos, Marrok fought Ahsoka Tano and was struck down by her blade. His armor split open and released a burst of thick green smoke as his body withered and collapsed, suggesting he had been animated or sustained by Nightsister magick. His identity was never revealed.

deathEvent.title: Ahsoka

title: Asajj Ventress
description: Asajj Ventress was a Dathomirian warrior whose life spanned nearly every role the galaxy could offer: Nightsister, Jedi Padawan, Sith-trained assassin, Separatist commander, bounty hunter, and ultimately a redeemed heroine. Born on Dathomir into a Nightsister clan, she was given away as a child and endured slavery before being taken as an apprentice by a stranded Jedi. She rose to prominence during the Clone Wars as Count Dooku's deadly dark-side assassin, dueling Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and Ahsoka Tano. After Dooku betrayed her, she returned to her Nightsister kin, survived General Grievous's massacre of them, and reinvented herself as a bounty hunter. Her arc culminated when she aided the fallen Jedi Quinlan Vos and sacrificed herself to save him from Dooku's Force lightning — though Nightsister magic later restored her to life, allowing her to resurface in the years after the Clone Wars.
timeline:
50 BBY - Birth on Dathomir

Asajj Ventress is born into a clan of Nightsister witches on the planet Dathomir.

~49 BBY - Given away as a child

As a young child, Ventress is handed over to the criminal Hal'Sted and taken offworld, where she ends up enslaved. (Elements of this early backstory, including her time on Rattatak, originate in Legends material.)

~40 BBY - Trained by a stranded Jedi

After her master's death, she is discovered and trained in the ways of the Force by the marooned Jedi Ky Narec, whom she later loses to violence — a loss that drives her toward the dark side.

~22 BBY - Assassin for Count Dooku

By the outbreak of the Clone Wars, Ventress serves Count Dooku as a dark-side assassin and Separatist commander, clashing repeatedly with Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and Ahsoka Tano.

~21 BBY - Betrayed by Dooku

Fearing her growing power, Dooku orders her killed. Surviving the betrayal, Ventress returns to Dathomir and rejoins Mother Talzin and the Nightsisters, plotting revenge.

~21 BBY - Massacre of the Nightsisters

General Grievous leads a droid army that slaughters the Nightsister clan. Ventress survives but is left without her people, becoming a wandering bounty hunter.

~20 BBY - Aiding Ahsoka Tano

Now a bounty hunter, Ventress crosses paths with Ahsoka Tano and helps clear the framed Padawan's name during Ahsoka's flight from the Jedi Order.

~19 BBY - Dark Disciple and sacrifice

Ventress joins the Jedi Quinlan Vos in a mission to assassinate Dooku, falling in love with him. She sacrifices herself, shielding Vos from Dooku's Force lightning, and appears to die.

~18 BBY - Restored to life

Through Nightsister magic she is resurrected, and in the years following the Clone Wars she resurfaces alive, appearing during the era of The Bad Batch.

events.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

title: Babu Frik
description: Babu Frik was an Anzellan male droidsmith who lived during the New Republic Era and the war between the First Order and the Resistance. Tiny in stature and highly accented in his speech, he maintained a workshop in the mountain city of Kijimi, where he worked as a black-market droidsmith among the Spice Runners of Kijimi. Renowned as one of the finest droidsmiths in the galaxy, he could reprogram or modify virtually any droid regardless of the security measures protecting its systems. He is best known for his role in the search for Emperor Palpatine, when the Resistance brought the protocol droid C-3PO to him so he could bypass the droid's programming restrictions and translate a forbidden inscription in the Sith language, a procedure that wiped C-3PO's memory. A fan-favorite introduced in Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker, Babu Frik became emblematic of his species, the Anzellans.
timeline:
~50 BBY - Birth

Babu Frik, an Anzellan, is born. His exact birth year is not stated in canon; it is derived from a reference-book statement that he was 85 years old during the events of The Rise of Skywalker (35 ABY).

By 35 ABY - Droidsmith of Kijimi

Babu Frik establishes himself as a black-market droidsmith with a workshop in the city of Kijimi, working among the Spice Runners of Kijimi and earning a reputation for being able to modify or reprogram nearly any droid.

35 ABY - Reprogramming C-3PO

During the Resistance's search for the Sith wayfinder, Poe Dameron leads Rey and the others to Babu Frik on Kijimi. Because C-3PO is forbidden by his programming to translate the Sith text held in his memory bank, Babu Frik bypasses those restrictions to read the inscription aloud, an act that erases the protocol droid's memory.

35 ABY - Survival on Kijimi

After aiding the Resistance, Babu Frik survives the events surrounding the final campaign against the Sith Eternal. Canon records no death for him.

endYearEvent: Episode IV: A New Hope

Episode III: Revenge of the Sith

title: Beru Lars
description: Beru Whitesun Lars was a human female moisture farmer from the desert world of Tatooine who, with her husband Owen Lars, raised Luke Skywalker as their own. Born into a family that had farmed moisture on Tatooine for generations, she was living and working at the Lars homestead by the time of the Clone Wars, where she met Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala when Anakin returned home searching for his mother, Shmi Skywalker Lars. After the fall of the Republic in 19 BBY, Obi-Wan Kenobi delivered the infant Luke — Anakin and Padmé's son — into Beru and Owen's care, and the couple spent the next two decades raising the boy in obscurity under the Galactic Empire, with Beru often serving as the softer counterweight to Owen's protective sternness and quietly recognizing that Luke's restlessness would eventually carry him off-world. Their family life was strained by the secret of Luke's parentage and by the lurking danger of Imperial and Inquisitorial attention on Tatooine. In 0 BBY, stormtroopers hunting the droids R2-D2 and C-3PO tracked them to the Lars homestead and killed Beru and Owen, burning the farm; finding their remains severed Luke's last tie to Tatooine and set him on the path to joining the Rebel Alliance.
timeline:
Unknown date - Birth on Tatooine

Beru Whitesun was born into a Tatooine moisture-farming family. Canon has never established her birth year; the frequently quoted 47 BBY figure is Legends-only.

~22 BBY - Life at the Lars homestead

By the eve of the Clone Wars, Beru was part of the Lars household alongside Owen Lars, his father Cliegg, and Cliegg's wife Shmi Skywalker Lars.

22 BBY - Meeting Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala

When Anakin came home in search of his mother, Beru welcomed him and Padmé, offering them food and shelter. Shmi died of her injuries after Anakin recovered her from a Tusken camp, and Beru attended her funeral.

~22-19 BBY - Marriage to Owen Lars

Beru married Owen and took the Lars name, continuing to work the moisture farm through the years of the Clone Wars.

19 BBY - Taking in Luke Skywalker

Following the fall of the Republic and Anakin's turn to the dark side, Obi-Wan Kenobi brought the newborn Luke to the homestead. Beru cradled the child as she and Owen looked out over the twin suns, beginning their life as his guardians.

~10 BBY - Raising Luke under the Empire

Beru helped bring up a young Luke on the farm, tempering Owen's insistence that the boy be kept ignorant of his heritage and hidden from Obi-Wan, who watched from a distance.

~10 BBY - Danger comes to the homestead

When the Inquisitor Reva hunted Luke to draw out Obi-Wan, Beru armed herself and fought to defend her nephew, holding the farm until Obi-Wan and Owen drove the threat off.

~0 BBY - A restless nephew

As Luke chafed at farm life and longed to leave for the Academy, Beru urged Owen to accept that the boy could not be kept on Tatooine forever.

0 BBY - Death at the Lars homestead

Imperial stormtroopers traced R2-D2 and C-3PO to the farm and killed Beru and Owen, torching the homestead. Luke returned to find their bodies and left Tatooine with Obi-Wan, ultimately joining the Rebel Alliance.

deathEvent.title: Episode IV: A New Hope

endYearEvent: Ahsoka
title: Morgan Elsbeth
description: Morgan Elsbeth was a Force-sensitive human female Nightsister of Dathomir who rose from the ashes of her clan's destruction to become one of the Galactic Empire's most important shipwrights and, later, the driving force behind Grand Admiral Thrawn's return from exile. Born on Dathomir before the Clone Wars, she survived the massacre of the Nightsisters — in which her mother was killed — and spent years as a wanderer and survivor, allying briefly with the Mountain Clan before her ruthlessness and talent for industry brought her into the Empire's service, where she helped construct the Imperial Navy and became a devoted follower of Thrawn. By the New Republic era she ruled the walled city of Calodan on Corvus as its magistrate, terrorizing its people with a regime that wore the chimaera emblem of Thrawn's 7th Fleet; there she offered a spear of pure beskar to the Mandalorian Din Djarin as payment to kill the former Jedi Ahsoka Tano, only to be defeated by Tano herself. Freed from New Republic custody by the mercenaries Baylan Skoll and Shin Hati, Elsbeth pursued a map to a distant galaxy, built the hyperspace ring Eye of Sion, and reached the world of Peridea, where the Great Mothers formally accepted her into the Nightsister order and granted her the gift of shadows and the Blade of Talzin. Fighting to buy Thrawn time to escape back to the known galaxy, she was struck down by Ahsoka Tano, dying with the hope of restoring Dathomir's culture unfulfilled.
timeline:
Before the Clone Wars - Birth on Dathomir

Morgan Elsbeth was born on Dathomir into the Nightsister clan sometime before the Clone Wars. Canon gives no year for her birth.

~19 BBY - Survivor of the Nightsister Massacre

Elsbeth witnessed the destruction of her people during the Separatist assault on Dathomir, in which her mother was killed. She survived alone amid the ruins of her clan.

~19 BBY - Wandering and the Mountain Clan

Left without a home, Elsbeth wandered Dathomir and eventually fell in with the Mountain Clan, who took her in before she turned on those who wronged her.

Reign of the Empire - Imperial Shipwright

Elsbeth's skill as a starship designer and industrialist made her instrumental in the construction of the Imperial Navy, and she became a dedicated follower of Grand Admiral Thrawn, whose chimaera emblem her forces later wore.

9 ABY - Magistrate of Calodan

By the New Republic era, Elsbeth ruled the walled city of Calodan on the planet Corvus, stripping the world bare and terrorizing its citizens with her guards and the mercenary Lang.

9 ABY - The Beskar Spear

She hired the Mandalorian Din Djarin, offering a spear of pure beskar as payment to kill the former Jedi Ahsoka Tano, who had come to Corvus hunting information about Thrawn.

9 ABY - Defeat on Corvus

Ahsoka Tano liberated Calodan and defeated Elsbeth in single combat, demanding to know the location of Grand Admiral Thrawn before taking her into custody.

9 ABY - Rescued by Baylan Skoll and Shin Hati

While being transported by the New Republic, Elsbeth was freed by the mercenaries Baylan Skoll and Shin Hati, whom she had employed, and resumed her search for Thrawn.

9 ABY - The Star Map and the Eye of Sion

Elsbeth recovered an ancient star map pointing to a distant galaxy and oversaw construction of the enormous hyperspace ring known as the Eye of Sion above Seatos.

9 ABY - Journey to Peridea

After a confrontation with Ahsoka Tano at Seatos, Elsbeth launched the Eye of Sion and crossed to Peridea, the ancestral world of the Dathomiri witches, where she found Thrawn in exile.

9 ABY - Accepted as a Nightsister

The Great Mothers formally inducted Elsbeth into the Nightsister order, granting her the gift of shadows and the Blade of Talzin.

9 ABY - Death on Peridea

During the Battle of Peridea, Elsbeth stayed behind at the Great Mothers' fortress to hold off Ahsoka Tano and allow Thrawn to escape. Declaring her sacrifice was for Dathomir rather than the Empire, she was killed by Tano.

title: Padmé Amidala Naberrie
description: Padmé Amidala Naberrie was a human stateswoman from the planet Naboo who served her people during the final decades of the Galactic Republic. Elected Queen of Naboo as a teenager, she rose to prominence during the Trade Federation's invasion of her homeworld, forging an alliance with the Gungans to liberate Naboo. After her royal terms ended, she became Naboo's representative in the Galactic Senate, where she championed democracy, diplomacy, and a peaceful end to the Clone Wars, opposing the militarization of the Republic. Her secret marriage to Jedi Knight Anakin Skywalker would prove fateful for the galaxy: pregnant with his children, she died in 19 BBY shortly after giving birth to the twins Luke and Leia, whose destinies would shape the fate of the galaxy for generations. She is remembered as an idealist whose commitment to freedom outlived her.
timeline:
46 BBY - Birth on Naboo

Padmé Naberrie is born on Naboo to Ruwee and Jobal Naberrie, growing up alongside her older sister Sola.

~40 BBY - Early public service

As a child and young teenager, Padmé becomes involved in politics and humanitarian work, serving in the Apprentice Legislators and as a junior member of Naboo's government.

~33 BBY - Elected Queen of Naboo

At age fourteen, Padmé is elected Queen of Naboo, taking the regnal name Amidala and beginning her reign.

32 BBY - Invasion of Naboo

The Trade Federation blockades and invades Naboo. Aided by the Jedi Qui-Gon Jinn and Obi-Wan Kenobi and meeting young Anakin Skywalker, Amidala escapes to Coruscant, calls for a vote of no confidence in Chancellor Valorum, then returns home to lead a Gungan-Naboo alliance that defeats the Federation.

~28 BBY - Senator of Naboo

After her terms as Queen conclude, Amidala is appointed to represent Naboo in the Galactic Senate, becoming a leading voice for democracy and peace.

22 BBY - Separatist Crisis and marriage

Targeted by assassination attempts over the Military Creation Act, Padmé is placed under Jedi protection and reunites with Anakin Skywalker. Following the Battle of Geonosis that opens the Clone Wars, she and Anakin secretly marry on Naboo.

22–19 BBY - The Clone Wars

Throughout the war, Senator Amidala undertakes numerous diplomatic missions, pursues peace initiatives, exposes corruption, and works to protect the Republic's democratic institutions while concealing her marriage to Anakin.

19 BBY - Pregnancy and Anakin's fall

Padmé becomes pregnant with Anakin's children. As the Clone Wars end and Order 66 is executed, Anakin turns to the dark side as Darth Vader. Padmé travels to Mustafar to reach him, only to be Force-choked by Anakin in his rage.

19 BBY - Death on Polis Massa

Taken to the medical facility on Polis Massa, Padmé gives birth to twins Luke and Leia before dying, having lost the will to live. Her death is concealed and she is later buried on Naboo, appearing pregnant to hide the twins' survival.

title: Mon Mothma
description: Mon Mothma is a human politician from Chandrila who rose from the Galactic Senate to become the founding leader of the Alliance to Restore the Republic and the first Chancellor of the New Republic. She came to prominence during the Separatist Crisis and Clone Wars as an outspoken advocate for peace and a critic of Supreme Chancellor Palpatine's accumulation of executive power, allied with senators such as Bail Organa and Padmé Amidala. Under the Empire she publicly served as the Chandrilan senator while secretly financing and organizing the growing rebellion — a double life dramatized in the series Andor — before openly breaking with the Empire to lead the Rebel Alliance. Her leadership guided the destruction of both Death Stars and the fall of the Empire at Endor, after which she established the New Republic and served as its Chancellor. She remains one of the most enduring figures of the galaxy's transition from Republic to Empire to Republic, and no canon work depicts her death.
timeline:
~48 BBY - Birth on Chandrila

Mon Mothma is born into a prominent political family on the planet Chandrila. Wookieepedia places her birth around 48 BBY, derived from canon references to her age when elected to the Senate.

~32 BBY - Elected to the Galactic Senate

At roughly sixteen years of age, Mothma becomes one of the youngest senators to represent Chandrila in the Galactic Senate, beginning a decades-long political career.

~24 BBY - Marriage to Perrin Fertha

Around this time Mothma enters an arranged Chandrilan marriage to Perrin Fertha, a union that later becomes politically and personally strained as her covert activities grow.

22 BBY - The Separatist Crisis and Clone Wars

As the Republic fractures and war breaks out with the Confederacy of Independent Systems, Mothma emerges as a leading voice for peace and diplomacy alongside Senators Bail Organa and Padmé Amidala.

~19 BBY - Opposing Palpatine's power

Mothma joins senators pushing back against the Supreme Chancellor's expanding emergency powers, laying the groundwork for organized political resistance as the Clone Wars end and the Empire is declared.

~5 BBY - Secret financier of the rebellion

Publicly serving as Chandrila's senator in the Imperial Senate, Mothma secretly funnels funds and coordinates disparate rebel cells, navigating Imperial surveillance while maintaining a respectable political facade.

~1 BBY - Break with the Empire

Her covert activities exposed and her position untenable, Mothma delivers a defiant speech against Imperial atrocities and flees the Senate to openly commit to armed rebellion.

0 BBY - Leading the Rebel Alliance

As a founder and leader of the Alliance to Restore the Republic, Mothma helps direct the effort to obtain the Death Star plans and endorses the mission that leads to the Battle of Scarif.

4 ABY - Fall of the Empire

Mothma leads the Alliance through the campaign culminating in the Battle of Endor and the destruction of the second Death Star and the death of the Emperor.

~4 ABY - Founding the New Republic

With the Empire in retreat, Mothma proclaims the restoration of the Republic and becomes the first Chancellor of the New Republic, moving the seat of government to Chandrila.

~9 ABY - Chancellor of the New Republic

Mothma continues to serve as Chancellor, guiding the fledgling New Republic, and appears in this role during the events of the Ahsoka era.

endYearEvent: Episode III: Revenge of the Sith

title: Kit Fisto
description: Kit Fisto was a Force-sensitive Nautolan male from the ocean world of Glee Anselm who rose to the rank of Jedi Master and served on the Jedi High Council during the final years of the Galactic Republic. Amphibious, famously good-humored, and regarded as one of the Order's finest lightsaber duelists — equally deadly on land and underwater — he fought in the Battle of Geonosis in 22 BBY that opened the Clone Wars and went on to serve as a Jedi General in the Grand Army of the Republic. Over the course of the war he hunted General Grievous to the Vassek system, where he lost his former Padawan Nahdar Vebb to the cyborg general and only narrowly escaped himself; he later led Republic forces in the underwater campaign on Mon Cala to protect Prince Lee-Char and restore the planet's throne. In 19 BBY, after Anakin Skywalker exposed Supreme Chancellor Sheev Palpatine as the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, Fisto joined Mace Windu, Agen Kolar and Saesee Tiin in the attempt to arrest him; Sidious cut down all three of Windu's companions within seconds, and Fisto died in the Chancellor's office on Coruscant, one of the last Jedi to fall before Order 66 destroyed the Order.
timeline:
Unknown date - Birth on Glee Anselm

Kit Fisto was born a Nautolan on the ocean world of Glee Anselm. No canon source gives a birth year; he was clearly born decades before the Clone Wars, as he was a seasoned Jedi Master by 22 BBY.

Before 22 BBY - Jedi Master and mentor

Fisto trained in the Jedi Order and attained the rank of Jedi Master, earning a reputation as an exceptional swordsman. He took the Mon Calamari Nahdar Vebb as his Padawan learner.

22 BBY - Battle of Geonosis

Fisto was among the roughly two hundred Jedi who followed Mace Windu to Geonosis to rescue Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi and Padmé Amidala. He survived the arena battle that began the Clone Wars and became a Jedi General of the Grand Army of the Republic.

22 BBY - Hunt for Nute Gunray and the lair of Grievous

Pursuing the escaped Viceroy Nute Gunray, Fisto tracked a Separatist shuttle to a fortress on the third moon of Vassek and reunited with Nahdar Vebb, now a Jedi Knight. The pair instead found General Grievous's private lair. Fisto fought Grievous nearly to a standstill, but Vebb was killed and Fisto was forced to flee with clone commander Fil's squad decimated behind him.

22-19 BBY - Service on the Jedi High Council

Fisto sat on the Jedi High Council during the war, weighing in on the Order's strategy, on the growing entanglement of the Jedi with the Republic military, and on matters such as Ahsoka Tano's trial and Anakin Skywalker's standing.

~21 BBY - The war for Mon Cala

When the Separatists and the Quarren Isolation League overthrew the Mon Calamari monarchy, Fisto led Republic clone forces in the underwater campaign, using his amphibious nature to fight alongside Anakin Skywalker, Ahsoka Tano and Padmé Amidala to protect the young Prince Lee-Char until Gungan reinforcements turned the tide.

19 BBY - The attempt to arrest Palpatine

After Anakin Skywalker revealed that Supreme Chancellor Palpatine was the Sith Lord Darth Sidious, Fisto joined Mace Windu, Agen Kolar and Saesee Tiin in confronting the Chancellor in his office on Coruscant to place him under arrest.

19 BBY - Death on Coruscant

Sidious drew his lightsaber and killed Kolar and Tiin almost instantly. Fisto held out only seconds longer before he too was struck down, leaving Windu alone against the Sith Lord moments before Order 66 was issued.

title: Breha Organa
description: Breha Organa was a human female of Alderaan who reigned as queen of that world during the final decades of the Galactic Republic and the early years of the Empire, also serving as her planet's Minister of Education and as head of the House of Organa. Married to Senator Bail Prestor Organa, she was unable to bear a healthy child of her own — a difficult pregnancy left her dependent on a cybernetic heart — and at the end of the Clone Wars in 19 BBY she and Bail adopted the newborn daughter of Padmé Amidala and Anakin Skywalker, raising her as Princess Leia Organa. Under Palpatine's New Order, Breha quietly turned Alderaan's resources, relief missions and refugee networks toward the growing rebellion, working alongside Bail as one of the movement's founding architects while shielding Leia's true parentage and, for as long as she could, keeping her daughter out of the fight; she mentored Leia in statecraft and taught her the traditions of Alderaanian leadership. Breha remained on Alderaan to the end, dying in 0 BBY when the Empire destroyed the planet with the Death Star's superlaser.
timeline:
~54 BBY - Birth on Alderaan

Breha is born into Alderaanian nobility. Canon never gives a year; sources place her birth somewhere between 59 and 49 BBY.

~33 BBY - Ascension to the Alderaanian throne

Breha becomes Queen of Alderaan, heading the royal family and later serving as the planet's Minister of Education.

Before 22 BBY - Marriage to Bail Organa

She weds Bail Prestor Organa, who represents Alderaan in the Galactic Senate while she rules from Aldera.

Before 19 BBY - A failed pregnancy and a cybernetic heart

Complications during pregnancy nearly kill Breha and leave her with a cybernetic heart; the couple are unable to have a biological child.

19 BBY - The adoption of Leia

At the end of the Clone Wars, Bail returns from Polis Massa with a newborn girl. Breha welcomes the infant, and the child is raised as Princess Leia Organa of Alderaan.

19 BBY onward - Quiet resistance under the Empire

As the New Order tightens, Breha lends Alderaan's ships, credits and relief operations to the nascent rebellion her husband is helping to assemble, while keeping Leia's origins secret.

9 BBY - Leia's abduction

When Leia is kidnapped by agents seeking to blackmail Bail, Breha endures an agonizing wait on Alderaan until Obi-Wan Kenobi recovers her daughter and returns her home.

~3 BBY - Leia's Day of Demand

Breha presides over the ceremony in which the sixteen-year-old Leia claims her right to the throne, then works to keep her daughter clear of the rebel network she and Bail are building.

2 BBY - The Alliance declared

With the rebellion openly formed, Alderaan under Breha becomes one of its crucial covert backers.

0 BBY - Death in the destruction of Alderaan

Grand Moff Tarkin orders the Death Star to fire on Alderaan. Breha dies with her world and its billions of inhabitants.

title: R2-D2
description:
"

R2-D2, pronounced \"Artoo-Detoo\" and commonly called Artoo, is an R2-series astromech droid built by Industrial Automaton and given masculine programming. Small, stubborn and improbably brave, he served a long chain of masters — the Royal Engineers of Naboo, Queen and Senator Padmé Amidala, Anakin Skywalker, Bail Organa, Princess Leia Organa, Luke Skywalker and finally the Resistance — and, uniquely among droids, was never given a full memory wipe, allowing decades of accumulated experience to shape a wilful, resourceful and often sardonic personality. He repaired the shield generator of Queen Amidala's royal starship during the Trade Federation blockade of Naboo in 32 BBY, flew as Anakin Skywalker's astromech throughout the Clone Wars, witnessed the fall of the Republic and the birth of the Empire, and carried both the stolen Death Star plans and Leia's plea to Obi-Wan Kenobi to Tatooine in 0 BBY, setting the Galactic Civil War's decisive events in motion. He went on to help destroy the first Death Star, accompany Luke to Dagobah, aid the rescue of Han Solo and the victory at Endor, serve at Luke's Jedi temple, and decades later supply the missing piece of the map that led the Resistance to the exiled Luke Skywalker. His fate after the Battle of Exegol in 35 ABY is not recorded in canon; he is last seen alive and operational.

"
timeline:
"

Prior to 32 BBY - Manufacture

R2-D2 is built by Industrial Automaton as an R2-series astromech droid with masculine programming. Canon gives no construction date, only that he existed before 32 BBY.

32 BBY - Service with the Royal Engineers of Naboo

R2-D2 is in the possession of Naboo's Royal Engineers and assigned as a maintenance droid aboard Queen Padmé Amidala's Royal Starship.

32 BBY - Repairing the Royal Starship

During the escape from the Trade Federation blockade of Naboo, R2-D2 is the only astromech to survive repairing the ship's shield generator under fire, allowing the vessel to reach Tatooine. Amidala honors him personally.

32 BBY - Meeting Anakin Skywalker

On Tatooine, R2-D2 meets the slave boy Anakin Skywalker and his protocol droid C-3PO, beginning the galaxy's longest droid partnership.

32 BBY - Battle of Naboo

R2-D2 accompanies Anakin in the starfighter that destroys the Droid Control Ship above Naboo, helping end the occupation.

22 BBY - Separatist Crisis

Now in Padmé Amidala's service as a senator, R2-D2 travels with her to Naboo and Tatooine and follows Anakin and Obi-Wan Kenobi to Geonosis, where he and C-3PO help rescue them from the execution arena.

22 BBY - The Clone Wars begin

R2-D2 becomes Anakin Skywalker's personal astromech, flying with him aboard his Jedi starfighter for the duration of the war.

~22 BBY - Lost and recovered

R2-D2 is lost in battle and sold to the scavenger Gha Nachkt, who delivers him to General Grievous; Anakin, unwilling to lose a droid carrying unwiped strategic data, mounts a rescue and recovers him from Grievous' listening post.

21 BBY - The Senate hostage crisis

During Cad Bane's raid on the Senate Building, R2-D2 aids the response, but Bane erases the droid's record of the building's ventilation shafts — the only memory wipe, partial at that, R2-D2 ever receives.

19 BBY - Rescue over Coruscant

R2-D2 accompanies Anakin and Obi-Wan aboard the Invisible Hand to rescue Chancellor Palpatine, holding off battle droids and guiding the crippled cruiser's crash landing.

19 BBY - Fall of the Republic

R2-D2 travels to Mustafar with Padmé and witnesses Anakin's fall to the dark side, then escapes aboard the Tantive IV. C-3PO's memory is wiped to protect the secret of the Skywalker twins; R2-D2's is not.

19 BBY to 0 BBY - Service to House Organa

R2-D2 enters the service of Senator Bail Organa of Alderaan, retaining full knowledge of the Jedi, the Clone Wars and the Skywalkers throughout the Imperial era.

~5 BBY - Missions for the rebellion

Working covertly for Bail Organa, R2-D2 and C-3PO cross paths with the Spectres rebel cell, including a weapons-acquisition mission that leads to the crew's first encounter with the growing Rebel Alliance.

1 BBY - Yavin 4

R2-D2 is present at the Rebel Alliance's Massassi base during the mobilization that follows the theft of the Death Star plans from Scarif.

0 BBY - Carrying the Death Star plans

Aboard the Tantive IV over Tatooine, Princess Leia Organa loads the Death Star schematics and a plea for help into R2-D2, who escapes by pod with C-3PO and seeks out Obi-Wan Kenobi.

0 BBY - Battle of Yavin

Bought by the Lars family, R2-D2 brings Luke Skywalker into the war, helps rescue Leia from the Death Star, and rides in Luke's X-wing during the trench run that destroys the battle station.

3 ABY - Hoth, Dagobah and Bespin

R2-D2 serves with Rogue Squadron on Hoth, then flies with Luke to Dagobah for his training under Yoda and on to Cloud City, where he restores the damaged Millennium Falcon's hyperdrive to allow their escape.

4 ABY - Jabba's palace and Endor

R2-D2 plays his part in the rescue of Han Solo on Tatooine, concealing Luke's lightsaber aboard the sail barge, then joins the strike team on Endor and is injured shorting the shield bunker's doors during the battle that destroys the second Death Star.

After 4 ABY - The New Republic era

R2-D2 remains with Luke Skywalker through his search for Jedi lore and the founding of his Jedi temple.

9 ABY - Reunion with the Skywalker legacy

R2-D2 accompanies Luke when he answers the call aboard Moff Gideon's cruiser to take Grogu as an apprentice, and later escorts the young Jedi at Luke's temple as he chooses between the Jedi path and Din Djarin.

~28 ABY - Destruction of the temple

After Ben Solo's fall and the razing of Luke's academy, a grieving R2-D2 is left behind and eventually enters a self-imposed low-power mode at the Resistance base on D'Qar.

34 ABY - The map to Luke Skywalker

Roused by BB-8's arrival, R2-D2 awakens and produces the missing portion of the star map, revealing Ahch-To and sending Rey to find Luke Skywalker.

34 ABY - Ahch-To and Crait

R2-D2 travels with the Resistance during the flight from the First Order, replaying Leia's original message to persuade Luke to help, and survives the evacuation of Crait.

35 ABY - The war against the Final Order

R2-D2 supports the Resistance from Ajan Kloss through the Battle of Exegol, restoring C-3PO's wiped memory afterward. He is last recorded alive and active; canon gives no death.

title: Greedo
description: Greedo was a male Rodian bounty hunter and mercenary, most infamous for his fatal encounter with the smuggler Han Solo in the Mos Eisley cantina. Born on the swamp world of Rodia, Greedo relocated in his youth to the desert planet Tatooine, growing up in Mos Espa. He fancied himself a big-time hunter working for the Hutt crime lord Jabba, though few took him seriously. During the Clone Wars he was hired amid the Trade Federation's blockade of Pantora to kidnap the daughters of Chairman Notluwiski Papanoida, only to be cornered by the chairman himself and forced to lead him to the captives. Years later, dispatched by Jabba to collect on Han Solo's debt, the overconfident and unlucky Rodian was gunned down by Solo before he could pull the trigger.
timeline:
44 BBY - Birth on Rodia

Greedo is born on the swamp planet Rodia, homeworld of the Rodian species. He relocates during his youth to Tatooine, growing up in Mos Espa and later entering the bounty hunting trade.

~21 BBY - Kidnapping of the Papanoida daughters

During the Clone Wars and the Trade Federation's blockade of Pantora, Greedo and a Gotal accomplice kidnap Che Amanwe and Chi Eekway Papanoida, daughters of Pantoran Chairman Notluwiski Papanoida, to pressure him toward the Separatists. Papanoida and his son Ion track Greedo down and force him to lead them to the captives.

0 BBY - Death in the Mos Eisley cantina

Hired by Jabba the Hutt to collect on Han Solo's debt, Greedo corners Solo in the Mos Eisley cantina on Tatooine. Before he can act, Solo shoots and kills the luckless Rodian, shortly before the Battle of Yavin.

deathEvent.title: Episode IV: A New Hope

title: Garazeb Orrelios
description: Garazeb "Zeb" Orrelios was a male Lasat who served as a Captain of the Lasan Honor Guard before becoming a founding member of the Spectres, the Lothal-based rebel cell that operated aboard the starship Ghost. Trained in the traditional Lasat martial art and armed with his bo-rifle, Zeb was one of the few members of his species to survive the Empire's brutal siege of his homeworld of Lasan, an atrocity that left him with a deep, personal hatred of the Empire and of Agent Kallus in particular. Temperamental, brash, and physically powerful yet possessed of a strong moral compass and quiet compassion, he fought alongside Hera Syndulla, Kanan Jarrus, Ezra Bridger, Sabine Wren, and the droid Chopper throughout the early rebellion against the Galactic Empire. His personal journey reached its emotional peak when he discovered Lira San, the Lasat's ancestral homeworld, learning that his people had not been rendered extinct. After the Galactic Civil War he eventually reconciled with Kallus and guided the reformed Imperial to Lira San, and he continued to live on into the era of the New Republic.
timeline:
44 BBY - Birth on Lasan

Garazeb Orrelios is born on the planet Lasan, homeworld of the Lasat species, roughly twelve years before the Battle of Naboo.

~5 BBY - Captain of the Lasan Honor Guard

Zeb rises to the rank of Captain in the elite Lasan Honor Guard, the warriors sworn to protect the Lasat royal family, and masters the bo-rifle, a weapon unique to the guard.

~5 BBY - The Siege of Lasan

The Galactic Empire, with Agent Kallus among its forces, devastates Lasan and all but exterminates the Lasat. Zeb survives, one of the few of his kind left, and is left carrying survivor's guilt and a burning hatred of the Empire.

~5 BBY - Joining the Ghost crew

Displaced and embittered, Zeb falls in with Hera Syndulla and Kanan Jarrus, becoming a founding member of the Spectres rebel cell operating from the freighter Ghost above Lothal.

~5 BBY - The arrival of Ezra Bridger

The crew takes in the young Lothal street orphan Ezra Bridger; Zeb forms a bickering but affectionate bond with him and continues raiding Imperial supply lines and disrupting Imperial operations.

~4 BBY - Confronting Kallus

Zeb clashes personally with Agent Kallus, the Imperial responsible for atrocities against the Lasat, and the two develop a bitter rivalry that will slowly evolve over the years.

~3 BBY - Discovery of Lira San

Guided by the Lasat mystics Chava and Gron and using his bo-rifle to navigate a collapsed star cluster, Zeb pilots the Ghost to Lira San, the Lasat's original homeworld, and discovers that his people survive there in great numbers, restoring his sense of purpose.

~3 BBY - Stranded with Kallus

Marooned together on an icy moon, Zeb and Kallus are forced to cooperate to survive, beginning the thaw in their relationship that eventually turns Kallus into a rebel informant and ally.

1 BBY - Liberation of Lothal

Zeb takes part in the Spectres' final campaign to free Lothal from Imperial control, one of the rebellion's key victories in the lead-up to the wider Galactic Civil War.

~5 ABY - Return to Lira San with Kallus

After the war, Zeb brings the former Imperial Agent Kallus to Lira San, showing him that he had not truly extinguished the Lasat and completing their reconciliation.

~9 ABY - Appearance during the New Republic era

Now living into the era of the New Republic, Zeb appears alive and well, reflecting his survival long past the fall of the Empire.

title: Lando Calrissian
description: Landonis Balthazar "Lando" Calrissian is a human smuggler, gambler, entrepreneur, and sabacc player who rose to become Baron Administrator of Cloud City on Bespin and, later, a general in the Rebel Alliance. Born on Socorro, he was the original owner of the Millennium Falcon before losing the freighter to Han Solo in a game of sabacc. Charming and self-interested but ultimately loyal, he is best known for his fraught reunion with Han at Cloud City—where he was coerced by Darth Vader into betraying his friends before turning against the Empire—and for piloting the Falcon during the Battle of Endor that destroyed the second Death Star. Decades later, having lived as a recluse after the First Order abducted his infant daughter, he answered the call to rally reinforcements for the Battle of Exegol, helping secure the Resistance's final victory over the Sith Eternal.
timeline:
~43 BBY - Birth on Socorro

Lando Calrissian is born on the desert world of Socorro. Canon gives this date only as an approximation, derived from his stated age of 78 during the war against the First Order.

~10 BBY - Losing the Millennium Falcon

An established smuggler and gambler, Lando owns the Millennium Falcon and joins Han Solo, Chewbacca, and Qi'ra on the Kessel heist. He loses the Falcon to Han in a game of sabacc on Numidian Prime and parts ways to resume his own ventures.

~5 BBY - Schemes on Lothal

Having eased back from full-time smuggling, Lando runs assorted schemes, including a small operation tied to Lothal, and crosses paths with the Ghost crew and Ezra Bridger during the early rebellion.

3 ABY - Betrayal and reversal at Cloud City

As Baron Administrator of Cloud City on Bespin, Lando is forced by Darth Vader and the Empire to lure Han Solo and his companions into a trap. Regretting the betrayal, he turns against the Empire, helps rescue Leia and the others, and joins the hunt to recover the carbonite-frozen Han.

4 ABY - General at the Battle of Endor

Commissioned a general in the Rebel Alliance, Lando pilots the Millennium Falcon into the second Death Star and destroys its reactor core during the Battle of Endor, helping bring down the Galactic Empire.

35 ABY - Rallying the fleet at Exegol

Living as a recluse after the First Order stole his daughter years earlier, Lando aids the Resistance and, alongside Chewbacca, leads a massive citizens' fleet to the Battle of Exegol, contributing to the defeat of the Sith Eternal. Afterward he sets out to help Jannah find her home and family.

title: Saw Gerrera
description: Saw Gerrera was a human male freedom fighter from Onderon whose four-decade war against tyranny made him both a founding figure of the rebellion and its most feared extremist. During the Clone Wars he and his younger sister Steela led the Onderon rebels against the Separatist-installed king Sanjay Rash, trained in insurgency by Jedi sent secretly by the Republic — Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, Ahsoka Tano and Captain Rex — and their victory restored King Ramsis Dendup to the throne, though Steela was killed in the fighting. When the Republic became the Galactic Empire, Saw simply kept fighting, building the Partisans into a militia whose brutal, uncompromising tactics alienated even sympathetic rebels such as Mon Mothma and Luthen Rael and eventually saw him expelled from the nascent Alliance. He rescued and raised Galen Erso's daughter Jyn after her mother's death, only to abandon her as a teenager, and he investigated Imperial atrocities from the emptied hives of Geonosis to the kyber mines of Jedha. Broken in body and dependent on breathing apparatus, Saw ran his final insurgency from the Catacombs of Cadera on Jedha, where he confirmed the truth of the Death Star through Galen's holographic message before choosing to stand his ground and die in the destruction of Jedha City.
timeline:
~42 BBY - Birth on Onderon

Saw is born on the Inner Rim world of Onderon, elder brother to Steela Gerrera. Canon never states his birth year; he is a grown man by the late Clone Wars and visibly middle-aged by his death.

~20 BBY - The Onderonian rebellion begins

After Separatist-backed King Sanjay Rash deposes Ramsis Dendup, Saw, Steela and Lux Bonteri form a rebel cell in the jungles outside Iziz.

~20 BBY - Trained by the Jedi

The Jedi Council covertly sends Anakin Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, Ahsoka Tano and Captain Rex to train the Onderon rebels in guerrilla warfare and sabotage.

~20 BBY - Capture and rescue

Saw is captured and tortured by droid forces after acting against orders; the rebels break him out during an operation in Iziz.

~20 BBY - Liberation of Onderon and Steela's death

Armed with Republic-supplied rocket launchers, the rebels destroy the Separatist droid presence and free King Dendup, but Steela falls to her death during the final battle — a loss that hardens Saw permanently.

19 BBY - The Empire rises

The Clone Wars end with the proclamation of the Galactic Empire, and Onderon's liberation gives way to Imperial occupation. Saw refuses to stand down.

~18 BBY - Insurgency with Clone Force 99

Now leading an early anti-Imperial fighting force on Onderon, Saw crosses paths with the renegade clones of the Bad Batch, warning them that neutrality is not an option and eventually abandoning his camp to take the fight elsewhere.

13 BBY - Rescuing Jyn Erso

Answering Galen and Lyra Erso's plea, Saw arrives on Lah'mu after Krennic's raid and takes the orphaned eight-year-old Jyn Erso into his care, raising her as a soldier.

~5 BBY - Abandoning Jyn

Fearing that her identity as Galen Erso's daughter makes her a liability — and her a liability to him — Saw leaves the sixteen-year-old Jyn behind to fend for herself.

5 BBY - Dealings with Luthen Rael

Saw negotiates with the rebel broker Luthen Rael for weapons and intelligence, while rejecting Luthen's push to unite the galaxy's fractured rebel cells under a common command.

~3 BBY - "Revolution is not for the sane"

Operating from a hidden fuel depot, Saw takes on the young Wilmon Paak and delivers his creed on the intoxicating madness of rebellion, exposing himself to rhydonium fumes that further ruin his lungs.

2 BBY - The mystery of Geonosis

Hunting for the truth behind the Empire's secret construction project, Saw meets the crew of the Ghost above Geonosis and discovers the near-total extermination of the Geonosian species, plus evidence of a massive kyber crystal shipment.

~1 BBY - The Partisans on Jedha

Following the kyber trail, Saw bases his Partisans in the Catacombs of Cadera and wages a violent street war against Imperial convoys in the Holy City, alienating the Rebel Alliance leadership with his methods.

0 BBY - Interrogating Bodhi Rook

Saw's Partisans capture the defecting Imperial cargo pilot Bodhi Rook, who carries Galen Erso's message; Saw subjects him to the mind-shredding Bor Gullet before accepting his story.

0 BBY - Reunion with Jyn Erso

Rebel intelligence brings Jyn back to Jedha. Saw, now frail and reliant on a breathing apparatus, apologises for abandoning her and shows her Galen's holographic confession revealing the Death Star's deliberate flaw.

0 BBY - Death in the destruction of Jedha City

When the Death Star fires a single-reactor blast at the Holy City, Saw refuses to flee, telling Jyn to save the Rebellion and "save the dream." He is killed as the shockwave engulfs the catacombs.

deathEvent.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story

title: Carson Teva
description: Carson Teva was a human male pilot and officer who fought for the Alliance to Restore the Republic during the Galactic Civil War and afterward served as a captain in the Adelphi Rangers, a starfighter squadron of the New Republic Defense Fleet's Starfighter Corps based out of Adelphi Base in the Outer Rim. Flying a T-65B X-wing, he became one of the few New Republic officers willing to treat the lawless Outer Rim as the Republic's problem rather than a distant nuisance, and he repeatedly clashed with a bureaucracy in the Core that dismissed reports of surviving Imperial warlords. He first crossed paths with the Mandalorian bounty hunter Din Djarin around 9 ABY over the Razor Crest's dead transponder, and thereafter became a recurring — and increasingly sympathetic — ally, investigating the massacre of Imperial remnant survivors on Morak, the Marshal Cara Dune's jurisdictional troubles on Nevarro, and Imperial-linked wreckage that Coruscant refused to take seriously. His warnings about a resurgent Imperial threat were vindicated as the Shadow Council and Grand Admiral Thrawn's return came to light; he supported Greef Karga and the Mandalorians during the fighting at Nevarro, and later backed General Hera Syndulla, testifying on her behalf before a New Republic tribunal on Coruscant and quietly aiding her unsanctioned search for Ezra Bridger. Teva is not recorded as having died in canon and remains alive as of the latest events in which he appears.
timeline:
Unknown - Birth

Carson Teva was born a human male at an unrecorded point before the Galactic Civil War; canon establishes no birth year or homeworld for him. Our timeline's placement is a positioning guess only.

Between 5 BBY and 4 ABY - Service with the Rebel Alliance

Teva fought for the Alliance to Restore the Republic against the Galactic Empire during the Galactic Civil War, an experience that shaped his lifelong distrust of Imperial remnants.

After 4 ABY - Commission in the New Republic

Following the Empire's defeat, Teva joined the New Republic Defense Fleet's Starfighter Corps, rose to the rank of captain, and was assigned to the Adelphi Rangers, patrolling the Outer Rim from Adelphi Base in a T-65B X-wing.

9 ABY - Intercepting the Razor Crest

While patrolling the Outer Rim with fellow pilot Trapper Wolf, Teva stopped the gunship Razor Crest over its inactive transponder. After linking its pilot, Din Djarin, to a breakout aboard a New Republic Correctional Transport, the pilots pursued him to Maldo Kreis, but let him go in exchange for his help capturing the escaped prisoners.

9 ABY - Investigating the Morak massacre

Teva investigated the wreckage of an Imperial refinery transport on Morak and recovered a Mandalorian-linked lead, taking his findings to Marshal Cara Dune on Nevarro and urging the New Republic to recognize the surviving Imperial threat.

9 ABY - The prisoner on Nevarro

Teva brought Marshal Dune an assignment concerning a wanted prisoner, drawing her into a matter with personal stakes and further entangling Nevarro's local authority with New Republic business.

9 ABY - Recruiting Din Djarin

With New Republic resources stretched thin across the Outer Rim, Teva approached Din Djarin about working as an independent contractor for the Rangers, an offer Djarin declined while still cooperating with him informally.

9 ABY - Ignored warnings on Coruscant

Teva presented evidence of coordinated Imperial remnant activity to New Republic Command on Coruscant, only to be brushed aside by an amnesty-program bureaucracy that refused to believe a remnant threat still existed.

9 ABY - Nevarro relief

When Nevarro and its Mandalorian allies came under attack by Imperial forces, Teva and the Adelphi Rangers responded, vindicating his long-dismissed warnings about a resurgent Empire.

9 ABY - Supporting General Syndulla

Teva backed General Hera Syndulla's pursuit of leads on Grand Admiral Thrawn's return, including her unsanctioned mission to Seatos, and testified in her defense when she faced a New Republic tribunal on Coruscant for acting without authorization.

After 9 ABY - Fate

Teva survived all recorded events and no death is established for him in canon; his later career remains unchronicled.
