title: Anakin Skywalker
description: Anakin Skywalker was a Human Force-sensitive born into slavery on Tatooine, discovered by Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn and believed to be the Chosen One prophesied to bring balance to the Force. Trained by Obi-Wan Kenobi, he became one of the most powerful Jedi of his generation and, as a General during the Clone Wars, earned the nickname the "Hero With No Fear." He secretly married Senator Padmé Amidala, and fear of losing her — manipulated by Chancellor Palpatine (Darth Sidious) — drove him to the dark side. Reborn as the Sith Lord Darth Vader, he helped exterminate the Jedi Order and enforce the Galactic Empire's rule for over two decades. Ultimately, confronted by his son Luke Skywalker, he turned against the Emperor to save Luke, fulfilling the prophecy and dying redeemed as Anakin Skywalker.
timeline:
41 BBY - Birth on Tatooine

Anakin is born to Shmi Skywalker, who claimed he had no father. He grows up enslaved on the desert world of Tatooine.

32 BBY - Discovered by Qui-Gon Jinn

At age nine, Anakin is found by Qui-Gon Jinn, wins his freedom by winning the Boonta Eve podrace, and travels to Coruscant. After the Battle of Naboo, Obi-Wan Kenobi vows to train him as a Jedi.

22 BBY - Reunion with Padmé and secret marriage

Now a Padawan, Anakin protects Senator Padmé Amidala, fights at the Battle of Geonosis where he loses his right forearm, and secretly marries Padmé, beginning the Clone Wars.

22-19 BBY - The Clone Wars

Anakin serves as a Jedi General and Knight, takes Ahsoka Tano as his Padawan, and becomes a celebrated war hero known as the "Hero With No Fear."

19 BBY - Fall to the dark side and rebirth as Darth Vader

Seduced by Palpatine's promise to save Padmé from death, Anakin becomes Darth Vader, participates in the destruction of the Jedi Order, is defeated by Obi-Wan on Mustafar, and is rebuilt into the armored Sith Lord.

9 BBY - Hunting the surviving Jedi

As Darth Vader, he pursues fugitive Jedi and duels his former master Obi-Wan Kenobi once more during Obi-Wan's efforts to protect the Skywalker children.

0 BBY - The Battle of Yavin

Vader oversees the Empire's efforts to recover the stolen Death Star plans and participates in the battle over Yavin, where his TIE fighter is knocked away before the station's destruction.

3 ABY - Pursuit of Luke Skywalker

At Cloud City, Vader defeats Luke in a duel, severs his hand, and reveals that he is Luke's father.

4 ABY - Redemption and death

Aboard the second Death Star, Vader turns back to the light to save Luke from Emperor Palpatine, killing the Emperor at the cost of his own life and dying redeemed as Anakin Skywalker.

~11 ABY - Force ghost

As a Force spirit, Anakin appears to his former Padawan Ahsoka Tano, guiding her through a vision of their shared past during the Clone Wars.

deathEvent.title: Episode VI: Return of the Jedi

title: Grogu
description: Grogu, affectionately nicknamed "Baby Yoda" and known to many simply as "the Child," is a Force-sensitive foundling belonging to the same mysterious species as Jedi Grand Master Yoda and Jedi Master Yaddle. Born around 41 BBY, he was raised as an initiate at the Jedi Temple on Coruscant during the Clone Wars before being rescued from the Great Jedi Purge and hidden away for decades. Long afterward he was tracked down by the Mandalorian bounty hunter Din Djarin, who defied his Imperial-aligned clients to protect the child, forging a deep father-and-son bond. Grogu briefly began Jedi training under Luke Skywalker but ultimately chose his attachment to Din over the Jedi path, was formally adopted as "Din Grogu," and continued at the Mandalorian's side. He is remarkable for his precocious command of the Force despite his youth and for embodying the intersection of the Jedi and Mandalorian traditions.
timeline:
~41 BBY - Birth

Grogu is born, a member of the same rare, unnamed species as Yoda. His homeworld is never identified in canon. This year is derived from his stated age of fifty during the events of 9 ABY.

~41 BBY to 19 BBY - Raised at the Jedi Temple

Grogu is taken to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant and raised as a Jedi initiate through the final years of the Republic and the Clone Wars, trained by several masters.

19 BBY - Rescue from the Great Jedi Purge

During Order 66 and the fall of the Jedi Order, the young Grogu is rescued from the Jedi Temple by Jedi Master Kelleran Beq with the aid of sympathetic Naboo forces, and is subsequently hidden away.

19 BBY to 9 ABY - Decades in hiding

For roughly the next fifty years Grogu's memories become clouded and his whereabouts unknown, as he is concealed from those who would exploit his connection to the Force.

9 ABY - Found by Din Djarin

The Mandalorian Din Djarin accepts a bounty and locates Grogu, but rather than surrender the child to his Imperial-aligned clients he rescues him, beginning their journey together across the galaxy.

9 ABY - Protected from the Empire

Din Djarin shields Grogu from Moff Gideon and Imperial remnant forces seeking the child for his blood, which is rich in the Force, as the pair seek out the Jedi.

9 ABY - Training under Luke Skywalker

After Grogu is captured and rescued, Luke Skywalker arrives and takes the child to begin Jedi training, offering him the choice between the Jedi path and his bond with Din.

9 ABY - Choosing the Mandalorian bond

Grogu chooses Din Djarin's beskar chain mail over Yoda's lightsaber, leaving Luke's tutelage to reunite with the Mandalorian in The Book of Boba Fett.

9 ABY - Adventures and adoption as Din Grogu

Grogu accompanies Din Djarin through further trials, including the retaking of Mandalore, and is formally adopted as a Mandalorian apprentice under the name "Din Grogu."

title: Fennec Shand
description: Fennec Shand is a human female bounty hunter, elite mercenary and master assassin who built a fearsome reputation working for the galaxy's top crime syndicates, known for her marksmanship with a long-range sniper rifle, her cunning, and her willingness to change allegiance when the payout justifies it. She first rose to prominence in the years just after the Clone Wars, when she was hired to track the young clone Omega — a hunt that took her from Pantora across the galaxy and repeatedly pitted her against Clone Force 99, though she ultimately proved a more ambiguous figure than a straightforward villain in that pursuit. Decades later, during the New Republic era, she was hunted on Tatooine by the novice bounty hunter Toro Calican and the Mandalorian Din Djarin and left for dead in the Dune Sea, only to be recovered and rebuilt with cybernetic implants by Boba Fett, to whom she then pledged her loyalty. As Fett's partner she helped him reclaim Jabba the Hutt's palace and install himself as daimyo of Mos Espa, fought in the war against the Pyke Syndicate, and joined Din Djarin's crew in the rescue of Grogu from Moff Gideon's cruiser. She is not known to have died in canon.
timeline:
Unknown - Birth

Canon does not record when or where Fennec Shand was born. By the end of the Clone Wars she was already an experienced operator, so any birth year on the timeline is an estimate for positioning only.

~19 BBY - Hired to hunt Omega

In the first months of the Galactic Empire, Shand was engaged by Kaminoan scientist Nala Se to recover the young clone Omega, beginning a long pursuit of the child and her protectors in Clone Force 99.

~19 BBY - Betrayal on Pantora

Shand won Omega's trust on Pantora before turning on her, fighting off Wrecker and chasing the girl across the city rooftops. Omega escaped back to Hunter, and Shand vowed to continue the hunt.

~19 BBY - Pursuit across the Outer Rim

She continued tracking Clone Force 99, at one point capturing Omega and delivering her toward Kamino, while quietly withholding information about the girl's true value from other interested parties.

~18 BBY - The Bad Batch's final campaign

Working alongside Nala Se's interests, Shand remained entangled with Omega's fate through the Empire's cloning program, ultimately aiding rather than opposing the effort to free the clones from Imperial custody.

9 ABY - Hunted on Tatooine

Now a wanted assassin with bounties from every major syndicate, Shand was tracked across the Dune Sea by the rookie bounty hunter Toro Calican and Din Djarin. Calican shot her and left her for dead in the sand.

9 ABY - Rebuilt by Boba Fett

A mysterious armored figure — Boba Fett — found her dying body and had her wounds replaced with cybernetics, saving her life. In return she pledged herself to Fett's service.

9 ABY - Recovery of Boba Fett's armor

Shand accompanied Fett to Tython, where he bargained with Din Djarin for the return of his Mandalorian armor and then covered the Mandalorian in the fight against Imperial forces.

9 ABY - Rescue of Grogu

She joined Din Djarin's assembled crew — Boba Fett, Cara Dune and Bo-Katan Kryze — in boarding Moff Gideon's light cruiser to free the child Grogu from Imperial captivity.

9 ABY - Seizing Jabba's palace

With Fett, Shand stormed the Hutt palace on Tatooine and helped install him as the new daimyo of Mos Espa, serving as his enforcer and chief strategist.

9 ABY - War with the Pyke Syndicate

Shand fought in the street battle for Mos Espa against Pyke forces alongside Fett, Din Djarin and the Mods, and afterward eliminated the crime bosses who had betrayed them.

After 9 ABY - Fate unknown

Shand survives every appearance recorded in canon to date; no death has been depicted, and her column on the timeline is open-ended rather than terminated by a death year.

title: Ahsoka Tano
description: Ahsoka Tano is a Togruta former Jedi and one of the most prominent figures spanning the fall of the Republic and the rise of the New Republic era. Discovered as a Force-sensitive child on her homeworld of Shili by Jedi Master Plo Koon, she trained at the Jedi Temple on Coruscant and, at the outbreak of the Clone Wars, was assigned by Grand Master Yoda as the Padawan of Anakin Skywalker, who nicknamed her "Snips." Over the course of the war she matured from an eager apprentice into a capable commander, but after being framed for a bombing of the Jedi Temple and cleared only after nearly being expelled, she chose to walk away from the Jedi Order. She survived Order 66, went into hiding, and later became a key organizer of the growing rebellion under the codename "Fulcrum," aiding rebel cells and confronting her fallen master, now Darth Vader. In the decades after the Empire's defeat she continued as a wandering ally of the New Republic, hunting the missing Grand Admiral Thrawn and mentoring Sabine Wren, becoming an iconic bridge between the prequel, original, and sequel eras of Star Wars.
timeline:
36 BBY - Birth on Shili

Ahsoka Tano is born on the Togruta homeworld of Shili.

~33 BBY - Discovered by Plo Koon

At around three years of age, her Force sensitivity is detected and Jedi Master Plo Koon brings her to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant for training.

22 BBY - Padawan to Anakin Skywalker

At the outbreak of the Clone Wars, Yoda assigns Ahsoka as Anakin Skywalker's apprentice; she joins him at the Battle of Christophsis, earning the nickname "Snips," and helps rescue Rotta the Huttlet to secure a Republic–Hutt alliance.

22–19 BBY - Commander in the Clone Wars

Ahsoka serves throughout the war, growing from a headstrong Padawan into a skilled commander alongside Anakin and Captain Rex.

19 BBY - Leaving the Jedi Order

Framed for the bombing of the Jedi Temple hangar and nearly expelled, Ahsoka is exonerated but, disillusioned with the Order, chooses to leave the Jedi.

19 BBY - Order 66 and the Siege of Mandalore

Reuniting with Rex during the Siege of Mandalore, she survives Order 66 when Rex's inhibitor chip is removed, and afterward fakes her death to go into hiding.

~18–4 BBY - Years in hiding and the rebellion

Living in obscurity, Ahsoka gradually becomes involved in early resistance to the Empire, eventually coordinating rebel cells.

~4–2 BBY - Fulcrum

Operating under the codename "Fulcrum," she supplies intelligence and support to the growing rebel network alongside the crew of the Ghost.

2 BBY - Confrontation on Malachor

Ahsoka faces Darth Vader and discovers he is her former master Anakin; she is presumed lost in the ruined Sith temple.

~1 BBY - The World Between Worlds

Ezra Bridger pulls Ahsoka out of Malachor through the World Between Worlds, saving her life.

~1 ABY - Searching for Ezra

After the Empire's fall at Endor, Ahsoka sets out to find the missing Ezra Bridger and the vanished Grand Admiral Thrawn.

9 ABY - The Mandalorian and Boba Fett era

Now a wandering warrior, Ahsoka appears on Corvus and later aids Din Djarin and Grogu, continuing her hunt for Thrawn.

9 ABY - The Ahsoka series

Ahsoka mentors Sabine Wren, journeys to a distant galaxy, and confronts the returned Grand Admiral Thrawn, working to prevent his return threatening the New Republic.

title: C-3PO
description: C-3PO, also spelled See-Threepio and usually called simply Threepio, is a 3PO-series protocol droid of Cybot Galactica design, programmed for etiquette and protocol and fluent in over six million forms of communication. Unusually among droids, he was not factory-built but assembled from salvaged scrap by the enslaved boy Anakin Skywalker in Mos Espa on Tatooine, and was operational — though still stripped of his outer plating — by 32 BBY. Fussy, anxious and endlessly voluble, he became the inseparable and perpetually exasperated counterpart of the astromech R2-D2, and the pair drifted through nearly every hinge point of galactic history: the Naboo crisis, the Battle of Geonosis and the Clone Wars, the secret marriage of Anakin and Padmé Amidala, the birth of the Skywalker twins, and a memory wipe that erased all of it before he passed into the service of Senator Bail Organa on Alderaan. Serving the Organa family and then the Rebel Alliance, he carried the stolen Death Star plans aboard the Tantive IV, was worshipped as a deity by the Ewoks of Endor, and later served the New Republic and General Leia Organa. In 35 ABY he sacrificed his own memory to translate forbidden Sith text and set the path to Exegol, a loss R2-D2 later undid. He is never destroyed in canon and survives the fall of the Final Order.
timeline:
~32 BBY - Assembled on Tatooine

Built from scavenged parts by the enslaved boy Anakin Skywalker in the family hovel in Mos Espa, C-3PO is operational but unfinished, lacking his outer plating. Anakin presents him to Padmé Amidala and intends him to help his mother Shmi.

32 BBY - Left behind on Tatooine

When Anakin wins his freedom and departs with Qui-Gon Jinn to become a Jedi, C-3PO remains on Tatooine in Shmi Skywalker's care.

22 BBY - Reunion and plating

Anakin returns to Tatooine searching for his mother and finds C-3PO in the service of the Lars homestead, now fitted with metal coverings. The droid joins Anakin, Padmé and R2-D2 on their journey to Geonosis.

22 BBY - Battle of Geonosis

Caught in a Geonosian droid factory, C-3PO's head is knocked onto a battle droid body and a battle droid head onto his, leaving him charging into battle shouting "die, Jedi dogs" before R2-D2 restores him. He afterwards witnesses the secret wedding of Anakin and Padmé on Naboo.

22 BBY - 19 BBY - The Clone Wars

Attached to Senator Amidala's household on Coruscant, C-3PO is repeatedly swept into the war alongside R2-D2 — abducted, marooned, mistaken for a spy and pressed into diplomatic missions across the galaxy.

19 BBY - Fall of the Republic and memory wipe

C-3PO is present at Padmé's death on Polis Massa and the birth of Luke and Leia. To protect the twins' secret, Bail Organa orders the protocol droid's memory erased, and C-3PO enters the service of House Organa on Alderaan with his past wiped clean.

9 BBY - Service on Alderaan

Now a fixture of the Organa royal household, C-3PO attends a formal reception at the palace during the years Bail and Breha are raising the young Princess Leia.

~5 BBY - Droids in distress

Sent by Senator Bail Organa on a covert errand involving stolen weapons, C-3PO and R2-D2 cross paths with the crew of the Ghost, unwittingly aiding the early rebellion.

1 BBY - Yavin 4

C-3PO is present at the Rebel Alliance's Massassi base as Jyn Erso's team departs for Scarif to steal the Death Star plans.

0 BBY - Capture of the Tantive IV

Aboard Leia's corvette when Darth Vader seizes it, C-3PO follows R2-D2 into an escape pod carrying the Death Star plans and crashes on Tatooine.

0 BBY - Sold to the Lars homestead

Captured by Jawas and sold to Owen Lars, C-3PO meets Luke Skywalker and helps set in motion the delivery of Leia's message to Obi-Wan Kenobi.

0 BBY - Battle of Yavin

C-3PO waits out the assault on the Death Star at the Yavin base and offers his own circuits to repair a badly damaged R2-D2 after the victory.

3 ABY - Hoth and Bespin

Serving the Alliance on Echo Base, C-3PO flees with Han, Leia and Chewbacca aboard the Millennium Falcon. On Cloud City he is blasted apart by stormtroopers and carried out in pieces on Chewbacca's back, then reassembled.

4 ABY - Tatooine and Endor

Offered to Jabba the Hutt as part of Luke's rescue plan, C-3PO serves as the Hutt's interpreter before escaping. On the forest moon of Endor the Ewoks take him for a golden deity, and his storytelling wins their alliance for the strike on the shield generator.

5 ABY - 34 ABY - New Republic service

C-3PO remains with Leia Organa through the founding of the New Republic and into the Resistance, eventually overseeing its droid network, and takes on a mismatched red arm for a period before restoring his gold plating.

9 ABY - New Republic Senate errand

Acting on Leia's authority, C-3PO delivers her intervention to a Senate committee on Hera Syndulla's behalf during the hearing over her unsanctioned mission.

34 ABY - The search for Luke Skywalker

C-3PO reunites with Han Solo and helps the Resistance assemble the map to Luke Skywalker's exile, then witnesses the destruction of the Hosnian system and the assault on Starkiller Base.

34 ABY - Crait

He survives the collapse of the Resistance fleet and the last stand on Crait among the handful of survivors aboard the Millennium Falcon.

35 ABY - Sacrifice of memory

To translate a Sith inscription pointing to Exegol, C-3PO permits a forbidden override that erases his memory entirely, taking one last look at his friends before the wipe.

35 ABY - Restoration and victory

R2-D2 restores C-3PO's memory from backup. He survives the Battle of Exegol and the fall of the Final Order, and celebrates with the victorious Resistance.

endYearEvent: Star Wars Rebels
title: Kanan Jarrus
description: Kanan Jarrus, born Caleb Dume, was a Force-sensitive human male Jedi Knight who survived Order 66 and became a founding member of the rebellion against the Galactic Empire. Trained as a Padawan by Jedi Master Depa Billaba, he was on Kaller when the clone troopers turned on the Jedi; his master's dying sacrifice allowed him to escape, and he shed his identity, abandoned the Jedi ways, and lived as a fugitive under the name Kanan Jarrus. After meeting the Twi'lek pilot Hera Syndulla during the Gorse conflict, he joined her nascent rebel cell aboard the Ghost. He is best known for leading that crew, for reluctantly returning to the Jedi path to train the orphan Ezra Bridger as his own Padawan, and for his role in the early Alliance to Restore the Republic. Blinded in a duel with Maul on Malachor, he continued to fight alongside his found family until he gave his life on Lothal to save Hera, Ezra, and Sabine from an exploding fuel depot.
timeline:
33 BBY - Birth on Coruscant

Caleb Dume is born on the galactic capital of Coruscant, a Force-sensitive human who is later identified and taken into the Jedi Order for training.

~22 BBY - Padawan to Depa Billaba

Around the outbreak of the Clone Wars, Caleb becomes the Padawan of Jedi Master Depa Billaba, serving alongside her and clone troopers in the conflict.

19 BBY - Order 66 on Kaller

While on Kaller with the Bad Batch clone squad and his master, Palpatine issues Order 66. Depa Billaba sacrifices herself so Caleb can flee, and the young Padawan narrowly escapes execution by the clones.

~19 BBY - Becoming Kanan Jarrus

Now a hunted fugitive, Caleb meets the smuggler Janus Kasmir, who teaches him to survive off the grid. He abandons the Jedi ways, adopts the alias Kanan Jarrus, and largely trades his lightsaber for a blaster.

~11 BBY - The Gorse Conflict

During the Gorse conflict, Kanan meets the Twi'lek pilot and rebel operative Hera Syndulla. Drawn to her cause, he agrees to join her growing rebel cell aboard the Ghost.

~5 BBY - Meeting Ezra Bridger

Operating out of Lothal with the Ghost crew, Kanan encounters the street orphan Ezra Bridger. Sensing the boy's strength in the Force, he reluctantly takes him on as his Padawan, returning to the Jedi path he once abandoned.

~4 BBY - Duel with the Grand Inquisitor

Kanan is captured and interrogated by the Empire, then rescued by his crew. In the ensuing confrontation he defeats the Grand Inquisitor, an early victory against the Imperial hunt for surviving Jedi.

~3 BBY - Blinded on Malachor

On the ancient Sith world of Malachor, Kanan is blinded by Maul during the battle. He adapts to his loss of sight, continuing to fight and train Ezra while wearing a protective mask over his eyes.

1 BBY - Death on Lothal

During the mission to rescue Hera on Lothal, Kanan uses the Force to hold back the flames of an exploding fuel depot, sacrificing himself so that Hera, Ezra, and Sabine can escape. He dies a Jedi, having come full circle from the frightened Padawan of Order 66.

events.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story

title: Cassian Andor
description: Cassian Jeron Andor was a Rebel intelligence officer, spy, and pilot who became one of the most important operatives of the Alliance to Restore the Republic. Born as a boy named Kassa on the remote, mining-scarred world of Kenari, he was orphaned as a child and taken to the industrial planet Ferrix, where he was raised by Clem and Maarva Andor and grew into a cynical scavenger and thief. Recruited by the rebel spymaster Luthen Rael, Cassian was radicalized through the Aldhani garrison heist, a brutal wrongful imprisonment on Narkina 5, and the Imperial atrocities he witnessed — including the massacre on Ghorman — transforming from a disillusioned criminal into a committed and ruthless rebel agent operating under the codename Fulcrum and numerous cover identities. He is best known for leading the Rogue One team that infiltrated the Imperial data vault on Scarif to steal the Death Star plans, an act that gave the Rebellion its first true chance to destroy the battle station. Cassian was killed in the Death Star's bombardment of Scarif, sacrificing his life alongside Jyn Erso to ensure the plans reached the Alliance.
timeline:
33 BBY - Birth on Kenari

Cassian is born on the isolated planet Kenari, where he lives among a tribe of children as a boy named Kassa.

~26 BBY - Orphaned and taken to Ferrix

After a Republic vessel crashes on Kenari, the young Kassa is taken in by salvagers Clem and Maarva Andor, who give him the name Cassian and a forged identity claiming Fest as his birthplace, raising him on the industrial world of Ferrix.

5 BBY - Killing on Morlana One and recruitment

While searching for his lost sister, Cassian kills two corporate security officers, drawing the attention of rebel spymaster Luthen Rael, who recruits him into the fledgling rebellion.

5 BBY - The Aldhani heist

Cassian joins a rebel cell to raid an Imperial garrison payroll vault on Aldhani, a daring robbery that helps galvanize open resistance to the Empire.

5 BBY - Imprisonment on Narkina 5

Arrested on a trumped-up charge, Cassian is sentenced to a brutal Imperial labor prison on Narkina 5, from which he engineers a mass escape.

5 BBY - The Ferrix uprising

Returning home for Maarva's funeral, Cassian witnesses her recorded call to resistance spark an uprising against Imperial forces and fully commits himself to the rebel cause.

~2 BBY - The Ghorman Massacre

Now an established rebel operative, Cassian is present as the Empire slaughters civilian protesters on Ghorman, an atrocity that hardens his resolve and deepens the Alliance's coordinated resistance.

~1 BBY - Rebel intelligence operative

Cassian carries out assassinations, extractions, and espionage missions for the Alliance under the codename Fulcrum and a series of cover identities, becoming a trusted agent of Rebel Intelligence.

0 BBY - Rogue One and the Battle of Scarif

Cassian leads the volunteer Rogue One team to infiltrate the Imperial archive on Scarif and steal the Death Star plans; after transmitting them to the fleet, he and Jyn Erso are killed in the Death Star's bombardment of the planet.

title: Boba Fett
description: Boba Fett is a legendary bounty hunter and one of the most feared figures in the galactic underworld. He is an unaltered, unmodified clone of the Mandalorian bounty hunter Jango Fett, created on Kamino and raised by Jango as his son. Unlike his clone-trooper "brothers," Boba aged at a normal human rate and received no growth acceleration or behavioral conditioning. After witnessing his father's death at the Battle of Geonosis, he grew into a ruthless and highly capable hunter, wearing a customized set of Mandalorian armor and piloting the Firespray-class starship inherited from Jango. During the reign of the Galactic Empire he took contracts for both Darth Vader and the crime lord Jabba the Hutt, most famously capturing Han Solo. Presumed dead after falling into the Sarlacc pit on Tatooine, Boba survived, reclaimed his armor, and years later seized control of Jabba's former criminal empire, becoming the self-styled Daimyo of Mos Espa.
timeline:
32 BBY - Creation on Kamino

Boba Fett is born as an unaltered clone of Jango Fett, given to the bounty hunter as unpaid compensation for serving as the genetic template of the Republic's clone army. Jango raises him as his own son on Kamino.

22 BBY - Encounter with Obi-Wan Kenobi

As a young boy, Boba is on Kamino when Jedi Obi-Wan Kenobi investigates the origins of the clone army. He and Jango flee, engaging Obi-Wan's starfighter in a battle over Kamino and later in the asteroid field of Geonosis.

22 BBY - Death of Jango Fett at Geonosis

During the First Battle of Geonosis that ignites the Clone Wars, Boba watches as Jedi Master Mace Windu kills his father. He cradles Jango's empty helmet, an event that shapes his lifelong bitterness.

~21 BBY - Vengeance against Mace Windu

Now orphaned, Boba infiltrates a Republic cruiser under a false identity, seeking revenge on Mace Windu. His plot fails and he is captured, falling in with bounty hunters such as Aurra Sing and Bossk.

~3 BBY - Rise as a bounty hunter

Over the following years Boba builds his reputation across the criminal underworld, taking on his father's armor and ship and becoming one of the galaxy's most feared hunters during the Imperial era.

3 ABY - Hunting the Millennium Falcon

Darth Vader hires Boba among a group of bounty hunters to track the Millennium Falcon. Boba trails the ship to Cloud City on Bespin.

3 ABY - Capturing Han Solo

At Bespin, Boba claims the carbonite-frozen Han Solo as his bounty and transports him aboard his ship to deliver him to Jabba the Hutt on Tatooine.

4 ABY - Fall into the Sarlacc

During the battle at the Great Pit of Carkoon, Boba is knocked into the Sarlacc's pit after Han Solo accidentally strikes his jetpack. He is presumed dead by those present.

~4 ABY - Escape from the Sarlacc

Boba survives the Sarlacc, clawing his way out of the pit, though he loses his armor to Jawa scavengers and is left near death in the Dune Sea.

~9 ABY - Reclaiming his armor

Boba tracks down his Mandalorian armor, which has passed to the Mandalorian Din Djarin, and allies with him to rescue Grogu, reasserting himself as a formidable force on Tatooine.

9 ABY - Daimyo of Mos Espa

Boba seizes Jabba the Hutt's former palace and criminal territory, installing himself as the new crime lord, or Daimyo, of Mos Espa and defending his rule against rival syndicates.

title: Rex
description: Rex, born CT-7567, was a clone trooper of the Grand Army of the Republic, created on Kamino as a genetic replica of the bounty hunter Jango Fett. Rising to the rank of Clone Captain (and later Commander), he led the 501st Legion's Torrent Company during the Clone Wars, serving as second-in-command to Jedi General Anakin Skywalker and forging a close bond with Skywalker's Padawan Ahsoka Tano and with Marshal Commander Cody. Renowned for his bravery, tactical skill, and fierce loyalty to his men, Rex grew increasingly troubled by the clones' expendable treatment. During Order 66 the inhibitor chip in his brain forced him to turn on Ahsoka, but she removed it in time, allowing him to survive; the pair faked their deaths and became fugitives from the Empire. Years later he was found in self-imposed retirement on Seelos alongside fellow clones Wolffe and Gregor before joining the Rebellion. Rex ultimately fought in the Battle of Endor, making him one of the few clone troopers to survive the Clone Wars and live to see the fall of the Empire.
timeline:
32 BBY - Creation on Kamino

Rex is grown and decanted on the ocean world of Kamino, a clone of the bounty hunter Jango Fett, and given the designation CT-7567.

~22 BBY - The Clone Wars Begin

Rex serves as Captain of the 501st Legion's Torrent Company under Jedi General Anakin Skywalker, quickly earning a reputation for courage and dedication to his men.

~22 BBY - Partnership with Ahsoka Tano

Rex mentors and fights alongside Anakin's new Padawan, Ahsoka Tano, forming one of the war's most enduring friendships.

22 BBY to 19 BBY - Campaigns of the Clone Wars

Rex leads the 501st through countless front-line battles against the Confederacy of Independent Systems, working closely with Commander Cody and General Obi-Wan Kenobi, and grows disillusioned by the heavy losses among his brothers.

~19 BBY - Discovery of the Inhibitor Chips

The malfunction of clone trooper Tup leads Rex to help uncover the existence of the secret inhibitor chips implanted in every clone, foreshadowing Order 66.

19 BBY - Siege of Mandalore

Now a Commander, Rex leads the 501st alongside Ahsoka Tano to liberate Mandalore and capture Maul.

19 BBY - Order 66

Palpatine issues Order 66; Rex's inhibitor chip forces him to turn on Ahsoka, but she removes it in time. Together they survive the crash of their Star Destroyer and fake their deaths to escape the new Empire.

19 BBY - A Fugitive from the Empire

Considered killed in action, Rex goes into hiding, later removing the inhibitor chips from fellow clones Wolffe and Gregor.

~1 BBY - Retirement on Seelos

Rex, Wolffe, and Gregor live in a modified AT-TE walker on Seelos until the crew of the Ghost recruits Rex to the growing rebellion.

~1 BBY to 1 ABY - Service in the Rebellion

Rex fights alongside the Spectres and the Phoenix cell against the Empire, including the liberation of his adopted homeworld of Lothal.

4 ABY - Battle of Endor

Rex fights in the Battle of Endor, helping the Rebel Alliance destroy the second Death Star and bring down the Galactic Empire, surviving one of the war's decisive victories.

title: Hunter
description: Hunter was a clone commando sergeant of the Grand Army of the Republic and the commanding officer of Clone Force 99, the experimental squad of genetically enhanced clones nicknamed "the Bad Batch." Grown on Kamino from the genetic template of Jango Fett, he carried a mutation that gave him extraordinarily heightened senses — able to track targets by scent, sound and electromagnetic fields — which made him the squad's scout and de facto leader alongside the super-strong Wrecker, the analytical Tech and the sharpshooter Crosshair. During the Clone Wars he led the Batch on high-risk operations, most notably the campaign on Anaxes where they recovered the long-missing ARC trooper Echo from Skako Minor and folded him into the squad. Because their aberrant genetics left them largely immune to the inhibitor chips, Hunter and most of his men did not carry out Order 66, and their refusal to fire on civilians turned them into fugitives from the new Galactic Empire. On Kamino he took responsibility for Omega, an unaltered female clone, and the pair became the emotional center of the squad as they scraped out a mercenary living, lost Crosshair to Imperial conditioning and later lost Tech entirely on Eriadu. Hunter's driving goal became finding Omega a safe home, which he found on the island world of Pabu, and after storming the Empire's cloning facility at Mount Tantiss to rescue her and the other captive clones, he settled there. Years later he let Omega go to join the Rebel Alliance, remaining on Pabu with Wrecker and a reconciled Crosshair.
timeline:
~32 BBY - Grown on Kamino

Hunter is created on Kamino as part of the first generation of Jango Fett clones, one of a handful of "defective" units whose mutations were deemed advantageous. His alteration grants him superhumanly acute senses. Like all clones he is engineered to age at twice the normal rate.

~22 BBY - The Clone Wars begin

With the war between the Republic and the Confederacy underway, Hunter and his squadmates Wrecker, Tech and Crosshair are formed into Clone Force 99, named in honor of the malformed Kaminoan maintenance clone 99. Their unorthodox methods earn them a reputation for taking missions regular clones cannot.

~19 BBY - The Anaxes campaign

Assigned to assist Captain Rex, Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano on Anaxes, Hunter leads the Batch on a raid of a Separatist cyber center. He then agrees to Rex's mission to Skako Minor, where the squad rescues the presumed-dead ARC trooper Echo from Wat Tambor's captivity.

~19 BBY - Order 66 and the fall of the Republic

On Kaller, Hunter's squad witnesses clone troopers turn on Jedi General Depa Billaba. Their mutations leave them largely unaffected by the inhibitor chips, and Hunter shields the Padawan Caleb Dume rather than hunting him. Only Crosshair complies with the order.

~19 BBY - Desertion and Omega

Returning to Kamino under the new Empire, the Batch refuse to fire on civilians on Onderon and are imprisoned. Hunter takes in Omega, an unaltered clone girl, and leads the squad's escape — leaving behind Crosshair, whose chip is intensified by Imperial doctors. They begin a fugitive life running jobs for Cid on Ord Mantell.

~19 BBY - Return to Kamino

Lured back to Tipoca City by Crosshair, Hunter and the squad narrowly survive the Empire's orbital bombardment of the Kaminoan cloning facilities. Crosshair saves them but refuses to leave with them, and the clones' birthworld is destroyed.

~18 BBY - Finding Pabu

After breaking with Cid, the squad discovers the island world of Pabu. Hunter, increasingly focused on giving Omega a childhood rather than a war, decides it should become their home.

~18 BBY - The death of Tech

During a mission on Eriadu to help Clone Captain Howzer, Tech sacrifices himself so the others can escape, falling from a collapsing rail line. Shortly afterward Omega is captured by Imperial forces and taken to Mount Tantiss.

~18 BBY - Hunting for Tantiss

Hunter and Wrecker search relentlessly for the hidden Imperial facility, eventually joining forces with a defecting Crosshair after Omega helps him escape. Hunter is briefly captured himself before being rescued by Omega, Crosshair and Wrecker.

~18 BBY - Assault on Mount Tantiss

The Batch infiltrate Dr. Royce Hemlock's cloning complex on Weyland, free the captive clone children and Omega, and destroy the facility, ending the Empire's Necromancer cloning program. Hunter, Wrecker, Crosshair, Omega and Echo survive.

~18 BBY - Retirement on Pabu

Hunter settles on Pabu with Wrecker, Crosshair and Omega, laying down his armor and living the peaceful life he had fought to secure for her.

~5 BBY - Omega joins the Rebellion

Years later, a grown Omega tells Hunter that the coming fight against the Empire is hers, not his, and departs Pabu as a Rebel pilot. Hunter watches her go and remains on Pabu with his brothers; canon records nothing of his fate afterward.

title: Han Solo
description: Han Solo was a human smuggler from the Core World of Corellia who rose to become a general and one of the most vital leaders of the Alliance to Restore the Republic during the Galactic Civil War. Orphaned into the criminal underworld of Corellia, he escaped his homeworld, briefly served as an Imperial cadet, and forged a lifelong friendship with the Wookiee Chewbacca, who swore him a life debt. Alongside his prized ship the Millennium Falcon — won from Lando Calrissian — he became famed for completing the Kessel Run in under twelve parsecs while working for gangsters such as Jabba the Hutt. Drawn into the Rebellion after agreeing to transport Luke Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi to Alderaan, he helped rescue Princess Leia Organa, aided in the destruction of the first Death Star at the Battle of Yavin, and fought through Hoth, Bespin, and the Battle of Endor. He later married Leia and fathered Ben Solo, but their son's fall to the dark side as Kylo Ren shattered the family. In 34 ABY, Han was killed by his own son aboard Starkiller Base while trying to bring him home.
timeline:
32 BBY - Birth on Corellia

Han is born in a rough district of the Core World Corellia during the final years of the Galactic Republic, growing up as a scrumrat in the planet's criminal underworld.

~10 BBY - Escape, the Empire, and the Kessel Run

Han flees Corellia, briefly serves as an Imperial cadet where a recruitment officer gives him the surname "Solo," meets Chewbacca, wins the Millennium Falcon from Lando Calrissian, and completes the legendary Kessel Run in under twelve parsecs.

0 BBY - Chartering passage on Tatooine

A smuggler indebted to Jabba the Hutt, Han agrees to fly Luke Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and the droids to Alderaan aboard the Falcon from Mos Eisley.

0 BBY - Rescue of Leia and the Battle of Yavin

Drawn aboard the Death Star, Han helps rescue Princess Leia, then returns to cover Luke's trench run, enabling the destruction of the battle station at the Battle of Yavin.

3 ABY - Hoth and Bespin

Now committed to the Rebellion, Han fights at the Battle of Hoth, flees through an asteroid field, and is betrayed at Cloud City, where the Empire freezes him in carbonite and hands him to the bounty hunter Boba Fett.

4 ABY - Rescue and the Battle of Endor

Rescued from Jabba's palace by his friends, Han is promoted to general and leads the strike team that disables the second Death Star's shield generator on Endor, helping secure the Rebellion's decisive victory.

~5 ABY - Marriage and family

In the years after Endor, Han marries Leia Organa and they have a son, Ben Solo. Han's later estrangement from Leia follows Ben's fall to the dark side.

34 ABY - Death on Starkiller Base

Reunited with the Resistance, Han confronts his son — now Kylo Ren — on Starkiller Base in an attempt to bring him home. Ren kills his father, and Han falls to his death.

deathEvent.title: Episode VII: The Force Awakens

title: Omega
description: Omega was a female human clone of the Mandalorian bounty hunter Jango Fett, one of only two unaltered, pure genetic replications of his template created on Kamino — the other being Boba Fett. Because she was unmodified, she aged at a normal human rate rather than the accelerated rate of the Republic's clone troopers, and she grew up in Tipoca City as a medical assistant to the Kaminoan chief scientist Nala Se, who quietly protected her. At the close of the Clone Wars she befriended Clone Force 99, the experimental squad known as the Bad Batch, and fled Kamino with them as the Republic became the Galactic Empire, becoming the youngest member of their found family. Her unique, uncorrupted genetic material made her a target: the bounty hunters Fennec Shand and Cad Bane both hunted her, and she was eventually captured and held at Doctor Royce Hemlock's Mount Tantiss facility, where her blood was sought for the Empire's secret cloning research that would one day underpin Project Necromancer. Omega escaped Tantiss, helped free the imprisoned clones and children held there, and saw the facility destroyed, ending Hemlock's work at the cost of her brothers' long fight — Tech having already died on Eriadu. She settled on the island world of Pabu with Hunter, Wrecker and Crosshair, and years later, grown, she left home to fly for the Rebel Alliance, carrying Tech's goggles with her.
timeline:
~32 BBY - Creation on Kamino

Omega is produced in Tipoca City as an unaltered, pure genetic replication of Jango Fett's template, one of two such clones alongside Boba Fett. Unlike the clone army, she is not given accelerated aging and grows at a normal human rate.

~32 BBY to 19 BBY - Childhood in Tipoca City

Raised in the cloning facility under the watch of Nala Se, Omega works as a medical assistant and never leaves Kamino, observing the clone troopers she considers brothers.

19 BBY - Meeting Clone Force 99

Omega takes an interest in the experimental squad Clone Force 99 when they return from Kaller, and befriends them as Admiral Tarkin arrives to evaluate the clones' future.

19 BBY - Escape from Kamino

With the Republic reorganized into the Galactic Empire and Order 66 issued, Omega flees Kamino aboard the Marauder with Hunter, Wrecker, Tech and Echo, leaving Crosshair behind.

19 BBY - Hunted by Fennec Shand

Nala Se secretly hires the bounty hunter Fennec Shand to retrieve Omega, beginning a long pursuit across the Outer Rim as the Batch take mercenary work for Cid on Ord Mantell.

19 BBY - Return to Kamino and its destruction

Lured back to Kamino by Crosshair, Omega and the squad survive the Empire's bombardment of Tipoca City, escaping the flooded ruins of the only home she had known.

~19 BBY - Captured by Cad Bane and rescued

Bane abducts Omega and delivers her to the Kaminoan scientist Nala Se's Imperial handlers; the Batch and Hunter track her down and recover her.

~18 BBY - Life on the run and the loss of Tech

Working with Echo and the clone underground, Omega witnesses Tech sacrifice himself on Eriadu so the rest of the squad can survive — the first death in her family.

~18 BBY - Capture and imprisonment at Mount Tantiss

Doctor Royce Hemlock seizes Omega and takes her to the hidden Mount Tantiss facility on Weyland, where her uncorrupted M-count blood is harvested for the Empire's secret cloning experiments.

~18 BBY - Escape from Tantiss and the fall of Hemlock

Omega organizes and leads the imprisoned clones and captive children out of the vault; the Batch assault the mountain, Hemlock is killed by Crosshair, and the facility is destroyed.

~18 BBY - Settling on Pabu

Omega makes a home on the island world of Pabu with Hunter, Wrecker and Crosshair, finally living out of the Empire's reach.

~5 BBY - Leaving to join the Rebel Alliance

Grown to adulthood, Omega departs Pabu in her own ship to fly for the Rebellion, keeping Tech's goggles on her dashboard. The epilogue's exact year is not given in canon.

title: Qi'ra
description: Qi'ra is a human woman born on Corellia who rose from the streets of Coronet City to become one of the most powerful crime lords in the galaxy. As a child she was a scrumrat in the White Worms gang under Lady Proxima, where she grew up alongside — and fell in love with — Han Solo. Separated from Han during a failed escape at the Coronet Spaceport, she was sold into slavery and ultimately came under the criminal syndicate Crimson Dawn, becoming the trusted lieutenant of Dryden Vos. Reunited briefly with Han during the Kessel Run heist, she chose ambition over their relationship, killing Vos and answering to the syndicate's true master, Maul. After Maul's death she rose to lead Crimson Dawn as "Lady Qi'ra," waging a shadow war against the Sith and the Empire before the syndicate collapsed, after which she passed her legacy to the young Cadeliah and vanished into hiding. She is known for her cunning, her survival instinct, and her tragic history with Han Solo.
timeline:
~31 BBY - Birth on Corellia

Qi'ra is born on the shipbuilding world of Corellia and grows up in the impoverished, dangerous streets of Coronet City.

~13 BBY - Life as a White Worms scrumrat

She becomes a scrumrat in Lady Proxima's White Worms gang, rising to the rank of Head Girl. During this time she and fellow orphan Han Solo become close and fall in love.

~10 BBY - Escape attempt and separation from Han

Han and Qi'ra try to flee Corellia using a stolen vial of coaxium, but they are separated at the Coronet Spaceport. Han escapes offworld, promising to return for her, while Qi'ra is captured.

~10 BBY - Sold into Crimson Dawn

Passed through slavery, Qi'ra eventually enters the service of Crimson Dawn and becomes the trusted lieutenant of Dryden Vos, the syndicate's public face.

~10 BBY - The Kessel Run and reunion with Han

Qi'ra reunites with Han during a coaxium heist. When Vos is exposed as a traitor to the syndicate, she kills him and reveals her allegiance to Crimson Dawn's true master. She parts ways with Han, keeping his ship and choosing her own path in the underworld.

~10 BBY - Answering to Maul

Qi'ra makes contact with Maul, the shadowy leader of Crimson Dawn, and departs for Dathomir to join him, cementing her rise within the organization.

~3 ABY - Leader of Crimson Dawn

Following Maul's death, Qi'ra takes control of Crimson Dawn as its ruling "Lady," orchestrating the War of the Bounty Hunters and launching a covert campaign against the Sith and the Galactic Empire.

~3 ABY - Collapse of Crimson Dawn and hiding

Her scheme to strike at Emperor Palpatine unravels when the Knights of Ren turn against her, and Crimson Dawn falls apart. Qi'ra bequeaths her remaining resources to the young Cadeliah and disappears into hiding, her ultimate fate left unrecorded.

title: Mando
description: Din Djarin, known to most simply as "the Mandalorian" or "Mando," was a human bounty hunter of the New Republic Era, recognizable by his beskar armor, IB-94 blaster pistol, Amban phase-pulse rifle, and the helmet he vowed never to remove. Orphaned as a child on Aq Vetina when Separatist battle droids overran his village during the Clone Wars, he was rescued by Mandalorians and raised on Concordia as a foundling of the Children of the Watch, an orthodox splinter sect whose creed he took to be the way of all Mandalorians. By 9 ABY he was a hardened hunter working out of the covert on Nevarro, where a commission from an Imperial remnant led him to a Force-sensitive infant of Yoda's species — later known as Grogu — whom he refused to hand over, becoming a fugitive and the child's adoptive father. His search for the Jedi took him across the galaxy alongside allies including Greef Karga, Cara Dune, Bo-Katan Kryze, Ahsoka Tano, Boba Fett, and Fennec Shand, and culminated in Grogu's rescue from Moff Gideon and the child's brief tutelage under Luke Skywalker. In defeating Gideon he unknowingly won the Darksaber, and though he ceded its symbolic claim to Bo-Katan Kryze, he helped unite the fractured Mandalorian clans, redeem himself in the Living Waters of Mandalore, and lead the reconquest of their homeworld — afterward settling on Nevarro as a New Republic marshal with Grogu, whom he formally adopted as his apprentice.
timeline:
~30 BBY - Birth on Aq Vetina

Din Djarin is born to human parents in a village on the Outer Rim world of Aq Vetina during the final years of the Galactic Republic. Canon never states the year; it is inferred from his apparent age during the Clone Wars-era attack on his home.

~22 BBY - Separatist droids destroy his village

During the Clone Wars, Separatist battle droids overrun Aq Vetina. Din's parents hide him in a cellar and are killed above him; a super battle droid finds the boy but he is saved at the last moment by armored Mandalorians who descend from the sky.

~22 BBY - Taken in as a foundling

Rescued and carried away, Din is brought to Concordia and raised as a foundling by the Children of the Watch, an orthodox splinter sect. He is trained in the Mandalorian fighting arts and swears the Creed, vowing never to remove his helmet before another living being.

~19 BBY-9 ABY - Life in the Tribe

Djarin comes of age within the Watch, eventually operating from a hidden covert on Nevarro. He becomes a bounty hunter with the Bounty Hunters' Guild under Greef Karga, flying the gunship Razor Crest and returning a share of his earnings to the covert.

9 ABY - The commission for the Child

Hired on Nevarro by an Imperial remnant client and Doctor Pershing, Djarin tracks a fifty-year-old asset to Arvala-7, where with the help of the Ugnaught Kuiil and the reprogrammed droid IG-11 he finds an infant of Yoda's species.

9 ABY - Rescue of the Child and flight from Nevarro

After delivering the Child and receiving a camtono of beskar, Djarin turns on his employers and steals the infant back. His covert is exposed defending his escape, and he becomes a hunted man across the Outer Rim.

9 ABY - On the run

Djarin hides on Sorgan, is ambushed by fellow hunters, is betrayed during a heist on an Imperial prison ship, and is nearly killed by the mercenary Fennec Shand's associates on Tatooine — all while keeping the Child alive.

9 ABY - The battle for Nevarro

Returning to Nevarro at Karga's request, Djarin is drawn into a trap by Moff Gideon. Kuiil is killed and IG-11 sacrifices itself, removing Djarin's helmet to heal him — the first time anyone sees his face since he took the Creed. He is charged by the Armorer with reuniting the Child with its own kind.

9 ABY - Searching for the Jedi

Djarin's quest takes him to Tatooine, to Trask where he meets Bo-Katan Kryze and learns the Children of the Watch are a cult rather than mainstream Mandalorians, and to Corvus, where Ahsoka Tano tells him the Child's name is Grogu and sends him to the seeing stone on Tython.

9 ABY - Tython and the loss of the Razor Crest

At the ruins on Tython, Grogu reaches out through the Force while Djarin fends off Boba Fett and Fennec Shand. Moff Gideon's dark troopers seize Grogu and Gideon's cruiser destroys the Razor Crest, leaving Djarin without his ship.

9 ABY - Rescue of Grogu and the Darksaber

With Fett, Shand, Kryze, Koska Reeves and Cara Dune, Djarin boards Gideon's cruiser, destroys the dark troopers, and defeats Gideon in single combat — unknowingly winning the Darksaber and its claim to rule Mandalore. Luke Skywalker arrives to take Grogu for training, and Djarin removes his helmet to say goodbye.

9 ABY - The battle of Mos Espa

Recruited by Boba Fett, Djarin fights alongside him against the Pyke Syndicate on Tatooine. He acquires a Naboo N-1 starfighter to replace the Razor Crest, is exiled by the Armorer for having bared his face, and is reunited with Grogu, who chooses him over the Jedi path.

9 ABY - Redemption in the Living Waters

Seeking atonement, Djarin travels to the ruins of Mandalore and descends into the mines beneath the Civic Center. He bathes in the Living Waters and is pulled out by Bo-Katan Kryze, who in doing so sees the mythosaur below; both are declared redeemed by the Armorer.

9 ABY - Uniting the clans

Djarin and Kryze bring the Children of the Watch and the exiled Nite Owls together. Djarin gives the Darksaber to Kryze, telling the covert she recovered it from an enemy and so returned it freely — clearing the way for her to lead.

9 ABY - The retaking of Mandalore

The united clans invade Mandalore, are ambushed by Moff Gideon's remnant and his beskar-armored praetorian guard, and fight their way through. Gideon is killed, Mandalore is reclaimed for resettlement, and Djarin's ally Paz Vizsla falls in the battle.

9 ABY - Marshal of Nevarro

Djarin takes a commission as an independent contractor for the New Republic under Captain Carson Teva, formally adopts Grogu as his apprentice — "Din Grogu" — and settles into a cabin on Nevarro, taking bounty work as it comes.

events.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story

title: Chopper
description: C1-10P, better known as "Chopper" or simply "Chop," is a cantankerous masculine C1-series astromech droid built by Industrial Automaton and one of the longest-serving members of the Ghost crew. Originally a navigational astromech in the Galactic Republic Navy during the Clone Wars, he was shot down over Ryloth in a Y-wing and salvaged by a young Hera Syndulla, who rebuilt him from mismatched parts — the origin of his patchwork frame and famously abrasive personality. Chopper aided the Free Ryloth movement, then became Hera's inseparable companion aboard the VCX-100 light freighter Ghost, maintaining the ship and serving as gunner, slicer, saboteur and reluctant undercover agent for the Spectres rebel cell alongside Kanan Jarrus, Ezra Bridger, Garazeb Orrelios and Sabine Wren. Despite a well-earned reputation for pranks, grumbling and outright malice toward strangers, he proved decisive again and again — infiltrating Imperial facilities, flying stolen craft, and surviving the rebellion's hardest losses. The Rebel Alliance's Atollon headquarters, Chopper Base, was named in his honor. He fought through the liberation of Lothal, was present at Yavin 4 during the Scarif crisis and at the Battle of Endor, later joined Sabine Wren's long search for the missing Ezra Bridger, and remained at Hera's side into the New Republic era.
timeline:
~22 BBY - Built by Industrial Automaton

C1-10P is manufactured as a C1-series astromech droid; canon never states his construction date, only that he was in service by the Clone Wars.

~22 BBY - Service with the Republic Navy

Chopper serves as a navigational astromech aboard Republic starfighters during the Clone Wars.

~20 BBY - Shot down over Ryloth

His Y-wing is shot down during the fighting on Ryloth. Badly damaged and abandoned in the wreck, he is found by a young Hera Syndulla, who repairs him with whatever parts she can scavenge.

~20 BBY - Joins the Free Ryloth movement

Chopper becomes Hera's constant companion, assisting the Twi'lek resistance against Separatist and later Imperial occupation.

~19 BBY - Aiding Hera against the early Empire

In the first year of the Galactic Empire, Chopper helps Hera on Ryloth during operations against Imperial forces occupying the world, including efforts to free captured members of the Syndulla family.

~11 BBY - Crew of the Ghost

Chopper serves aboard Hera's VCX-100 light freighter Ghost as she meets Kanan Jarrus during the events on Gorse, becoming the ship's permanent — and permanently grumpy — maintenance droid.

5 BBY - The Spectres on Lothal

Chopper is part of the rebel cell that harasses Imperial operations on Lothal and takes in the young street thief Ezra Bridger, flying missions, slicing Imperial systems and pulling pranks on the crew throughout.

4 BBY - Phoenix Squadron

The Spectres join the wider rebel network; Chopper takes part in supply raids, rescues and infiltrations, often disguised or posing as an Imperial droid.

3 BBY - Chopper Base

The rebels establish a headquarters on Atollon, nicknamed Chopper Base in his honor after he helps clear the site. He survives the Empire's devastating assault there.

2 BBY - Undercover and in the field

Chopper repeatedly infiltrates Imperial installations, including a stint aboard an Imperial vessel where his rough manners nearly expose the crew.

1 BBY - Liberation of Lothal

Chopper takes part in the final campaign to free Lothal, aboard the Ghost as Ezra Bridger vanishes into hyperspace with Grand Admiral Thrawn and the purrgil.

0 BBY - Yavin 4

Chopper is present at the Rebel Alliance's Massassi base on Yavin 4 during the crisis that leads to the theft of the Death Star plans at Scarif.

4 ABY - Battle of Endor

Chopper serves aboard the Ghost as the Alliance fleet destroys the second Death Star and breaks the Empire.

~5 ABY - Searching for Ezra

After the war, Chopper accompanies Sabine Wren as she sets out to find the missing Ezra Bridger.

~9 ABY - The New Republic era

Still at Hera Syndulla's side, now a New Republic general, Chopper assists in the effort to track Thrawn's return and recover Ezra, remaining active and unmistakably cantankerous.

title: Reva
description:
"

Reva Sevander, known as the Third Sister, was a Force-sensitive human female who served the Galactic Empire as a member of the Inquisitorius during the early Imperial Era. Raised as a Jedi initiate in the Jedi Temple on Coruscant, she was one of the few younglings to survive the Temple's fall during Order 66, playing dead after Darth Vader cut down her clan, and she spent the following decade consumed by a desire for revenge against him. Joining the Inquisitorius under Vader's own command, she rose as the ruthless and openly ambitious Third Sister, hunting surviving Jedi while secretly plotting to get close enough to kill her master. In 9 BBY she orchestrated the abduction of Princess Leia Organa to draw the hiding Obi-Wan Kenobi out of exile, struck down the Grand Inquisitor to claim his title, and pursued Kenobi from Daiyu to Mapuzo and Jabiim. When she finally revealed her true intent to Vader, he overpowered her and ran her through with her own lightsaber, but she survived; sent afterward to kill Kenobi's secret charge Luke Skywalker on Tatooine, she found herself unable to murder a child as she had once nearly been murdered, returned the boy to his family, and abandoned the dark side. Her fate beyond that moment is unrecorded in canon.

"
timeline:
"

~31 BBY - Birth

Reva Sevander is born a Force-sensitive human female. Canon never names her birth world, and the year is an approximation based on her apparent age during the Great Jedi Purge.

~31 BBY - Taken into the Jedi Order

Identified as Force-sensitive in infancy, she is brought to the Jedi Temple on Coruscant and raised among the Order's youngest members.

~24 BBY - Training as a Jedi initiate

Reva trains with her clan of younglings in the Temple, studying under Jedi Master Minas Velti alongside friends who would not survive the coming war.

19 BBY - Order 66 and the fall of the Jedi Temple

As the Clone Wars end, Darth Vader and the 501st Legion storm the Jedi Temple. Master Velti is gunned down by clone troopers, and Vader slaughters Reva's clan, stabbing her in the process. She survives by playing dead among the bodies and swears revenge on the man who was Anakin Skywalker.

~19 BBY - Falling to the dark side

Orphaned by the Purge and driven by hatred, Reva turns from the Jedi teachings she was raised on and sets herself on a path toward the very Empire that destroyed her home.

~14 BBY - Joining the Inquisitorius

She is recruited into the Inquisitorius, the Empire's order of dark side Jedi hunters, taking the designation Third Sister and serving directly under Darth Vader — the only position from which she believes she can one day kill him.

9 BBY - The hunt on Tatooine

The Third Sister arrives on Tatooine with the Grand Inquisitor and the Fifth Brother, tracking the fugitive Jedi Nari. Her open aggression and obsession with finding Obi-Wan Kenobi draw the Grand Inquisitor's contempt.

9 BBY - The abduction of Leia Organa

Reva secretly arranges for bounty hunters to kidnap Princess Leia Organa from Alderaan, correctly calculating that the child's connection to Kenobi will force him out of a decade of hiding.

9 BBY - Confrontation on Daiyu

Kenobi surfaces on Daiyu to recover Leia. Reva pursues them through the city, revealing to Obi-Wan that a youngling survived the Temple and that she knows what he did — or failed to do.

9 BBY - Claiming the title of Grand Inquisitor

Confronted over her unsanctioned scheme, Reva strikes down the Grand Inquisitor with her lightsaber and, once her plan delivers Kenobi to Vader, is elevated to Grand Inquisitor in his place. Her predecessor, unknown to her, survives the wound.

9 BBY - Mapuzo and the Path

Reva hunts Kenobi and Leia across the mining world of Mapuzo, where Vader himself joins the pursuit, and later infiltrates the Path's hidden safehouse on Jabiim in her drive to capture the Jedi.

9 BBY - Fortress Inquisitorius and Jabiim

Kenobi's rescue mission into the Fortress Inquisitorius on Nur and the Empire's siege of the Path's refuge on Jabiim leave Reva increasingly isolated, her true motives close to the surface.

9 BBY - Revenge against Vader

Reva finally turns her blade on Darth Vader, declaring herself the youngling he left for dead. Vader overpowers her effortlessly and impales her with her own lightsaber, leaving her to die — but she survives a second time.

9 BBY - Sparing Luke Skywalker on Tatooine

Learning of Vader's hidden son, Reva travels to the Lars homestead on Tatooine and corners the boy Luke Skywalker. Standing over a helpless child with her blade raised, she sees herself in him and cannot strike, returning Luke to Owen and Beru Lars.

9 BBY - Abandoning the dark side

Reva casts aside her Inquisitor lightsaber in the Tatooine desert, telling Kenobi she has become what she hated and choosing to walk away. Canon records nothing of her life afterward, and she is not known to have died.

"

title: Hera Syndulla
description: Hera Syndulla is a Twi'lek revolutionary leader and ace pilot who became one of the founding figures of the rebellion against the Galactic Empire and the Alliance to Restore the Republic. Born on Ryloth to the resistance leader General Cham Syndulla and Eleni Syndulla, she witnessed the devastation of the Clone Wars and Imperial occupation firsthand before leaving her homeworld to build her own fight across the galaxy aboard her starship, the Ghost. As commander of the rebel cell known as the Spectres, she led a crew that included the fallen Jedi Kanan Jarrus (who became her partner and the father of her son), the Mandalorian Sabine Wren, the Lasat Garazeb Orrelios, the astromech droid Chopper, and the Force-sensitive Padawan Ezra Bridger. She played key roles in the early insurgency on Lothal, the Battle of Atollon, and the liberation of Lothal, rising to the rank of general. Following the Empire's fall she continued to serve as a general in the New Republic, raising her son Jacen and searching for the missing Ezra Bridger, events central to the Ahsoka series.
timeline:
29 BBY - Birth on Ryloth

Hera Syndulla is born on the Twi'lek homeworld of Ryloth to Cham Syndulla, a freedom fighter, and his wife Eleni.

~19 BBY - Growing up amid the Clone Wars

As a child during the Clone Wars, Hera experiences the Separatist occupation of Ryloth and her father's resistance movement, developing her lifelong drive to fight oppression and forming a bond with the droid Chopper.

~11 BBY - Meeting Kanan Jarrus

During the Gorse conflict, Hera crosses paths with Kanan Jarrus, a Jedi who survived Order 66; he later joins her crew aboard the Ghost and becomes her partner.

~5 BBY - The Spectres on Lothal

Leading her rebel cell from the Ghost, Hera recruits the street orphan Ezra Bridger and unites her crew — Kanan, Sabine Wren, Zeb Orrelios, and Chopper — into an insurgency against the Empire on Lothal.

~4 BBY - Joining the wider rebellion

Hera's cell links up with the growing rebel network under Phoenix Squadron and Commander Sato, and she reunites with her father Cham during a mission on Ryloth.

~3 BBY - Battle of Atollon

Hera fights in the disastrous Battle of Atollon against Grand Admiral Thrawn's forces, escaping with a portion of the rebel fleet.

~2 BBY - Promotion and the Liberation of Lothal

Now a rebel general, Hera leads the campaign to free Lothal. During the fighting Kanan Jarrus sacrifices himself to save her and the crew, and Ezra Bridger vanishes into hyperspace with Thrawn.

~1 BBY - Birth of Jacen Syndulla

Hera gives birth to her and Kanan's son, Jacen Syndulla, who later shows an aptitude for the Force.

0 BBY–0 ABY - Galactic Civil War

Hera continues to serve the Alliance to Restore the Republic as a pilot and general through the era of the Battle of Yavin.

4 ABY - Battle of Endor

General Syndulla participates in the Rebel Alliance's decisive victory over the Empire at Endor.

~9 ABY - New Republic general

Serving as a general in the New Republic, Hera raises Jacen, works to convince the Senate of the Imperial remnant and Thrawn threat, and joins Ahsoka Tano and Sabine Wren in the search for the missing Ezra Bridger during the events of the Ahsoka series.

events.title: Episode II: Attack of the Clones

title: R5-D4
description: R5-D4, nicknamed "Red" by Luke Skywalker, is a red-and-white R5-series astromech droid built by Industrial Automaton — one of the last units off a budget production line notorious for malfunctions. Active on Tatooine as early as the era of the Clone Wars, when he was glimpsed rolling through the streets of Mos Espa, he later fell into the hands of a band of Jawas who wiped his memory and hawked him from their sandcrawler. His moment of galactic consequence came shortly before the Battle of Yavin: offered for sale to Owen Lars alongside R2-D2, R5-D4 blew his own motivator so that Lars would take R2-D2 instead, ensuring the astromech carrying the stolen Death Star plans reached Luke Skywalker. R5 survived the Imperial raid that destroyed the sandcrawler and its Jawa crew, and was still operational decades later in the New Republic era, working out of Peli Motto's hangar in Mos Eisley, where he projected a holomap of Tatooine for the Mandalorian Din Djarin. Motto later sold him to Djarin outright, and R5 accompanied the Mandalorian to Mandalore, surviving an Alamite ambush to confirm the ruined planet's atmosphere was breathable — a scan that opened the way for the Mandalorians' return home.
timeline:
Unknown date - Manufacture by Industrial Automaton

R5-D4 is produced as one of the last units of the R5-series astromech line, a low-cost design plagued by defects and poor customer reception. Canon does not give a construction year.

~22 BBY - Seen in the streets of Mos Espa

R5-D4, then serving a master on Tatooine, rolls through Mos Espa around the time Anakin Skywalker returns to the planet in search of his mother.

~4 BBY - Acquired by Jawas and memory-wiped

The droid falls into the possession of a Jawa scavenger crew, who wipe his memory and press him into service as salvage stock aboard their sandcrawler.

0 BBY - The blown motivator

Jawas offer R5-D4 to Owen Lars at the Lars moisture farm. On R2-D2's advice, R5 deliberately blows his own motivator, causing Lars to buy R2-D2 — and the Death Star plans hidden in his memory — instead.

0 BBY - Survives the sandcrawler massacre

Imperial stormtroopers hunting the missing droids destroy the sandcrawler and slaughter its Jawa crew. R5-D4 survives the attack.

9 ABY - In service at Peli Motto's hangar

Now working for Mos Eisley hangar manager Peli Motto, R5-D4 projects a holographic map of Tatooine to help Din Djarin locate the Tusken Raiders and a mudhorn-hunting job.

9 ABY - Continued work in Mos Eisley

R5 remains part of Motto's hangar operation during Boba Fett's rise on Tatooine, assisting with repairs and errands around Docking Bay 3-5.

9 ABY - Sold to Din Djarin

Motto sells the reluctant droid to Din Djarin — persuading him with the threat of being sold back to the Jawas — so the Mandalorian has an astromech for his journey to Mandalore.

9 ABY - Atmospheric survey of Mandalore

Sent out alone onto the ruined surface of Mandalore, R5 is attacked by an Alamite but escapes and confirms the atmosphere is breathable, allowing Djarin to explore the Mines of Mandalore and ultimately enabling the Mandalorians' return to their homeworld.

title: Rotta the Hutt
description: Rotta, commonly called Rotta the Hutt and nicknamed "Pedunkee Mufkin" by his father and "Stinky" by Ahsoka Tano, is a male Hutt and the son of the Tatooine crime lord Jabba Desilijic Tiure. Born shortly before the outbreak of the Clone Wars, he became a galactic flashpoint as an infant when Count Dooku and the Confederacy of Independent Systems — aided by his treacherous great-uncle Ziro the Hutt — arranged his abduction by Asajj Ventress, intending to frame the Jedi Order for the crime and wreck negotiations that would have opened Hutt-controlled hyperspace lanes to the Galactic Republic. Anakin Skywalker and his new Padawan Ahsoka Tano tracked the huttlet to a B'omarr monastery on Teth and fought their way out with him; Rotta fell gravely ill during the flight to Tatooine and survived largely because of Ahsoka's care. After the pair evaded Dooku's attempt to kill the child and pin it on the Jedi, Rotta was returned to his father, and the Hutts granted the Republic passage through their space. He was later seen at Jabba's Palace during Baron Notluwiski Papanoida's visit. Rotta survived his father's death above the Great Pit of Carkoon and, by the New Republic era, had grown into a heavily muscled arena gladiator, becoming the object of a rescue mission undertaken by Din Djarin and Grogu on the New Republic's behalf.
timeline:
~23 BBY - Birth

Rotta is born to Jabba Desilijic Tiure, the Hutt crime lord who rules Tatooine from his palace in the Dune Sea. Canon never states his birth year outright; it can only be placed sometime between 24 BBY and 22 BBY, shortly before the Clone Wars.

22 BBY - Abducted by the Separatists

In the opening months of the Clone Wars, the infant Rotta is kidnapped by Asajj Ventress on Count Dooku's orders, in a plot secretly aided by his great-uncle Ziro the Hutt. The Separatists intend to blame the Jedi and prevent an alliance between the Republic and the Hutt Clan.

22 BBY - Rescue on Teth

Jedi Knight Anakin Skywalker and his newly assigned Padawan Ahsoka Tano assault the B'omarr monastery on Teth and recover the huttlet, whom Ahsoka dubs "Stinky."

22 BBY - Illness aboard the Twilight

Rotta falls dangerously ill during the flight to Tatooine and nearly dies. Ahsoka's persistent care keeps him alive long enough to reach his father.

22 BBY - Returned to Jabba

After Anakin defeats Dooku's attempt to murder the child and frame the Jedi, and Padmé Amidala exposes Ziro's treachery, Rotta is delivered safely to Jabba's Palace. A grateful Jabba grants the Republic military access to Hutt Space hyperlanes.

~21 BBY - At his father's court

Rotta is present in Jabba's Palace when Baron Notluwiski Papanoida of Pantora comes seeking help in recovering his kidnapped daughters.

4 ABY - Death of Jabba

Jabba the Hutt is killed above the Great Pit of Carkoon during Luke Skywalker's rescue of Han Solo, and his criminal empire on Tatooine fragments. Rotta is not present and survives his father.

~9 ABY - Gladiator in the New Republic era

Now a grown, unusually muscular Hutt fighting as an arena gladiator, Rotta becomes the target of a New Republic-sanctioned rescue: Din Djarin and Grogu are sent to retrieve him in exchange for information from the Hutt Clan.

title: Sabine Wren
description: Sabine Wren is a human Mandalorian warrior, artist, and revolutionary from Clan Wren of House Vizsla, born in 21 BBY. A former cadet at the Imperial Academy on Mandalore, she defected after realizing the weapons she designed would be turned against her own people, and became "Spectre 5," the weapons and explosives expert of the Ghost crew during the early rebellion against the Galactic Empire. A gifted graffiti artist, her painted starbird came to inspire the symbol of the Alliance to Restore the Republic. She wielded the ancient Darksaber after taking it from Maul and trained with it under Kanan Jarrus despite having no notable Force sensitivity, ultimately surrendering it to Bo-Katan Kryze to unite the Mandalorian clans against Imperial rule. After the liberation of Lothal and the disappearance of Ezra Bridger and Grand Admiral Thrawn, she dedicated herself to finding her lost friend, later training as Ahsoka Tano's Padawan. In the Ahsoka era she helped track Thrawn's return and became stranded with Ahsoka in the distant galaxy of Peridea.
timeline:
21 BBY - Birth

Sabine Wren is born into Clan Wren, a Mandalorian family aligned with House Vizsla, with ties to the ancestral homeworld of Krownest.

~7 BBY - Imperial Academy cadet on Mandalore

As a teenager, Sabine trains at the Imperial Academy on Mandalore, where her talents as a weapons designer are recognized.

~6 BBY - Defection from the Empire

Realizing that the Imperial weapons she helped develop are meant to be used against fellow Mandalorians, Sabine defects, becoming estranged from her family and a target of the Empire.

~5 BBY - Joins the Ghost crew

Sabine joins the rebel cell aboard the Ghost as "Spectre 5," serving as its explosives and weapons expert and painting her art across their operations.

~4 BBY - Rise of the rebellion on Lothal

Sabine fights alongside the crew against Imperial forces, and her starbird artwork becomes an emblem of hope for the growing rebellion.

~3 BBY - Claiming the Darksaber

Sabine recovers the ancient Darksaber and, though not Force-sensitive, undertakes lightsaber training with Kanan Jarrus, embracing her role as a symbol for uniting Mandalore.

~3 BBY - Reunion with Clan Wren

Sabine returns to Krownest and helps free her father and reconcile with her family, rallying Clan Wren against the Empire's rule of Mandalore.

~2 BBY - Surrendering the Darksaber

To unite the Mandalorian clans, Sabine passes the Darksaber to Bo-Katan Kryze, affirming Bo-Katan's claim to lead their people.

~1 BBY - Liberation of Lothal

Sabine takes part in the final battle to free Lothal, during which Ezra Bridger vanishes into hyperspace with Grand Admiral Thrawn.

~5 ABY - The search for Ezra begins

After the fall of the Empire, Sabine sets out with Ahsoka Tano to find the missing Ezra Bridger, later becoming Ahsoka's Padawan.

~9 ABY - Hunt for Thrawn in the Ahsoka era

Sabine and Ahsoka investigate rumors of Thrawn's survival. After clashing with mercenaries Baylan Skoll and Shin Hati, Sabine helps reach the distant galaxy of Peridea, reunites with Ezra, and becomes stranded there with Ahsoka.

events.title: Episode VI: Return of the Jedi

title: Max Rebo
description: Maximilian "Max" Rebo was a male Ortolan from the cold world of Orto and one of the galaxy's best-known jizz-wailer musicians, famous as the leader of the Max Rebo Band. Short, blue-skinned and long-snouted, he was instantly recognizable behind his red ball jett organ, an instrument he played with his feet rather than his hands. Rebo and his band made a career of playing for criminals and gangsters, most notoriously as the house act of the Hutt crime lord Jabba Desilijic Tiure, performing in Jabba's Tatooine palace and aboard his sail barge in 4 ABY — where the band's number "Jedi Rocks" accompanied the dancer Oola's death in the rancor pit and Jabba's capture of Leia Organa. Rebo survived the destruction of the sail barge over the Great Pit of Carkoon, and by 9 ABY he had found steadier and safer work on Tatooine as the resident musician at Garsa Fwip's cantina, the Sanctuary in Mos Espa, still playing a red ball jett organ when the newly installed Daimyo Boba Fett came calling. His fate after the Sanctuary's destruction is not established in canon.
timeline:
Unknown date - Birth on Orto

Maximilian Rebo was born a member of the Ortolan species on the frigid planet Orto. Canon does not record the year of his birth, nor his age at any later point.

Unknown date - Rise as a jizz-wailer

Rebo became a popular musician in the jizz genre and formed the Max Rebo Band, taking the red ball jett organ as his signature instrument and playing its keys with his feet. The band built its reputation performing for criminals, gangsters and crime lords.

By 4 ABY - House band for Jabba the Hutt

Rebo's band took up residence in the Tatooine palace of the Hutt crime lord Jabba Desilijic Tiure, becoming the court's entertainment.

4 ABY - Performing in Jabba's palace

The Max Rebo Band played for Jabba's court as the Twi'lek dancer Oola was dropped into the rancor pit, and were still performing when Jabba sprang his trap on Leia Organa, who had infiltrated the palace disguised as the bounty hunter Boushh to free Han Solo from carbonite.

4 ABY - Aboard the sail barge and survival

Rebo and his band accompanied Jabba aboard the sail barge Khetanna on its journey to the Great Pit of Carkoon for the execution of Luke Skywalker, Han Solo and Chewbacca. The barge was destroyed in the ensuing fight, but Rebo survived.

9 ABY - Playing the Sanctuary in Mos Espa

Rebo was employed as the musician at the Sanctuary, the Mos Espa cantina run by the Twi'lek Garsa Fwip, where he performed on a newer model of red ball jett organ. He was playing there on more than one occasion when the former bounty hunter and new Daimyo of Mos Espa, Boba Fett, visited the establishment.

After 9 ABY - Fate unrecorded

No canon source records Rebo's death or his whereabouts after his appearances in Mos Espa; he is presumed alive.
