title: Luke Skywalker
description: Luke Skywalker was a legendary human Jedi Master, hero of the Rebel Alliance, and a central figure in the Galactic Civil War against the Galactic Empire. Born in 19 BBY as the son of Anakin Skywalker (Darth Vader) and Padmé Amidala, and the twin brother of Leia Organa, he was raised in obscurity as a moisture farmer on Tatooine. Drawn into the conflict after acquiring the droids R2-D2 and C-3PO, he trained under Obi-Wan Kenobi and Yoda, destroyed the first Death Star at the Battle of Yavin, confronted his father, and ultimately redeemed Anakin at the Battle of Endor, bringing about the Emperor's downfall. In the decades that followed he sought to restore the Jedi Order, but the fall of his nephew Ben Solo to the dark side drove him into self-imposed exile on Ahch-To. He re-emerged during the war against the First Order, aiding the Resistance at Crait through an extraordinary Force projection before becoming one with the Force in 34 ABY. Remembered as one of the greatest Jedi in galactic history, his legend endured and later inspired a new generation.
timeline:
19 BBY - Birth on Polis Massa

Luke is born to Padmé Amidala and Anakin Skywalker shortly after the end of the Clone Wars, moments after his twin sister Leia. Separated from his sister to hide them from their fallen father, he is taken by Obi-Wan Kenobi to Tatooine.

19 BBY - Raised on Tatooine

Luke is placed in the care of his step-relatives Owen and Beru Lars, growing up as a moisture farmer while Obi-Wan watches over him from a distance.

0 BBY - Meeting the droids and Obi-Wan

Acquiring R2-D2 and C-3PO, Luke discovers Leia's plea for help, meets Obi-Wan Kenobi, receives his father's lightsaber, and leaves Tatooine after the Empire murders his aunt and uncle.

0 BBY - Destruction of the Death Star

Aided by the Force and Han Solo, Luke fires the shot that destroys the first Death Star at the Battle of Yavin, becoming a hero of the Rebel Alliance.

3 ABY - Training with Yoda

After the Rebel defeat on Hoth, Luke travels to Dagobah to train as a Jedi under Master Yoda. At Cloud City he duels Darth Vader, loses his hand, and learns that Vader is his father.

4 ABY - Redemption of Anakin at Endor

Luke surrenders to Vader and confronts Emperor Palpatine aboard the second Death Star. Refusing the dark side, he turns his father back to the light; Anakin destroys the Emperor, and Luke witnesses the Rebel victory at the Battle of Endor.

~28 ABY - Rebuilding the Jedi Order

In the years after the war, Luke works to restore the Jedi, training a new generation of apprentices, including his nephew Ben Solo.

~28 ABY - Fall of the temple and exile

After a moment of fear and Ben's turn to the dark side, Luke's temple is destroyed and his students scattered. Blaming himself, Luke exiles himself to the ancient Jedi world of Ahch-To.

34 ABY - Rey and the last lesson

Rey arrives on Ahch-To seeking Luke's help. Reluctant at first, he is eventually persuaded by Yoda's spirit to pass on the Jedi legacy.

34 ABY - Sacrifice at Crait and death

Luke projects himself across the galaxy to confront Kylo Ren and buy time for the Resistance to escape Crait. The immense effort drains him, and he peacefully becomes one with the Force on Ahch-To.

35 ABY - Return as a Force spirit

Luke appears to Rey as a Force ghost, lifting his old X-wing from the sea and encouraging her to face Palpatine, aiding the final victory over the Sith.

title: Leia Organa
description: Leia Skywalker Organa Solo was a princess, senator, revolutionary, and military leader who became one of the most pivotal figures in the fight against tyranny across three galactic conflicts. The biological daughter of Padmé Amidala and Anakin Skywalker (later Darth Vader) and twin sister of Luke Skywalker, she was born in 19 BBY and adopted at birth by Senator Bail Organa and Queen Breha Organa of Alderaan, becoming Princess Leia of the Core World's royal House of Organa. Serving in the Imperial Senate as a cover for her work with the growing rebellion, she was captured while carrying the stolen Death Star plans, witnessed the Empire destroy Alderaan, and was rescued in the events of A New Hope. She became a leading figure of the Rebel Alliance, helped bring down the Empire at the Battle of Endor, and married the smuggler Han Solo, with whom she had a son, Ben. In the New Republic era she founded and led the Resistance against the First Order as General Organa, and though a trained but largely unfulfilled Force-sensitive, she passed her Jedi knowledge to Rey before dying in 35 ABY on Ajan Kloss, becoming one with the Force as she reached out to her fallen son.
timeline:
19 BBY - Birth on Polis Massa

Leia is born alongside her twin brother Luke to Padmé Amidala, who dies shortly after childbirth. To hide the children from Darth Vader and the Emperor, Leia is adopted by Senator Bail Organa and Queen Breha Organa of Alderaan.

~10 BBY - Childhood adventure with Obi-Wan Kenobi

As a headstrong young princess, Leia is kidnapped and rescued by Obi-Wan Kenobi, forging an early connection with the exiled Jedi Master during the events of the Obi-Wan Kenobi series.

~5 BBY - Secret work for the rebellion

Following in her adoptive father's footsteps, Leia becomes a member of the Imperial Senate while covertly aiding the growing rebel movement against the Galactic Empire.

0 BBY - The Death Star plans and the fall of Alderaan

Entrusted with the stolen Death Star plans, Leia is captured by Darth Vader aboard the Tantive IV. She is forced to watch the Empire destroy Alderaan, then is rescued from the Death Star by Luke Skywalker, Han Solo, and Obi-Wan Kenobi in A New Hope.

3 ABY - Escape from Hoth and capture at Cloud City

After the Empire overruns Echo Base, Leia flees with Han Solo, and the two fall in love before being betrayed at Cloud City, where Han is frozen in carbonite, as depicted in The Empire Strikes Back.

4 ABY - Victory at Endor

Leia rescues Han from Jabba the Hutt, learns that Luke is her twin brother and Vader her father, and helps the Rebel Alliance destroy the second Death Star at the Battle of Endor in Return of the Jedi.

~5 ABY - Marriage to Han Solo

In the aftermath of the Empire's defeat, Leia continues to build the New Republic and marries Han Solo.

~5 ABY - Birth of Ben Solo

Leia and Han have a son, Ben, who is later trained by Luke before falling to the dark side as Kylo Ren.

~28 ABY - Founding the Resistance

Disillusioned with the New Republic's complacency toward the rising First Order, General Leia Organa founds and leads the Resistance.

34 ABY - The war against the First Order

Leia leads the Resistance through the destruction of the Hosnian system and the Starkiller Base crisis, survives an attack in the vacuum of space using the Force, and guides the battered remnants of her fleet to survival during the events of The Force Awakens and The Last Jedi.

35 ABY - Death on Ajan Kloss

Leia completes Rey's Jedi training and, in a final act, reaches out through the Force to her son Ben in an effort to turn him from the dark side. The effort costs her life, and she becomes one with the Force during the events of The Rise of Skywalker.

deathEvent.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

title: Ezra Bridger
description: Ezra Bridger is a human Force-sensitive from the planet Lothal who became a Jedi Padawan, con artist turned freedom fighter, and revolutionary leader in the early rebellion against the Galactic Empire. Born on Empire Day in 19 BBY to Ephraim and Mira Bridger, he was orphaned as a boy after his parents were imprisoned for broadcasting anti-Imperial messages, and survived alone on Lothal's streets. In 5 BBY he crossed paths with the rebel crew of the starship Ghost and was taken in and trained in the ways of the Force by Kanan Jarrus. Bridger's abilities grew rapidly, and his broadcast message of hope helped unite scattered rebel cells. He is best known for his central role in Star Wars Rebels, culminating in the liberation of Lothal, when he sacrificed his freedom to defeat Grand Admiral Thrawn by having purrgil pull Thrawn's flagship — with Ezra aboard — into hyperspace and out of the galaxy. Long presumed lost, he was revealed alive on the distant world of Peridea and returned to the known galaxy in the New Republic era during the Ahsoka series.
timeline:
19 BBY - Birth on Lothal (Empire Day)

Ezra Bridger is born on Lothal to Ephraim and Mira Bridger on the very day the Galactic Empire is proclaimed, making his birthday coincide with Empire Day.

~12 BBY - Orphaned

His parents are arrested and imprisoned by the Empire for broadcasting messages criticizing Imperial rule, leaving the young Ezra to fend for himself as a street orphan on Lothal.

5 BBY - Joining the Ghost crew

Ezra encounters the rebel crew of the Ghost during a raid on Imperial cargo and joins them, beginning his training in the Force under Kanan Jarrus (events of the Star Wars Rebels pilot, "Spark of Rebellion").

~4 BBY - Growth as a Padawan

Ezra deepens his Jedi training, acquires and later loses his first lightsaber, and encounters figures including Ahsoka Tano and remnants of the Jedi Order as the rebel cell fights the Empire.

~3 BBY - Facing the dark side

Ezra explores a Sith holocron and dark-side temptations while the crew's operations expand, drawing the attention of Imperial forces including the Inquisitors.

2 BBY - Message of hope and the Battle of Atollon

Ezra broadcasts a message of hope across the Lothal sector that helps inspire disparate rebel cells to unite, and the growing rebellion survives Grand Admiral Thrawn's assault on Chopper Base at Atollon.

1 BBY - Liberation of Lothal

In the Rebels finale, Ezra helps free his homeworld and defeats Thrawn by summoning purrgil that seize Thrawn's Star Destroyer and jump into hyperspace, carrying both Ezra and Thrawn far beyond the known galaxy.

~5 ABY - Presumed lost, search begins

After the Battle of Endor, an epilogue shows Ezra's comrades — chiefly Sabine Wren — setting out to find him, as his fate remains unknown to the galaxy.

~9 ABY - Found on Peridea and return (Ahsoka)

Ezra is revealed alive on the extragalactic world of Peridea alongside Thrawn; with the help of Sabine Wren and Ahsoka Tano he escapes and returns to the known galaxy during the New Republic era.

events.title: Episode VI: Return of the Jedi

title: 8D8
description: 8D8 was a masculine-programmed 8D-series smelter droid manufactured by the Roche Hive, originally built for ore processing before being lobotomized and reprogrammed into a torture-chamber assistant. In that role he served EV-9D9, the chief of Cyborg Operations in Jabba Desilijic Tiure's palace on Tatooine, where he helped brand, mutilate and terrorize the droids pressed into the Hutt's service — a role in which he was glimpsed heating a branding iron over a droid strapped to a rack when C-3PO and R2-D2 were processed in 4 ABY. Deactivated along with the rest of the palace's droid pool after Jabba's death above the Great Pit of Carkoon, 8D8 was reactivated years later by Boba Fett, who took over the palace and installed the droid as his majordomo. In that capacity 8D8 announced visiting dignitaries and tribute-bearers, translated for Fett in negotiations with Mos Espa's crime families and Hutt rivals, coordinated the daimyo's day-to-day affairs, and stood with Fett's people through the war against the Pyke Syndicate. Long-suffering and dryly acerbic, he habitually urged Fett to rule through fear and intimidation rather than the reciprocal loyalty his master preferred, and he retained an open resentment of the more sophisticated protocol and astromech droids he had once tormented.
timeline:
Date unknown - Manufacture on Roche

8D8 was built by the Roche Hive as an 8D-series smelter droid, a 1.83-meter industrial unit intended for ore processing. Canon gives no construction date.

Date unknown - Lobotomized and repurposed

At some point before Jabba the Hutt's death, 8D8 was lobotomized and reprogrammed to serve EV-9D9 in the droid torture chamber beneath Jabba's palace on Tatooine, terrorizing and mutilating the palace's droid pool.

4 ABY - Processing C-3PO and R2-D2

When Luke Skywalker's droids were delivered to Jabba's palace, 8D8 worked alongside EV-9D9 in the torture chamber, branding the feet of a screaming Power droid as the newcomers were assigned their duties.

4 ABY - Deactivation after Jabba's death

Following Jabba's death at the Great Pit of Carkoon and the collapse of his criminal empire, the palace droids — including 8D8 — were shut down and left dormant.

9 ABY - Reactivated by Boba Fett

After Boba Fett killed Bib Fortuna and claimed the palace and the title of daimyo of Mos Espa, 8D8 was reactivated and made Fett's majordomo, announcing visitors and presenting tribute alongside Fennec Shand.

9 ABY - Serving the daimyo

8D8 translated for Fett in dealings with the Hutt twins, the mayor of Mos Espa's majordomo and the heads of the city's crime families, and repeatedly counseled his master that a crime lord must rule through fear rather than respect.

9 ABY - The war for Mos Espa

8D8 remained at Fett's side through the escalating conflict with the Pyke Syndicate, coordinating the daimyo's household and forces as fighting broke out in the streets of Mos Espa.

After 9 ABY - Continued service

8D8 survives the events of the war; canon records no destruction or deactivation, and his fate beyond this point is unknown.

events.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

title: Temmin Wexley
description: Temmin "Snap" Wexley was a human male pilot born in the city of Myrra on the planet Akiva. As a child he was separated from his parents when his father Brentin was seized by the Galactic Empire and his mother Norra left to fight for the Rebel Alliance, leaving him in the care of his aunt. A gifted mechanic, he became an independent junk dealer and built a modified B1 battle droid, Mister Bones, as a bodyguard and companion. After the Battle of Endor he reunited with his mother and helped foil a secret Imperial gathering on Akiva. He went on to train as a pilot, gained Wedge Antilles as a stepfather, and flew at the Battle of Jakku. Decades later he became a founding pilot of the Resistance, serving in Black Squadron under Poe Dameron and marrying fellow pilot Karé Kun. He fought at the destruction of Starkiller Base and was ultimately shot down and killed during the Battle of Exegol.
timeline:
11 BBY - Birth on Akiva

Temmin Wexley is born in the city of Myrra on Akiva to Brentin and Norra Wexley.

~4 ABY - Separated from his parents

His father Brentin is taken by the Galactic Empire and his mother Norra leaves to join the Rebel Alliance, leaving young Temmin in the care of his aunt. He becomes an independent junk dealer and builds the battle droid Mister Bones.

4 ABY - Reunion on Akiva

Following the Battle of Endor, the teenage Temmin reconciles with his mother Norra and joins her to disrupt a secret Imperial meeting on Akiva.

~4 ABY - A new family

Temmin begins training as a pilot, and his mother's relationship with ace Wedge Antilles eventually makes Antilles his stepfather.

5 ABY - Battle of Jakku

Temmin flies an X-wing for the first time during the final major battle of the Galactic Civil War.

~34 ABY - Founding pilot of the Resistance

Known now as "Snap," he becomes one of the Resistance's founding pilots, serving in Black Squadron under Poe Dameron and marrying fellow pilot Karé Kun.

34 ABY - Assault on Starkiller Base

Snap flies with the Resistance in the attack that destroys the First Order's Starkiller Base.

35 ABY - Death at the Battle of Exegol

Fighting in his X-wing against the Sith Eternal fleet, Snap Wexley is shot down and killed during the Resistance's victory at Exegol.

deathEvent.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

title: Jacen Syndulla
description: Jacen Syndulla is a Force-sensitive human/Twi'lek hybrid, the son of the rebel General Hera Syndulla and the late Jedi Knight Kanan Jarrus, and the grandson of the Twi'lek resistance leader Cham Syndulla. Born during the Galactic Civil War shortly after his father's sacrifice, he first appeared as an infant and toddler in the epilogue of Star Wars Rebels, glimpsed alongside his mother in the years following the Battle of Endor. He made his live-action debut as a boy in the Disney+ series Ahsoka, where he expressed a wish to become a Jedi like his father, hinting at inherited Force potential. Named by creator Dave Filoni as a nod to Jacen Solo of the old Expanded Universe, he represents the next generation of the Syndulla and Jarrus legacy.
timeline:
1 BBY - Birth

Jacen Syndulla is born to Hera Syndulla and Kanan Jarrus during the Galactic Civil War, shortly after Kanan's death; he never knew his father, who sacrificed himself to save the Spectres.

~5 ABY - Seen in the Rebels epilogue

Following the Battle of Endor and the liberation of Lothal, a young Jacen is shown with his mother Hera as Sabine Wren departs to help find Ezra Bridger, marking his first appearance.

~9 ABY - Live-action debut in Ahsoka

Now a boy living with his mother, Jacen appears in the series Ahsoka, showing curiosity about the Jedi and Ezra Bridger and telling Hera he wants to become a Jedi—an early hint of his Force sensitivity.

endYearEvent: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

events.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

title: Armitage Hux
description: Armitage Hux was a human male general in the military forces of the First Order during the New Republic Era. The illegitimate son of Imperial Commander Brendol Hux and a kitchen worker on Arkanis, he was raised among the Imperial remnants that fled to the Unknown Regions and grew into a fanatical, ambitious officer. With the backing of Supreme Leader Snoke, Hux took command of the planet-killing Starkiller Base and championed technology that allowed the First Order to track ships through hyperspace. He is best known for his zealous oratory, his bitter power struggle with Kylo Ren, and his ultimate downfall: after Snoke's death and Ren's rise to Supreme Leader, a resentful Hux secretly leaked intelligence to the Resistance as their mole before being exposed and executed by Allegiant General Pryde during the First Order's final campaign.
timeline:
~1 BBY - Birth on Arkanis

Armitage Hux is born on Arkanis, the illegitimate son of Imperial Commander Brendol Hux and a kitchen worker, around the time of the Battle of Yavin.

~5 ABY - Flight to the Unknown Regions

As the Galactic Empire collapses, the young Hux is taken by his father and other Imperial loyalists into the Unknown Regions, where the survivors lay the foundations of the First Order.

~34 ABY - General of the First Order

Hux rises to the rank of general, commanding Starkiller Base and promoting the hyperspace-tracking technology intended to hunt down the Resistance.

34 ABY - Destruction of the Hosnian system

Hux delivers a fiery address and orders Starkiller Base to fire on the New Republic capital in the Hosnian system, before the Resistance destroys the superweapon during their assault.

34 ABY - Snoke's death and Ren's ascension

Aboard the Supremacy, Snoke is killed by Kylo Ren, who seizes the title of Supreme Leader, derailing Hux's ambitions and deepening his resentment toward his rival.

35 ABY - Resistance mole

Sidelined under Ren's rule, Hux secretly begins feeding intelligence to the Resistance, aiding the escape of captured heroes to spite the new Supreme Leader.

35 ABY - Death by execution

Exposed as the First Order's mole, Hux is shot and killed by Allegiant General Pryde aboard a Star Destroyer during the events of the Battle of Exegol campaign.

deathEvent.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

events.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

title: Poe Dameron
description: Poe Dameron is a human pilot and military leader, widely regarded as the best starfighter pilot in the Resistance. Born on Yavin 4 to Rebel Alliance veterans Kes Dameron and Shara Bey, he grew up idolizing his mother's flying and, as a rebellious teenager, briefly ran with the Spice Runners of Kijimi alongside Zorii Bliss before turning his life around. He joined the New Republic Defense Fleet, commanding Rapier Squadron, but grew frustrated with the Republic's inaction against the First Order and defected to General Leia Organa's Resistance. Flying his customized T-70 X-wing "Black One" under the callsign Black Leader, he became one of Leia's most trusted operatives, leading the assault that destroyed Starkiller Base. After a hard lesson in the cost of reckless heroism during the evacuation of D'Qar and the Battle of Crait, he matured into a leader, was named acting General, and ultimately co-commanded the Resistance fleet with Finn at the Battle of Exegol, helping bring down the resurrected Emperor Palpatine.
timeline:
2 ABY - Birth on Yavin 4

Poe Dameron is born on the jungle moon of Yavin 4 to Rebel Alliance pilot Shara Bey and Pathfinder sergeant Kes Dameron, growing up on their homestead near a Force-sensitive tree gifted by Luke Skywalker.

~18 ABY - Running with the Spice Runners of Kijimi

As a rebellious teenager, Poe leaves home and falls in with the Spice Runners of Kijimi, forming a bond with Zorii Bliss before abandoning the criminal life to pursue a more honorable path.

~28 ABY - Service in the New Republic

Poe follows in his late mother's footsteps and joins the New Republic Defense Fleet, eventually rising to command Rapier Squadron.

~32 ABY - Defection to the Resistance

Disillusioned by the New Republic's refusal to confront the First Order's violations of the Galactic Concordance, Poe leaves the Defense Fleet and joins Leia Organa's Resistance, becoming one of her most trusted operatives.

~34 ABY - Missions with the Resistance

Flying under the callsign Black Leader in his T-70 X-wing Black One, Poe undertakes covert operations for the Resistance, crossing paths with figures tied to the events depicted in Star Wars Resistance.

34 ABY - The Force Awakens

Captured by the First Order while retrieving a map to Luke Skywalker, Poe escapes with the stormtrooper he names Finn, is reunited with his droid BB-8, and leads the successful starfighter assault that destroys Starkiller Base.

34 ABY - The Last Jedi

Poe's reckless heroism during the evacuation of D'Qar costs lives and gets him demoted by Leia and Vice Admiral Holdo; he learns the burden of leadership and helps the survivors escape the Battle of Crait.

35 ABY - The Rise of Skywalker

Named acting General by Leia, Poe leads the Resistance in confronting the resurrected Emperor Palpatine, revealing his spice-running past, and co-commands the fleet with Finn at the Battle of Exegol, ending the threat of the Final Order.

endYearEvent: Episode VIII: The Last Jedi
title: Snoke
description: Snoke was a Force-sensitive humanoid male who reigned as the first Supreme Leader of the First Order during the New Republic era, commanding its military conquest of the galaxy and personally seducing Ben Solo to the dark side as his apprentice Kylo Ren. In reality Snoke was not a natural being but an artificial genetic construct — a "strandcast" — created on the hidden Sith world of Exegol by the resurrected Darth Sidious and his Sith Eternal cultists to serve as a manipulable proxy through which the Emperor could rule the First Order from the shadows. Though engineered as a puppet, Snoke possessed an independent mind and free will, and he is best known for training Kylo Ren, orchestrating the destruction of the New Republic's Hosnian system, and taunting Rey aboard the flagship Supremacy. His reign ended when Kylo Ren, feigning loyalty, cut him in half with Rey's lightsaber — a death that ultimately served Sidious's larger design, as additional Snoke clones remained in stasis on Exegol.
timeline:
~4 ABY - Creation on Exegol

In the aftermath of Emperor Palpatine's death at the Battle of Endor, the Sith Eternal on Exegol used cloning and genetic sciences to engineer Snoke as a strandcast — an artificial being designed to act as the resurrected Sidious's proxy in the wider galaxy.

~28-34 ABY - Rise of the First Order

Emerging from the Unknown Regions, Snoke came to rule the First Order as its Supreme Leader, rebuilding Imperial-derived military power while, unknown to most, being influenced from afar by Sidious.

~28-34 ABY - Corruption of Ben Solo

Snoke reached out to the Force-sensitive Ben Solo, son of Han Solo and Leia Organa, turning him against his uncle Luke Skywalker and molding him into the dark-side warrior Kylo Ren.

34 ABY - The Hunt for Luke Skywalker

Appearing as a towering hologram, Snoke directed General Hux and Kylo Ren in the search for the missing Luke Skywalker and oversaw the First Order's assault that destroyed the Hosnian system and the New Republic capital.

34 ABY - Death aboard the Supremacy

Aboard his flagship Supremacy, Snoke tortured the captured Rey and ordered Kylo Ren to kill her. Believing his apprentice fully turned, Snoke was instead betrayed and bisected by Ren, who ignited Rey's lightsaber at his side and seized the mantle of Supreme Leader.

35 ABY - Snoke revealed as a puppet

On Exegol, the surviving Sidious revealed dormant backup Snoke bodies suspended in tanks, confirming that Snoke had always been a manufactured vessel and vessel-line for the Sith Lord's will.

events.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

title: Ben Solo
description: Ben Solo, better known as the dark warrior Kylo Ren, was a human male Force-sensitive who served as master of the Knights of Ren and, ultimately, Supreme Leader of the First Order before returning to the light shortly before his death. Born in 5 ABY on the Core World of Chandrila to General Han Solo and Princess Leia Organa, he was the heir to the Skywalker bloodline and possessed immense raw power in the Force. Trained as a Jedi apprentice by his uncle, Luke Skywalker, during the New Republic Era, he was manipulated across his youth by the resurrected Darth Sidious and Sidious's creation, Supreme Leader Snoke, who lured him to the dark side and drove him to destroy Luke's fledgling temple. As Kylo Ren he hunted the last Jedi, killed his own father Han Solo, slew Snoke to seize command of the First Order, and clashed repeatedly with the scavenger Rey — with whom, unknown to either, he shared a rare dyad in the Force. Confronted by the memory of his father and Rey's compassion, Ben Solo rejected the dark side at the Battle of Exegol in 35 ABY, helped defeat the reborn Emperor, and gave his own life to revive Rey, ending the Skywalker line.
timeline:
5 ABY - Birth on Chandrila

Ben Solo is born on the Core World of Chandrila to Han Solo and Leia Organa on the very day the Galactic Empire surrendered to the New Republic, ending the Galactic Civil War.

~28 ABY - Fall of Luke's Temple

As a young apprentice at Luke Skywalker's Jedi temple, Ben is preyed upon by Snoke and Sidious. A moment of fear from Luke drives him fully to the dark side; he destroys the temple, takes fellow students as the Knights of Ren, and becomes Kylo Ren.

34 ABY - Hunt for the Map to Skywalker

Now a commander of the First Order, Kylo Ren captures the pilot Poe Dameron and the droid BB-8 on Jakku while searching for a map to the exiled Luke Skywalker.

34 ABY - Death of Han Solo

Aboard Starkiller Base, Kylo Ren confronts his father Han Solo on a bridge and kills him in an attempt to cast off the light. He is then wounded in a duel with Rey in the surrounding forest.

34 ABY - The First Battle with Rey

Overpowered by the untrained scavenger Rey, Kylo Ren is scarred and forced to flee as Starkiller Base collapses, marking the beginning of his obsession with her.

34 ABY - Supreme Leader of the First Order

Drawn into a Force-bond with Rey, Kylo Ren brings her before Snoke, then kills Snoke and battles the Praetorian Guard alongside her. He proclaims himself Supreme Leader of the First Order but fails to turn Rey to his side.

35 ABY - Alliance with the Resurrected Sidious

Kylo Ren travels to Exegol and pledges himself to the returned Darth Sidious, taking command of the Sith Eternal's Final Order fleet while continuing to pursue Rey across the galaxy.

35 ABY - Redemption of Ben Solo

After Rey wounds and then heals him, and confronted by a memory of his father, Kylo Ren casts away his lightsaber and reclaims his identity as Ben Solo, rejecting the dark side for good.

35 ABY - Death at the Battle of Exegol

Ben Solo joins Rey against Sidious on Exegol. After the Emperor's final defeat, he uses the Force to revive a fallen Rey, sacrificing his own life force and passing away as the last of the Skywalker bloodline.

deathEvent.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

endYearEvent: Episode VIII: The Last Jedi
title: Phasma
description: Phasma was a human warrior from the nuclear-ravaged world of Parnassos who rose to become a captain in the First Order and commander of its stormtrooper legions. Born into the Scyre clan, she survived Parnassos's brutal wastelands to become her people's fiercest fighter before the arrival of the crashed First Order officer Brendol Hux gave her a path offworld — a path she seized by betraying her brother Keldo and her own clan. Rising through the First Order in her distinctive chromium armor, she is known for leading the assault on Jakku, for her ruthless efficiency, and for her rivalry with the defector Finn (FN-2187). She stood among the First Order's leadership alongside General Hux and Kylo Ren, and met her end during the Crait campaign aboard the Star Dreadnought Supremacy, falling into flames after a final duel with Finn.
timeline:
6 ABY - Birth on Parnassos

Phasma is born on the harsh, post-apocalyptic world of Parnassos, growing up as a member of the Scyre clan.

~24 ABY - Warrior of the Scyre

Hardened by Parnassos's deadly environment, Phasma becomes the Scyre clan's most formidable fighter and a leader of its warriors, serving alongside her brother Keldo.

~28 ABY - Betrayal and departure from Parnassos

When First Order General Brendol Hux crash-lands on Parnassos, Phasma helps him reach his ship in exchange for passage offworld, betraying her brother Keldo and the remnants of her clan to secure her escape.

~28 ABY - Joining the First Order

Rescued from Parnassos, Phasma abandons her former life, rises through the First Order to become captain of its stormtrooper corps, and adopts her signature chromium armor.

34 ABY - Assault on Jakku and the Force Awakens

Phasma leads the First Order attack on Jakku, later reprimands the wavering trooper FN-2187 (Finn), and is captured aboard Starkiller Base, where she is forced at gunpoint to lower the base's shields before it is destroyed.

34 ABY - Star Wars Resistance

Around the events of the Force Awakens, Phasma appears in operations against the Resistance, reinforcing the First Order's tightening grip on the galaxy.

34 ABY - Death aboard the Supremacy

During the First Order's pursuit of the Resistance, Phasma confronts Finn aboard the Star Dreadnought Supremacy. After a fierce duel the deck gives way beneath her, and she falls into the flames below, ending her life during the events of The Last Jedi.

deathEvent.title: Episode VIII: The Last Jedi

events.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

title: Rose Tico
description: Rose Tico is a human woman born in 11 ABY on the Otomok-system world of Hays Minor, whose people were exploited and brutalized by the First Order — an experience that fueled her lifelong hatred of the regime. She and her older sister Paige both joined the Resistance, where Rose served as a flight engineer and maintenance technician. She is best known for her role during the war against the First Order: after Paige died as a bomber gunner at the Battle of D'Qar, Rose partnered with the former stormtrooper Finn on a covert mission to the casino city of Canto Bight to find a codebreaker who could disable the First Order's hyperspace tracker. Though the plan failed after they were betrayed by the slicer DJ, Rose fought in the Battle of Crait, where she saved Finn's life by stopping him from a suicidal charge. She continued serving the Resistance through the final campaign against the resurgent Sith Eternal, remaining at the base during the Battle of Exegol, and survived the war.
timeline:
11 ABY - Birth on Hays Minor

Rose Tico is born on Hays Minor in the Otomok system, a world stripped and poisoned by the First Order, alongside her older sister Paige.

~34 ABY - Joins the Resistance

Rose and Paige enlist with the Resistance, Rose working as a flight engineer and maintenance technician while Paige serves as a bomber gunner.

34 ABY - Death of Paige at the Battle of D'Qar

During the Resistance evacuation of D'Qar, Paige Tico is killed manning her bomber against the First Order dreadnought, leaving Rose grieving her sister.

34 ABY - Meeting Finn and the Canto Bight mission

Rose stops the former stormtrooper Finn from deserting the fleet, and the two set out for the casino city of Canto Bight to recruit a codebreaker capable of disabling the First Order's hyperspace tracker.

34 ABY - Betrayal and capture

Rose, Finn, and BB-8 instead enlist the slicer DJ, who betrays them to the First Order after their infiltration of the Supremacy fails.

34 ABY - Battle of Crait

In the ground battle on the mineral world of Crait, Rose saves Finn's life by ramming his speeder to stop his suicidal charge, and afterward shares a moment with him before escaping aboard the Millennium Falcon.

35 ABY - The war against the Sith Eternal

Rose continues to serve the Resistance during its final campaign, remaining at the base to help plan the assault on Exegol against the resurgent Emperor Palpatine's fleet.

35 ABY - Victory and survival

Rose survives the Battle of Exegol and the defeat of the First Order and Sith Eternal, celebrating the Resistance's triumph.

events.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

title: Finn
description: Finn, formerly designated FN-2187 ("Eight-Seven"), is a Force-sensitive human male and former First Order stormtrooper who deserted and defected to the Resistance during the First Order–Resistance War. Taken from his family as an infant and raised from birth to be an obedient soldier, he experienced a crisis of conscience during a First Order massacre on Jakku, refusing to fire on civilians and fleeing the regime. Initially reluctant to commit to a cause he feared was doomed, he was drawn into the conflict through his friendships with the pilot Poe Dameron and the scavenger Rey. Over the course of the war he rescued Poe, helped destroy Starkiller Base, survived a lightsaber duel with Kylo Ren, joined the strike team at Canto Bight and the Battle of Crait, and ultimately rose to become a general of the Resistance. During the campaign against the resurrected Emperor Palpatine he came to recognize his own connection to the Force, bonded with fellow deserter Jannah, and helped win the decisive Battle of Exegol.
timeline:
11 ABY - Birth

Finn is born during the New Republic Era. As an infant he is taken from his family by the First Order to be raised as a soldier.

~11 ABY - Conscription as FN-2187

Separated from his parents, the child is stripped of a name, designated FN-2187, and trained from birth in the First Order's stormtrooper program.

34 ABY - Defection at the Battle of Jakku

Deployed on his first combat mission, FN-2187 refuses to fire on the villagers of Tuanul during a massacre. Unwilling to serve, he frees captured Resistance pilot Poe Dameron and helps him escape, and Poe gives him the name "Finn."

34 ABY - Meeting Rey and joining the fight

Shot down over Jakku, Finn meets the scavenger Rey and BB-8. After the First Order attacks Takodana, he reconnects with the Resistance and reluctantly agrees to help.

34 ABY - Assault on Starkiller Base

Posing as an expert to rescue Rey, Finn helps the Resistance strike team infiltrate Starkiller Base. He duels Kylo Ren with Luke Skywalker's lightsaber, is gravely wounded, and falls into a coma.

34 ABY - The mission to Canto Bight

Awakening from his coma aboard the Raddus, Finn partners with mechanic Rose Tico on a covert mission to Canto Bight to disable the First Order fleet's hyperspace tracker.

34 ABY - Battle of Crait

During the last stand on Crait, Finn attempts to sacrifice himself against a First Order siege cannon, but Rose stops him, and he escapes with the surviving Resistance.

35 ABY - General of the Resistance

As the war intensifies against the resurrected Emperor Palpatine, Finn becomes a leader within the Resistance and searches for a Sith wayfinder alongside Rey, Poe, and their allies.

35 ABY - Meeting Jannah and sensing the Force

Finn encounters Jannah, a fellow former stormtrooper who deserted, and on the wreckage of the second Death Star he grows convinced that his instincts are guided by the Force.

35 ABY - Battle of Exegol

Finn leads Resistance ground forces aboard Palpatine's Sith fleet, helping to destroy a Star Destroyer's navigation tower and secure victory at Exegol. He survives the war.

events.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

title: Kaydel Ko Connix
description: Kaydel Ko Connix was a human female who served the Resistance during its war against the First Order. Born on Dulathia around 13 ABY, she joined the Resistance and became an operations controller in Fleet Command, working under General Leia Organa at the base on D'Qar. She coordinated communications during the assault on Starkiller Base, after which she was promoted to lieutenant. During the subsequent Resistance evacuation she helped organize the withdrawal from D'Qar, served aboard the flagship Raddus, and joined Poe Dameron's mutiny against Vice Admiral Amilyn Holdo before surviving the Battle of Crait. By the time of the war's climax she had risen to the rank of commander and took part in the final campaign against the Sith Eternal at the Battle of Exegol, which she survived. She is remembered as one of the dedicated officers of Leia Organa's command staff who helped keep the Resistance alive through its darkest years.
timeline:
~13 ABY - Birth on Dulathia

Kaydel Ko Connix is born on the planet Dulathia. Her birth year is derived from her stated age of twenty-two during 35 ABY, not from a directly recorded date.

~34 ABY - Serving in Fleet Command on D'Qar

Connix serves as an operations controller in Fleet Command at the Resistance's D'Qar headquarters, working under General Leia Organa.

34 ABY - Coordinating the Starkiller Base raid

During the war with the First Order, Connix coordinates communications for the Resistance assault on Starkiller Base. Her service leads to a promotion to lieutenant.

34 ABY - Evacuation of D'Qar and the Holdo mutiny

Connix helps organize the evacuation of D'Qar ahead of the First Order and serves aboard the flagship Raddus. During the pursuit she joins Poe Dameron's mutiny against Vice Admiral Amilyn Holdo, and afterward survives the Battle of Crait.

35 ABY - Battle of Exegol

Now holding the rank of commander, Connix takes part in the Resistance's final campaign against the First Order and the Sith Eternal at the Battle of Exegol, which the Resistance wins. She survives the battle.

events.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

title: Rey Skywalker
description: Rey Skywalker was a human female scavenger from the desert world of Jakku who rose to become one of the last and most powerful Jedi of her era, fighting for the Resistance against the First Order during the final days of the New Republic Era. Abandoned on Jakku as a child by parents desperate to hide her from her grandfather, the resurrected Emperor Sheev Palpatine, she survived for years salvaging wreckage before her encounter with the droid BB-8 drew her into galaxy-spanning events. Strong in the Force, she sought out the exiled Luke Skywalker for training and formed a rare bond known as a dyad in the Force with Ben Solo, the fallen Jedi known as Kylo Ren. Tempted by the dark side yet ultimately choosing the light, Rey confronted and destroyed Palpatine on Exegol, ending the Sith once and for all with Ben's sacrifice. Rejecting the Palpatine name, she took the surname Skywalker in honor of her masters Luke and Leia, becoming heir to their legacy and pledging to rebuild the Jedi Order.
timeline:
15 ABY - Birth

Rey is born to Dathan — a genetic "son" and failed clone of Emperor Palpatine — and his wife Miramir, making her the granddaughter of Sheev Palpatine.

~19 ABY - Abandoned on Jakku

To keep her hidden from Palpatine and his Sith Eternal cultists, Rey's parents leave her in the care of the junk boss Unkar Plutt on Jakku and flee, later being killed. Rey grows up alone as a scavenger, awaiting a return that never comes.

34 ABY - The Force Awakens

Rey takes in the droid BB-8, flees Jakku aboard the Millennium Falcon with the former stormtrooper Finn, and experiences a Force awakening. After Han Solo's death and a duel with Kylo Ren on Starkiller Base, she travels to Ahch-To to find Luke Skywalker.

34 ABY - The Last Jedi

Rey trains briefly under a reluctant Luke Skywalker and develops a mysterious Force-bond with Kylo Ren. She fails to turn Ben Solo back to the light and rejoins the battered Resistance, helping them escape Crait.

35 ABY - The Rise of Skywalker

Rey learns she is Palpatine's granddaughter and confronts the resurrected Emperor on Exegol. Drawing on the strength of past Jedi, she destroys Palpatine, dying briefly before Ben Solo revives her through their dyad bond at the cost of his own life.

35 ABY - Taking the Skywalker Name

Returning to the abandoned Lars homestead on Tatooine, Rey buries the Skywalker lightsabers and, when asked her name, declares herself Rey Skywalker — embracing the legacy of her masters over her Palpatine bloodline.

events.title: Episode IX: The Rise of Skywalker

title: BB-8
description: BB-8, sometimes spelled Beebee-Ate and nicknamed BB, was a spherical BB-series astromech droid built in 29 ABY on Hosnian Prime. Distinguished by a free-rolling ball body topped with a domed head reminiscent of an R2-series unit, BB-8 was primarily white with orange and silver accents and a single black photoreceptor. He became the loyal companion of Resistance pilot Poe Dameron, accompanying him aboard his T-70 X-wing starfighter. BB-8 is best known for safeguarding a fragment of the map leading to the exiled Luke Skywalker after Poe entrusted it to him on Jakku, setting in motion the events of the war between the Resistance and the First Order. Plucky, resourceful, and fiercely loyal, he aided Rey, Finn, and the Resistance across the campaigns against the First Order and the resurrected Emperor Palpatine, surviving the conflict as one of the era's most beloved droids.
timeline:
29 ABY - Construction on Hosnian Prime

BB-8 is built as a BB-series astromech droid on Hosnian Prime and eventually enters the service of the Resistance.

~30s ABY - Companion to Poe Dameron

BB-8 becomes the astromech partner of Resistance pilot Poe Dameron, riding aboard his T-70 X-wing and serving alongside him during missions depicted in Star Wars Resistance.

34 ABY - The Search for Luke Skywalker

On Jakku, Poe entrusts BB-8 with a fragment of the map to the exiled Luke Skywalker. BB-8 escapes the First Order, encounters the scavenger Rey and the defector Finn, and helps deliver the map to the Resistance, playing a key role in the events of The Force Awakens.

34 ABY - The War Against the First Order

BB-8 accompanies the Resistance through its desperate flight from the First Order fleet and the stand on Crait, assisting Poe, Rey, and Finn during the events of The Last Jedi.

35 ABY - The Fall of the Final Order

BB-8 joins the final campaign against the resurrected Emperor Palpatine and the Sith Eternal fleet at Exegol, surviving the Resistance's victory during the events of The Rise of Skywalker.
